en.editorNote: 'Clanker' is a Star Wars-era robot slur that went viral on TikTok in 2025 thanks to widespread AI fatigue. By 2026 it's the default way tech workers vent about chatbots, agents, and any LLM standing between them and a real human. Pure exhaustion energy
variations: Work version: 'Thirty-minute meeting, all clankers summarizing each other, no humans spoke once.' Perfect for the AI-mediated meeting that didn't need to happen.

items.sourceNote: 「shrekking」由 TikToker @jaxitodwyer 2025-08 提出，描述故意降標準交往以求被珍惜；2026 已成主流約會詞

zh-tw.content:
我以為自己在 shrekking，
以為他會因為我願意降標準而拼命對我好。

結果他理所當然，
而我變成那個更卑微的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「shrekking」源自 TikToker @jaxitodwyer 2025 年八月的影片，意思是故意跟你覺得「條件比你差」的人交往，期待對方因此死心塌地。靈感來自費歐娜公主和史瑞克的童話結局，但現實版多半翻車。2026 已經是 Threads 感情貼文常出現的反省關鍵字
variations: 事後版：「我不是在 shrekking，我只是 shrekked——被自以為善良的選擇反咬一口。」適合用在感情復盤的長文裡。

en.content:
I thought I was shrekking —
thought he'd worship me for lowering the bar.

Turns out he just took it for granted,
and I became the smaller person.
en.editorNote: 'Shrekking' was coined by TikToker @jaxitodwyer in August 2025 — deliberately dating someone you find 'below' your standards on the assumption they'll treat you like royalty out of gratitude. The Fiona-and-Shrek logic. By 2026 it's the go-to confessional keyword for dating-app post-mortems
variations: Post-mortem version: 'I wasn't shrekking, I got shrekked — punished for an act of supposed kindness.' Use it in the long Threads caption after the breakup.

items.sourceNote: 「side quest」源自 RPG 用語，2022 起在 TikTok 蔓延，2026 成為 Gen Z 描述生活脫線時刻的標配詞

zh-tw.content:
出門買牙膏，
結果現在在朋友家吃滷肉飯，
手裡多一隻流浪貓。

side quest。
我的主線今天可以休息了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「side quest」原本是 RPG 遊戲的支線任務，2022 開始在 TikTok 被拿來形容「本來不該發生但發生了」的生活岔路。2026 在台灣 Threads 直接寫英文也通，重點是承認自己今天沒在過主線。用法越荒謬越精準
variations: 厭世版：「我的人生整個都是 side quest，主線 NPC 還沒給我。」用來自嘲沒有方向感的時期。

en.content:
Went out for toothpaste.
Now I'm at a friend's place eating leftovers
with a stray cat in my lap.

Side quest.
Main story takes the day off.
en.editorNote: 'Side quest' jumped from RPG menus to TikTok around 2022 and by 2026 it's the universal Gen Z phrase for the chaotic detour you didn't plan. The more absurd the detour, the better the post. Confessing your main story is paused is the whole vibe
variations: Existential version: 'My entire life is side quest content, the main story NPC hasn't loaded in yet.' Self-deprecating direction-less-era energy.

items.sourceNote: 「我給ㄅ級分」源自 YouTuber 金童「ㄅㄆㄇ評分系統」，2026 經網紅李怡蓁帶紅，Threads 上常見的中性偏委婉評價

zh-tw.content:
新開的拉麵店，
湯頭普普、麵條軟、配料誠意夠。

我給ㄅ級分。
不會再去，但不討厭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「我給ㄅ級分」是 2026 Threads 的隱形流行用法，源自 YouTuber 金童發明的「ㄅㄆㄇ評分系統」（ㄅ＞ㄆ＞ㄇ），ㄅ就是「還可以但沒驚喜」。比說「普通」更有梗、比說「難吃」更有禮貌，台灣人專屬的委婉評價
variations: 升級版：「他人很好但我給ㄅ級分。」適用於相親、聯誼後跟朋友報告的場景，意思就是「不到 NG，但也沒火花」。

en.content:
New ramen spot in town.
Broth fine, noodles soft, toppings generous.

I'd give it a 'B-rank'.
Not going back, but not mad about it.
en.editorNote: 'I give it B-rank' (我給ㄅ級分) comes from Taiwanese YouTuber Jin Tong's bopomofo grading system — using the first three letters of the Mandarin phonetic alphabet (ㄅ > ㄆ > ㄇ) as ranks. B means 'fine but unremarkable'. It went mainstream on Threads in 2026 as the polite Taiwanese way to say 'mid' without sounding harsh
variations: Dating version: 'He was nice, but I'd give it a B-rank.' Use after a setup or blind date — translates to 'not a red flag, just no spark.'

items.sourceNote: 「炎上」源自日文，2026 在台灣 Threads 被廣泛用來描述被網友圍剿、罵爆的狀態

zh-tw.content:
我只是發了一句
「珍奶不用半糖會更好喝」，

結果留言區炎上。

我以為我在分享，
他們以為我在宣戰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「炎上」原本是日文，形容名人發言惹議被網友放火燒爛。2026 在台灣 Threads 完全本土化，從藝人公關危機到自己的貼文翻車都能用。比說「被罵爆」多一層戲劇性，自嘲時特別好笑
variations: 預警版：「這篇我預感會炎上，但我還是要發。」用來自我搞笑兼免責聲明。

en.content:
All I posted was
'boba is better without the half-sugar option,'

and my comments turned into a bonfire.

I thought I was sharing.
They thought I was declaring war.
en.editorNote: 'Enjou' (炎上, literally 'going up in flames') is a Japanese loanword that fully naturalized in Taiwan's Threads scene by 2026. It describes the moment a post gets dogpiled into oblivion. Way more cinematic than 'got dragged' — works for both celebrity scandals and your own innocent takes that went sideways
variations: Preemptive version: 'I have a feeling this post is gonna get torched, but I'm posting it anyway.' Self-roast plus disclaimer in one move.

items.sourceNote: 「Fanum tax」源自 Twitch 實況主 Fanum 偷吃朋友食物的梗，2024 爆紅後 2026 仍是 Gen Z 飯局常用詞

zh-tw.content:
你薯條剩兩根？
那兩根是我的。

Fanum tax。

好朋友之間不需要解釋，
只需要繳稅。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「Fanum tax」源自美國 Twitch 實況主 Fanum，他直播時會偷吃朋友的食物，後來變成 Gen Z 朋友圈內的「強制分食稅」。2026 在台灣聚餐、宿舍生活也很實用，伸手拿走朋友最後一塊雞排前先喊一聲就行了
variations: 反抗版：「我拒繳 Fanum tax，這碗鹹酥雞是我的退休金。」用來保護自己的食物。

en.content:
You've got two fries left?
Those two fries are mine.

Fanum tax.

Real friends don't need explanations.
They just pay up.
en.editorNote: 'Fanum tax' comes from Twitch streamer Fanum, who built a bit out of stealing his friends' food on stream. It evolved into Gen Z shorthand for the mandatory 'best friend food tithe' and was still everywhere in 2026. Yell it before snatching the last nugget — it's basically a permission slip
variations: Resistance version: 'I refuse to pay the Fanum tax — this popcorn chicken is my retirement fund.' Use to defend your snacks from grabby friends.

items.sourceNote: 「Brain AFK」是 2026 Slangwise 觀察榜的新興詞，形容大腦離線但身體還在運作的狀態

zh-tw.content:
會議開到第三小時，
我點頭、我微笑、我打了一句「了解」。

但我 brain AFK。

身體還在線，
靈魂去買便當了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「Brain AFK」是 2026 新興 Gen Z 用語，AFK 是遊戲術語「Away From Keyboard」，腦袋 AFK 意思就是「人在心不在」。比說「放空」精準，因為強調「身體還在演」這個荒謬感。打工人最愛
variations: 週一版：「我整個禮拜一都 brain AFK，請等到禮拜二再找我談重要的事。」適合公告自己當前狀態。

en.content:
Hour three of the meeting.
I'm nodding, I'm smiling, I just typed 'got it.'

But my brain is AFK.

The body's still online.
The soul stepped out to grab lunch.
en.editorNote: 'Brain AFK' (Away From Keyboard) is a 2026 watchlist entry — it captures the exact feeling of being physically present but mentally elsewhere. More precise than 'zoning out' because it leans into the comedy of your body still going through the motions. Office-worker gold
variations: Monday version: 'I'll be brain AFK all Monday, please defer all important conversations to Tuesday.' Pre-emptive status update.

items.sourceNote: 「ate (and left no crumbs)」2024 起在 TikTok 爆紅，2026 仍是稱讚某人表現完美的招牌句

zh-tw.content:
她上台 30 秒，
台詞穩、走位準、最後還回眸笑了一下。

She ate. 
And left no crumbs.

意思是：她吃乾抹淨，
沒留一點失誤給觀眾撿。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「ate (and left no crumbs)」是 TikTok 招牌稱讚句，「ate」是「表現超強」，「left no crumbs」是「連屑都沒剩」，連起來就是「完美到無懈可擊」。2026 在台灣 IG 留言區直接寫英文也通，比說「太神了」多一層粉絲狂熱感
variations: 簡短版：「She ate.」三個字就能誇人，配在朋友的限動底下最有禮貌的炸裂。

en.content:
She walked on stage for 30 seconds.
Lines locked, marks hit, threw in a glance back at the end.

She ate.
And left no crumbs.

Translation: she devoured the whole performance.
Not a single misstep left on the plate.
en.editorNote: 'Ate (and left no crumbs)' has been a TikTok mainstay since 2024 and was still going strong in 2026 — 'ate' means crushed it, 'no crumbs' means flawlessly. Drop it in the comments under a friend's outfit pic or a stage performance. More fan-energy than 'killed it' and somehow more affectionate
variations: Short version: 'She ate.' Two words is all you need under a friend's story. Maximum hype, minimum effort.

items.sourceNote: 「NPC behavior」源自電玩中無腦走位的非玩家角色，2026 已成 Gen Z 形容人僵硬、機械化的萬用詞

zh-tw.content:
他每天九點打卡，
午餐永遠是同一家便當，
下班走同一條路回家。

NPC behavior。

他活得太穩，
穩到我懷疑遊戲還沒讀取到他的劇情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「NPC behavior」（非玩家角色行為）形容人活得像電玩裡的背景角色——重複動作、固定路線、零選擇分支。2026 Gen Z 用來吐槽身邊太規律的朋友，或自嘲自己人生卡關沒在跑主線。沒有惡意，就是好笑
variations: 自嘲版：「我這禮拜整個 NPC behavior，連午餐都選同一個 set meal A。」承認自己人生暫停讀取中。

en.content:
Clocks in at 9 sharp every day.
Lunch is the same bento from the same shop.
Walks the same route home.

NPC behavior.

He's living too consistently.
Makes me wonder if the game hasn't loaded his quest line yet.
en.editorNote: 'NPC behavior' compares someone to a non-playable video-game character — repetitive movements, fixed routines, no branching dialogue. In 2026 Gen Z uses it to gently roast friends with rigid routines, or to self-deprecate when their own life feels stuck on autopilot. No real malice, just observational comedy
variations: Self-roast version: 'Full NPC behavior this week — even ordered set meal A for lunch five days straight.' Admitting your main quest is buffering.

items.sourceNote: 「pop off」2026 teen slang glossary 收錄；意指表現驚艷、做得超好

zh-tw.content:
她開口第一句，
整個會議室突然安靜下來。

Go on, pop off, queen.

意思是：
你儘管炸，我們負責聽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「pop off」在 2026 是萬用稱讚動詞，意思是「儘管展現、儘管發揮」。朋友剛貼一張超殺的自拍、剛丟出一段超酸的金句，留「pop off」三個字就贏。比「太強了」更有臨場感
variations: 鼓勵版：「Pop off, bestie.」朋友要上台簡報前丟這句，比加油更熱血。

en.content:
She opened with one line,
and the whole conference room went silent.

Go on, pop off, queen.

Meaning:
you cook, we're listening.
en.editorNote: 'Pop off' is the catch-all 2026 hype verb — 'go ahead, show out, we're watching.' Drop it under a friend's killer selfie or a brutal takedown post. More immediate than 'you killed it' because it's in real time, cheering them on mid-flex
variations: Cheerleader version: 'Pop off, bestie.' Send right before a friend's presentation — more electric than 'good luck.'

items.sourceNote: 「based」2026 持續流行；形容某人真誠、不在乎別人眼光、活得有種

zh-tw.content:
他在會議上直接說：
「這個 KPI 不合理，我不做。」

全場沈默三秒。

That's based.

意思是：
他活得比我有種多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「based」是 2026 形容「活得有種、不演、不在乎別人怎麼看」的最高讚美。不是「自私」，而是「我清楚我是誰，我不解釋」。在台灣 IG 留言區直接打英文也通，比說「他真酷」多一層精神認同
variations: 酸版：「Wow, so based of you.」朋友做了一件超中二的事，用反諷語氣回。

en.content:
He said it straight in the meeting:
'This KPI doesn't make sense. I'm not doing it.'

Three seconds of silence.

That's based.

Meaning:
he's living with more spine than I am.
en.editorNote: 'Based' in 2026 is the highest compliment for living authentically — confident, unfiltered, doesn't care who's watching. Not selfish, just self-defined. Use it when someone holds a controversial-but-honest stance, or for anyone unapologetically themselves
variations: Sarcastic version: 'Wow, so based of you.' When a friend does something extremely cringe, deliver in deadpan.

items.sourceNote: 「PMO」（pissing me off）2026 algospeak 縮寫；避開髒話過濾器但傳達怒氣

zh-tw.content:
捷運站員廣播：
「列車誤點 15 分鐘。」

旁邊那個人放音樂沒戴耳機。

空調又壞了。

PMO so bad rn.

我快爆炸但我不能爆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「PMO」是 pissing me off 的縮寫，2026 algospeak（演算法用語）代表作。直接打髒話會被平台過濾掉，所以年輕人改用縮寫。發文吐槽日常但又想避開過審，就用這三個字母
variations: 升級版：「PMO fr fr.」（real fr）加在後面強調是真的被氣到，不是開玩笑。

en.content:
Station announcer:
'Train delayed 15 minutes.'

Guy next to me playing music without headphones.

AC is broken again.

PMO so bad rn.

I'm about to lose it but I can't lose it.
en.editorNote: 'PMO' (pissing me off) is the flagship 2026 algospeak abbreviation — platforms filter the full phrase, so Gen Z shortened it. Use when venting on TikTok or X about daily annoyances without tripping the profanity flag. Pair with 'fr' for double emphasis
variations: Maxed version: 'PMO fr fr.' (for real, for real) — stacks the abbreviations to signal you're genuinely done, not joking.

items.sourceNote: 「6-7」2026 Gen Alpha 萬用迷因詞；沒有固定意義，純粹氛圍與內部暗號

zh-tw.content:
「等等你說什麼？」
「6-7。」
「什麼意思？」
「6-7。」
「可以解釋嗎？」
「6-7。」

我放棄了。
他贏了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「6-7」是 2026 Gen Alpha（α 世代，2010 後）的萬用迷因詞，沒有任何固定意義——它就是一個氛圍、一個暗號、一個讓大人聽不懂的圈內梗。問他什麼意思他也答不出來，因為「沒意義」就是意義本身
variations: 對話終結版：吵架吵到一半，對方丟一句「6-7」，你就知道戰局結束了，因為已經沒有共同語言。

en.content:
'Wait, what did you say?'
'6-7.'
'What does that mean?'
'6-7.'
'Can you explain?'
'6-7.'

I gave up.
He won.
en.editorNote: '6-7' is the 2026 Gen Alpha meme-phrase with zero fixed meaning — it's a vibe, a coded handshake, an inside joke designed specifically so adults can't decode it. Ask what it means and the kid can't tell you either, because the meaninglessness IS the point
variations: Conversation-ender version: Mid-argument, the other person drops a flat '6-7' — that's the signal the linguistic common ground has collapsed.

items.sourceNote: 「高麗菜煮蛋那桌」源自插畫家 Kurt Wu 的火鍋撇步；2026 Threads 用來形容「生活智慧頂規」的最高讚美

zh-tw.content:
她教我用吸管
把草莓蒂頭推掉。

這位可以坐
高麗菜煮蛋那桌。

智商和手感兼具，
是這年頭最稀有的組合。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「高麗菜煮蛋那桌」是 2026 Threads 最高級的稱讚，源自網友用一片高麗菜當天然湯勺托住生蛋的火鍋撇步。誰分享的生活小智慧夠神，就會被網友推進這張桌。比說「太聰明了」多了 Threads 限定的圈內感
variations: 工具版：「教我這招冰箱收納的網友，請坐高麗菜煮蛋那桌，主位。」直接幫對方安排位置。

en.content:
She taught me to pop strawberry stems out
with a drinking straw.

This one belongs at
the cabbage-poaches-egg table.

Brains and hands working together —
the rarest combo of this decade.
en.editorNote: 'The cabbage-poaches-egg table' is the 2026 highest Taiwan Threads compliment — born from a viral life hack where a cabbage leaf acts as a natural ladle to keep an egg intact in hot pot. Anyone whose tip is genius enough gets a seat at that imaginary table. More inside-joke energy than 'so smart'
variations: Practical version: 'Whoever taught me this fridge-organizing trick, please take the head seat at the cabbage-poaches-egg table.' Seating chart, assigned.

items.sourceNote: 「本斥但大」源自日漫《刃牙道》；2026 Threads 形容「本來想罵，但對方優點太強就算了」

zh-tw.content:
他遲到 40 分鐘。
本來想開罵。

結果他手上拎著
我最愛的那家麻辣鴨血。

本斥但大。

我的原則
在熱湯前面排隊投降。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「本斥但大」是 2026 Threads 高頻反差梗，意思是「本來要斥責，但對方優勢太大所以算了」。用在自嘲被收買、形容戀愛濾鏡或被禮物哄回來的時候最對味，比直接說「被收買」多一層精準
variations: 感情版：「他每次都忘記紀念日，但記得我生理期，本斥但大，繼續愛。」

en.content:
He showed up 40 minutes late.
I was about to go off.

Then I saw him holding
my favorite duck-blood hot pot to-go.

Originally-mad-but-perks-too-big.

My principles
lined up to surrender to the soup.
en.editorNote: 'Originally-mad-but-perks-too-big' (本斥但大) is a 2026 Taiwan Threads contrast meme — you were ready to scold, then the other party flexed an advantage so big you let it slide. Pinpoint vocabulary for being lovingly bribed, blinded by a crush, or won over by a well-timed snack
variations: Dating version: 'He forgets every anniversary, but remembers my period. Originally-mad-but-perks-too-big. Continuing the relationship.'

items.sourceNote: 「crash out」2026 Gen Z 高頻情緒詞；形容在公開場合情緒失控大爆發

zh-tw.content:
他傳：「我們需要談談。」

我已讀。
手機放下。
咖啡灑了。
貓也嚇跑了。

I'm about to crash out.

我不是脆弱，
是即將進入大爆發模式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「crash out」是 2026 Gen Z 形容「即將公開崩潰、情緒大爆發」的招牌動詞。比 melt down 更戲劇化，因為強調「眾目睽睽下完全失控」的場面感。職場群組、約會分手、追星塌房都能用
variations: 預警版：「Don't talk to me, I'm about to crash out.」這句一出，朋友會自動退三步。

en.content:
He texted: 'we need to talk.'

I read it.
Put the phone down.
Spilled the coffee.
Scared the cat off.

I'm about to crash out.

I'm not fragile.
I'm entering full meltdown mode.
en.editorNote: 'Crash out' is the 2026 Gen Z flagship verb for an emotional meltdown that's about to happen in public. More cinematic than 'melt down' because it leans into the spectacle — losing it spectacularly while everyone watches. Workplace, breakup, fandom collapse, all valid
variations: Warning version: 'Don't talk to me, I'm about to crash out.' One sentence, friends auto-back-up three steps.

items.sourceNote: 「clanker」2026 Gen Z 新興用語；嘲諷過度依賴 AI 工具的人或事物

zh-tw.content:
他傳訊息給我：
「親愛的，我注意到你似乎情緒低落。
讓我們一起探討你的感受。」

Clanker。

你是不是
直接複製貼上 ChatGPT？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「clanker」是 2026 Gen Z 用來吐槽「講話像 AI、做事像機器人」的人的新詞，原意是金屬碰撞聲。男友傳出超級制式的關心、同事寫出明顯 ChatGPT 的訊息，丟一句 clanker 比說「你很假」更精準也更壞
variations: 自嘲版：「我今天寫的信被同事說很 clanker，原來連我都被 AI 同化了。」承認自己也淪陷。

en.content:
He texted me:
'Darling, I notice you seem to be experiencing low mood.
Let's explore your feelings together.'

Clanker.

Did you just
copy-paste from ChatGPT?
en.editorNote: 'Clanker' is a 2026 Gen Z roast for anyone — or anything — that talks like AI and moves like a robot. The word literally evokes clanging metal. When your boyfriend texts robotic concern or a coworker emails obvious GPT output, dropping a single 'clanker' lands sharper than 'that's fake'
variations: Self-roast version: 'A coworker said my email sounded clanker today. Even I've been assimilated by the AI.' Admitting you've crossed over.

items.sourceNote: 「delulu」2025 收錄劍橋詞典；2026 Threads 形容戀愛或職場上的不切實際幻想，自嘲意味濃

zh-tw.content:
他已讀我訊息三天，
但我覺得他只是太忙。

我知道，
我 delulu。

但 delulu is the solulu，
幻想是我今天唯一的能量飲料。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「delulu」是 delusional 的縮寫，2026 Threads 戀愛版頂流自嘲詞，意思是「我知道我在幻想，但我選擇繼續」。配上「delulu is the solulu」（妄想就是解方），就成了當代年輕人對抗現實的精神勝利法
variations: 職場版：「我覺得這次升遷有機會，雖然主管沒暗示，雖然我才進公司半年。Delulu，但 solulu。」

en.content:
He's left me on read for three days,
but I'm telling myself he's just busy.

I know.
I'm delulu.

But delulu is the solulu —
delusion is the only energy drink I have today.
en.editorNote: 'Delulu' (short for delusional) is 2026's signature self-aware crush vocabulary — admitting you're imagining things but choosing to ride the high anyway. Paired with the mantra 'delulu is the solulu' (delusion is the solution), it's Gen Z's preferred form of magical thinking
variations: Career version: 'I feel like this promotion is coming, even though my boss hasn't hinted at it, even though I've been here six months. Delulu, but solulu.'

items.sourceNote: 「brainrot」（腦腐）2025 收錄劍橋詞典；2026 形容短影音看到腦袋退化的狀態

zh-tw.content:
我今天滑了四小時 Reels，
講話開始夾雜「sigma」「rizz」「6-7」。

這就是 brainrot。

我的大腦皺褶
正在被演算法熨平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「brainrot」（腦腐）是 2026 形容「短影音看到腦袋退化」的最高自嘲。連續刷三小時 TikTok 之後開始講出沒人聽得懂的迷因詞，就是腦腐本人。傳給也在滑手機的朋友，比說「我廢了」更有畫面
variations: 預警版：「今天進入 brainrot mode，請勿要求我做需要邏輯的事。」幫朋友設好預期值。

en.content:
Scrolled Reels for four hours today,
now I'm dropping 'sigma' 'rizz' '6-7' mid-sentence.

This is brainrot.

My brain folds
are being ironed flat by the algorithm.
en.editorNote: 'Brainrot' is 2026's flagship self-aware admission that short-form video has hollowed out your higher functions. After three hours of TikTok you start speaking in incomprehensible memes — that IS brainrot. Sharper than 'I'm fried' because it names the cause
variations: Heads-up version: 'Entering brainrot mode today, please don't ask me to do anything that requires logic.' Setting expectations early.

items.sourceNote: 「沒事我自己」2026 Threads 高頻自嘲；形容期待落空後乾脆放生自己的情緒

zh-tw.content:
我問他要不要一起吃飯，
他說在忙。

沒事我自己。

我自己吃，
我自己笑，
我自己存活，
反正我自己是最熟的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「沒事我自己」是 2026 Threads 期待落空時的招牌自嘲。比說「算了」更有戲，把「我自己」當成可以拿來說嘴的事，配在被放鳥、被句點、被冷處理的瞬間最共感
variations: 升級版：「沒事我自己，反正我已經把『我自己』練成才藝了。」把孤獨包裝成技能。

en.content:
I asked if he wanted to grab dinner.
He said he was busy.

No worries, I got me.

I eat with me,
I laugh with me,
I survive with me —
turns out I'm my own oldest friend.
en.editorNote: 'No worries, I got me' (沒事我自己) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' signature self-roast for the moment a plan falls through. More performative than 'never mind' — you flex 'myself' like an accomplishment. Perfect after being ghosted, declined, or coldly dismissed
variations: Upgrade: 'No worries, I got me — at this point being my own company is basically a skill on my resume.' Repackaging solitude as a flex.

items.sourceNote: 「lock in」2026 TikTok 高頻動詞；形容下定決心專注做一件事的瞬間

zh-tw.content:
報告剩 12 小時要交，
素材都還沒整理。

是時候 lock in 了。

關 Threads，
關群組，
關掉那個會讓我分心三小時的男的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「lock in」是 2026 TikTok 高頻動詞，意思是「進入專注模式、不再被任何事干擾」。比說「我要認真了」更有切換感，像是手動把自己鎖在任務裡。考試週、deadline 前、健身目標都能用
variations: 鼓勵版：「You got this, lock in.」朋友面前丟下這句，他會自動進入戰鬥模式。

en.content:
Report due in 12 hours,
research still scattered.

Time to lock in.

Close Threads,
mute the group chat,
block that guy who'll distract me for three hours.
en.editorNote: 'Lock in' is the 2026 TikTok flagship verb for switching into total focus mode — no distractions get in. More tactile than 'time to grind' because it sounds like a manual deadbolt clicking shut. Use during finals week, before deadlines, or for any goal that needs blinders
variations: Hype version: 'You got this, lock in.' Drop it on a friend and watch them snap into combat mode.

items.sourceNote: 「腦袋空空」2026 Threads 自我描述用語；形容什麼都不想思考的放空狀態

zh-tw.content:
他問我在想什麼。

我想了五秒，
誠實回他：

腦袋空空。

不是憂鬱，
不是逃避，
是我終於不想替任何事負責了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「腦袋空空」是 2026 Threads 從負面詞變成自我療癒語的代表。以前是被罵，現在是炫耀「我終於可以什麼都不想」。配在週末躺平、放假發呆的限動最有共鳴
variations: 工具版：「今天目標：腦袋空空到下午五點，然後再決定要不要吃晚餐。」把放空寫成行程。

en.content:
He asked what I was thinking about.

I considered it for five seconds,
then answered honestly:

Empty head.

Not depression,
not avoidance —
I just finally stopped wanting to be responsible for anything.
en.editorNote: 'Empty head' (腦袋空空) flipped from insult to flex in 2026 Taiwan Threads — used to be a roast, now it's a brag about reaching pure mental stillness. Pair with a weekend horizontal-living story for instant resonance
variations: Practical version: 'Today's goal: empty head until 5pm, then decide if dinner is happening.' Scheduling the void.

items.sourceNote: 「擺爛」2025–2026 高頻自嘲詞；形容主動放棄掙扎、躺平不努力

zh-tw.content:
週一的我：這週要拚一波。
週三的我：先擺爛一下下。
週五的我：已經擺到地心。

別找我，
我跟我的爛黏在一起了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「擺爛」是 2026 Threads 高頻自嘲詞，指「乾脆放棄掙扎、躺著不努力」。比「我累了」更有畫面也更可愛，週中崩潰或專案做不完的時候貼這句最對味
variations: 升級版：「不是我想擺爛，是事情多到我只能用擺爛回應。」
回覆版：「他問我計畫是什麼，我只回兩個字：擺爛。」

en.content:
Monday me: gonna grind hard this week.
Wednesday me: just gonna slack off a tiny bit.
Friday me: slacked all the way to the earth's core.

Don't look for me —
me and my slacking are fused now.
en.editorNote: 'Slacking-out' (擺爛) is a 2026 Taiwan Threads self-roast for deliberately giving up the fight and lying flat. More vivid and lovable than 'I'm tired' — perfect for a midweek meltdown or an unfinishable project
variations: Upgrade: 'It's not that I want to slack out — there's just so much that slacking is my only possible response.'
Reply version: 'He asked what my plan was. I sent back one word: slacking.'

items.sourceNote: 「水逆」源自占星水星逆行；2026 Threads 用來甩鍋一切不順的萬用藉口

zh-tw.content:
手機摔了，
錢包掉了，
喜歡的人讀了不回。

不是我的問題，
是水逆。

等水星轉正，
我一定會變成更好的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「水逆」原本是占星說法，2026 Threads 已經變成「萬用甩鍋藉口」——任何不順都能推給它。重點是說完還是不會改，這就是它好笑的地方
variations: 戀愛版：「他已讀我，不是不愛我，是水逆害他手指動不了。」
工作版：「報表算錯三次，水逆，跟我的能力完全無關。」

en.content:
Phone cracked,
wallet lost,
crush left me on read.

Not my fault —
Mercury's in retrograde.

Once it goes direct,
I'll definitely become a better person.
en.editorNote: 'Mercury retrograde' (水逆) started as astrology and by 2026 became Taiwan Threads' all-purpose blame-shift — pin any streak of bad luck on it. The joke is you say it and then change absolutely nothing
variations: Dating version: 'He left me on read — it's not that he doesn't love me, retrograde just froze his fingers.'
Work version: 'Miscalculated the report three times. Retrograde. Has nothing to do with my skills.'

items.sourceNote: 「emo」2025–2026 Threads 高頻情緒詞；形容突然低落、想很多的深夜狀態

zh-tw.content:
白天明明好好的，
一到半夜十二點
就開始 emo。

不是真的難過，
是腦袋偷偷打開了
那個叫「想太多」的資料夾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「emo」在 2026 Threads 是「突然低落、莫名想很多」的代名詞，多半發生在深夜。比「難過」更輕、更日常，深夜限動配這句最對味
variations: 自嘲版：「我不是憂鬱，我只是定時 emo，每天午夜準時上線。」
關心版：「看你最近常 emo，要不要我陪你語音到睡著？」

en.content:
Totally fine all day,
then the clock hits midnight
and I start to emo.

Not actually sad —
my brain just quietly opened
that folder labeled 'overthinking.'
en.editorNote: 'Emo' on 2026 Taiwan Threads means a sudden, low-key spiral of overthinking, usually after midnight. Lighter and more everyday than 'sad' — the perfect caption for a late-night story
variations: Self-roast: 'I'm not depressed, I'm just on a schedule — emo logs on at midnight sharp every day.'
Caring version: 'You've been emo a lot lately — want me to stay on call until you fall asleep?'

items.sourceNote: 「純愛戰士」2025–2026 Threads 用語；自稱只相信真愛、拒絕速食關係的人

zh-tw.content:
別人在玩三條曖昧線，
我還在相信
一個人愛一輩子。

純愛戰士，
孤獨，
但不投降。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「純愛戰士」是 2026 Threads 自稱「只相信真愛、不玩速食關係」的人，常帶點自嘲。在一片已讀亂回的時代，認領這個身分意外地浪漫
variations: 自嘲版：「純愛戰士的下場就是單身三年，但我問心無愧。」
宣言版：「我不接曖昧，只接真心，純愛戰士不打折。」

en.content:
Everyone else is juggling three situationships,
and I still believe in
loving one person for life.

A pure-love warrior:
lonely,
but never surrendering.
en.editorNote: 'Pure-love warrior' (純愛戰士) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' self-label for someone who only believes in real love and refuses casual flings, usually with a wink. In an age of left-on-read, claiming the title is oddly romantic
variations: Self-roast: 'The fate of a pure-love warrior is three years single, but my conscience is clear.'
Manifesto: 'I don't do situationships, only the real thing — pure-love warriors don't offer discounts.'

items.sourceNote: 「老best」結合「老友」與 best friend，2026 Threads 形容最麻吉的那一位

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點傳「我餓了」
會立刻回「下樓」的那個人，
就是老best。

不用解釋，
不用客套，
直接出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「老best」是「老友」加 best friend 的混血詞，2026 Threads 用來指那位最不用裝、隨時能找的死黨。標記摯友的限動或合照配這句剛剛好，比「閨密」更有現在感
variations: 認證版：「能看我素顏崩潰還不嫌棄的，才有資格叫老best。」
撒嬌版：「@你 老best，這格留給你，別人坐我翻臉。」

en.content:
The one who replies 'coming down' the second
you text 'I'm hungry' at 2am —
that's your old-best.

No explaining,
no politeness,
just shows up.
en.editorNote: 'Old-best' (老best) mashes 'old friend' with 'best friend' — 2026 Taiwan Threads' word for the ride-or-die you never have to perform for. Perfect caption for a story or photo tagging your closest person; reads fresher than 'BFF'
variations: Certified version: 'If they can watch you melt down bare-faced and still stay, they qualify as old-best.'
Clingy version: '@you, old-best, this seat's yours — anyone else sits here and I lose it.'

items.sourceNote: 「0尊」=「零尊重」，2026 Threads 形容某人言行誇張到讓人毫無敬意

zh-tw.content:
他借我五百
拖了三個月，
還回我一句「有差嗎」。

0尊。

我對他的尊重
餘額：零。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「0尊」就是「零尊重」，2026 Threads 用來形容某人講話或做事誇張到讓你瞬間失去敬意。比直接罵更有梗，朋友耍白目時甩一個 0尊，輕鬆又到位
variations: 回應版：「他遲到一小時還問我幹嘛臭臉，0尊，連解釋都不配。」
自嘲版：「我昨天把鬧鐘設成下午一點，對自己 0尊。」

en.content:
He borrowed five hundred,
dragged it out three months,
then hit me with 'does it even matter?'

Zero-spect.

My respect for him —
balance: zero.
en.editorNote: 'Zero-spect' (0尊, short for 'zero respect') is 2026 Taiwan Threads slang for when someone's words or actions instantly drain all the regard you had for them. Funnier than cursing — toss it out when a friend's being shameless
variations: Reply version: 'He was an hour late and asked why I looked annoyed. Zero-spect — doesn't even deserve an explanation.'
Self-roast: 'I set my alarm for 1pm yesterday. Zero-spect for myself.'

items.sourceNote: 「張中和」諧音「尊重我」，2026 Threads 用人名梗要求被認真對待

zh-tw.content:
我認真講出夢想，
全桌笑成一團。

喂，張中和呢？

夢想可以小，
但張中和不能少。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「張中和」是「尊重我」的諧音人名梗，2026 Threads 用來在被當玩笑時半開玩笑地討尊重。把抽象要求變成一個人名，吐槽起來更萌、更不傷感情
variations: 群組版：「我精心選的餐廳被秒否決，請各位拿出一點張中和。」
撒嬌版：「我新髮型欸，張中和一下會怎樣。」

en.content:
I shared my dream out loud,
the whole table cracked up.

Hey — where's my respect?

Dreams can be small,
but respect can't go missing.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin '張中和' is a fake personal name that sounds exactly like 'respect me' — 2026 Taiwan Threads uses it to half-jokingly demand to be taken seriously. Turning an abstract ask into a 'person' makes the complaint cuter and less prickly
variations: Group chat version: 'My carefully chosen restaurant got shot down in one second. A little respect, please.'
Clingy version: 'It's my new haircut — would a little respect kill you?'

items.sourceNote: 2026 Threads 熱議：年輕人不講「笑死」改講「超好笑」，流行語復古化現象

zh-tw.content:
現在年輕人不講「笑死」了，
要講「超好笑」。

我以為「超好笑」
是更老的人在講的，
欸看來連流行語也吹起復古風。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 Threads 熱議的反差現象：年輕人開始放下「笑死」，回頭講看似更樸素的「超好笑」。重點是這種「返璞歸真」的潮，拿來自嘲跟不跟得上都好用
variations: 跟風版：「我決定戒掉『笑死』，從今天起只說『超好笑』，當個復古潮人。」
吐槽版：「他傳『笑死』，我回『超好笑』，瞬間分出誰比較潮。」

en.content:
Kids these days don't say 'dying-laughing' anymore —
now it's 'so funny.'

I thought 'so funny'
was what older folks said.
Guess even slang is going retro.
en.editorNote: A 2026 Taiwan Threads talking point: young people are dropping 'dying-laughing' (笑死) and circling back to the plainer 'so funny' (超好笑). The whole joke is that 'going back to basics' is the new trend — great for roasting yourself whether or not you keep up
variations: Bandwagon version: 'I'm quitting dying-laughing. From today I only say so funny — retro and proud.'
Roast version: 'He texted dying-laughing, I replied so funny. Instantly clear who's more current.'

items.sourceNote: 「主打一個」2025–2026 高頻句型；強調某種態度或風格，後接核心關鍵詞

zh-tw.content:
別人週末主打一個精緻露營，
我週末
主打一個躺到地球停止轉動。

各有各的浪漫，
只是我的比較省錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「主打一個」是 2025–2026 高頻句型，後面接一個關鍵詞，用來宣告自己這次的核心態度或風格。語氣帶點自我調侃，講生活選擇或追星心境都好套
variations: 戀愛版：「我談戀愛主打一個慢熱，急的人請左轉。」
工作版：「今天上班主打一個人在心不在，靈魂已經在放假。」

en.content:
Other people's weekends headline 'aesthetic camping.'
My weekend
headlines 'lie down till the planet stops spinning.'

Different kinds of romance —
mine just costs less.
en.editorNote: 'Headlining one thing' (主打一個) is a 2025–2026 high-frequency sentence frame: you tack on a keyword to declare your core attitude or vibe for the moment. It carries a self-deprecating wink — works for life choices or stan-mode confessions alike
variations: Dating version: 'My love life headlines slow-burn. If you're in a rush, hang a left.'
Work version: 'Today at the office I'm headlining body-present-soul-absent. My spirit's already on vacation.'

items.sourceNote: 「廢水／肥仔水」2026 Z 世代用語；指高糖高熱量手搖飲的暱稱

zh-tw.content:
醫生說我要少糖。

我點頭，
然後叫了一杯黑糖珍奶。

廢水嘛，
喝下去是會後悔，
但不喝會先死。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「廢水／肥仔水」是 2026 Z 世代對高糖高熱量手搖飲的暱稱，半自嘲半放縱。明知不健康還是要喝的那種，貼在週五犒賞自己的限動最對味
variations: 揪團版：「下午三點，誰要跟我一起墮落，廢水我請。」
減肥版：「我這禮拜唯一的運動，是走去買肥仔水的那段路。」

en.content:
Doctor told me to cut the sugar.

I nodded,
then ordered a brown-sugar boba.

It's junk-juice —
you regret drinking it,
but not drinking it kills you first.
en.editorNote: 'Junk-juice / fatty-water' (廢水／肥仔水) is 2026 Gen Z's affectionate nickname for high-sugar bubble tea and the like — half self-roast, half full surrender. For the Friday treat-yourself story everyone secretly relates to
variations: Group-order version: '3pm. Who's spiraling with me? Junk-juice on me.'
Diet version: 'My only exercise this week was the walk to buy fatty-water.'

items.sourceUrl: https://www.edigest.hk/職場熱話/潮語-sls-sldpk-老best-張中和-經一提你/
items.sourceNote: 「老best」2026 流行語；老友＋best friend 合體，稱呼最親密的死黨

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點崩潰，
第一個傳訊息的對象——

我的老best。

不用解釋前因後果，
他只回一句「下樓，我買了廢水」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「老best」是「老友」加「best friend」的合體，2026 用來稱呼那種不用客套、最親密的死黨。寫友情貼文或標記閨密時超好用，比「閨蜜」少一點距離
variations: 標記版：「這張醜照只有老best能看，外人請自動跳過。」
感性版：「成年後最難得的，是還留得住一個老best。」

en.content:
2am, falling apart,
first person I text —

my ol'-best.

No backstory needed.
They just reply: 'come downstairs, I bought junk-juice.'
en.editorNote: 'Ol'-best' (老best) fuses 'old friend' with 'best friend' — 2026 shorthand for the no-formalities, closest-of-the-close ride-or-die. Great for friendship posts or tagging your person, warmer than the stiff 'bestie'
variations: Tag version: 'Only my ol'-best is allowed to see this cursed photo. Everyone else, scroll on.'
Sentimental version: 'The rarest thing about adulthood is still having one ol'-best left.'

items.sourceNote: 「ven 一下」2026 Threads 互動動詞；源自分享／轉貼動作的暱稱，連蔡英文都 ven 過

zh-tw.content:
看到太好笑的貼文，
手比腦快，
直接 ven 一下。

等回過神，
我已經把這篇
推到全宇宙的動態。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「ven 一下」是 2026 Threads 把「分享／轉貼」動作暱稱化的高頻動詞，連前總統蔡英文都 ven 過國民情侶的影片。看到好東西想擴散時用，比「我幫你轉」潮
variations: 互拉版：「這篇我先 ven 了，換你 ven 回來，我們互相壯大彼此的演算法。」
懶人版：「不想打字就 ven 一下，誠意一樣到位。」

en.content:
Saw a post too funny to keep to myself.
Hands faster than brain,
I just ven'd it.

By the time I came to,
I'd already pushed it
to everyone's feed in the galaxy.
en.editorNote: 'Ven it' is 2026 Threads' pet verb for sharing or reposting — even former President Tsai Ing-wen ven'd the famous couple's clip. Use it when something's too good not to spread, way fresher than 'let me reshare that'
variations: Mutual-boost version: 'I ven'd yours, now ven mine back — let's pump each other's algorithms.'
Lazy version: 'Too lazy to type? Just ven it. Same amount of love.'

items.sourceNote: 「0尊」2026 流行語；「零尊重」縮寫，自嘲式表達擺爛、不裝了

zh-tw.content:
早上對世界還有點期待。

中午被改第三次需求後——

0尊。

對工作、對人生、對那份試算表，
通通零尊重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「0尊」是「零尊重」的縮寫，2026 用來自嘲「我已經不裝了、徹底擺爛」。語氣比生氣更躺平，職場吐槽或耍廢宣言都好用
variations: 宣言版：「今天起床決定 0尊度日，誰惹我我就翻白眼給他看。」
回應版：「他又遲到，我直接 0尊，連假笑都省了。」

en.content:
Had some hope for the world this morning.

Then the specs got changed a third time at noon —

Zero-respect mode.

For the job, for life, for that spreadsheet —
respect level: zero.
en.editorNote: 'Zero-respect' (0尊) shortens 'zero respect' — 2026 self-roast for 'I've stopped pretending, I'm fully checked out.' More horizontal than angry, perfect for work rants or a slacker manifesto
variations: Manifesto version: 'Woke up and chose zero-respect mode. Mess with me and you get the eye-roll.'
Reply version: 'He was late again. Straight to zero-respect — didn't even fake the smile.'

items.sourceNote: 「你今天吃陳皮糖了嗎」源自 2026 爆紅劇《逐玉》台詞；Threads 上演化為帶梗的問候語

zh-tw.content:
早安，
你今天吃陳皮糖了嗎？

沒吃也沒關係，
我把今天的甜
先分你一半。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「你今天吃陳皮糖了嗎」源自 2026 爆紅劇的金句，Threads 上變成一句帶梗的問候語，等於「你今天過得還行嗎」。傳給想關心又怕太正經的對象剛剛好
variations: 曖昧版：「別人問你吃飽沒，我問你今天吃陳皮糖了嗎，差別在我想多聊兩句。」
回應版：「還沒吃，但看到你訊息，嘴裡突然有點甜。」

en.content:
Morning.
Have you had your tangerine-peel candy today?

If not, that's okay —
I'll split today's sweetness
with you.
en.editorNote: 'Have you had your tangerine-peel candy today?' comes from a hit 2026 Taiwan drama line and turned into a meme-y Threads greeting — basically 'has your day been okay?' Perfect when you want to check in on someone without sounding too serious
variations: Flirty version: 'Other people ask if you've eaten. I ask if you've had your candy — the difference is I want to keep talking.'
Reply version: 'Not yet, but your text just made my mouth a little sweet.'

items.sourceNote: 「班味」2025–2026 打工人高頻用語；形容被上班狀態浸透後的疲憊氣質

zh-tw.content:
週五晚上照鏡子，
眼神空洞，肩膀下垂，
連笑都笑得很省力。

班味好重。

重到我懷疑
香水都蓋不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「班味」形容一個人被上班狀態浸透後散發出的疲憊氣質，2025–2026 打工人語境超穩定。自嘲下班後整個人垮掉的樣子最對味
variations: 對照版：「放假第一天臉是發光的，收假前一晚班味就自動回來了。」
互嗆版：「你這身班味，洗三次澡都洗不掉，認命吧。」

en.content:
Friday night, I look in the mirror —
hollow eyes, slumped shoulders,
even my smile is running on low battery.

The office-stink is strong.

So strong I doubt
any cologne could cover it.
en.editorNote: 'Office-stink' (班味) describes the worn-down aura a person gives off once the job has fully soaked into them — a rock-solid 2025–2026 working-person term. Best for self-roasting how you collapse the moment you clock out
variations: Contrast version: 'Day one of vacation my face glows. The night before going back, the office-stink returns on its own.'
Teasing version: 'That office-stink on you won't wash off in three showers. Just accept it.'

items.sourceNote: 「大癲」2026 跨海華語圈 Gen Z 用語；形容某事誇張到瘋狂、超展開

zh-tw.content:
他求婚場地選在
我們第一次吵架的便利商店。

大癲。

但我哭著答應了，
所以是好的那種大癲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「大癲」是 2026 Gen Z 形容「誇張到瘋狂、超展開」的用語，褒貶都能用，重點在那個失控感。配在反差或浮誇場景最有戲
variations: 吐槽版：「他穿西裝來吃鹽酥雞，大癲，但意外地有說服力。」
讚嘆版：「這部結局大癲，我看完三天還在原地震驚。」

en.content:
He picked our proposal spot:
the convenience store where we had our first fight.

Unhinged.

But I said yes through tears,
so it's the good kind of unhinged.
en.editorNote: 'Unhinged' (大癲) is 2026 Gen Z slang for 'wild, over-the-top, plot-twist energy' — works as praise or critique, the point is the out-of-control vibe. Best paired with anything dramatic or extra
variations: Roast version: 'He wore a full suit to eat popcorn chicken. Unhinged — but weirdly convincing.'
Praise version: 'That finale was unhinged. Three days later I'm still standing here shook.'

items.sourceNote: 「已老實求放過」2026 Threads 投降式自嘲；表示認輸、不再硬撐

zh-tw.content:
減肥第三天，
蛋糕在冰箱裡叫我的名字。

已老實，求放過。

我不跟意志力打了，
它贏，我吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「已老實求放過」是 2026 Threads 投降式自嘲，意思是「我認輸了、別再為難我」。撐不住、躺平、舉白旗的瞬間最好用，比「我放棄」可愛十倍
variations: 上班版：「需求改第五次，已老實求放過，你說什麼都對。」
戀愛版：「他笑起來有酒窩，我已老實求放過，根本招架不住。」

en.content:
Day three of the diet,
the cake in the fridge is calling my name.

I confess, please let me off.

I'm done fighting my willpower.
It wins, I eat.
en.editorNote: 'I confess, please let me off' (已老實求放過) is 2026 Threads' white-flag self-roast — meaning 'I surrender, stop torturing me.' Perfect for the moment you give up, lie flat, or wave the towel. Way cuter than just 'I quit'
variations: Office version: 'Specs changed a fifth time. I confess, please let me off — whatever you say is right.'
Crush version: 'His smile has dimples. I confess, please let me off — I have zero defenses left.'

items.sourceNote: 「愛你老己」2025–2026 年度流行語；台語腔「愛你自己」，輕鬆叫人善待自己

zh-tw.content:
加班加到懷疑人生，
別檢討自己哪裡不夠好。

愛你老己。

先去吃一頓好的，
剩下的明天再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「愛你老己」是台語腔的「愛你自己」，2026 年度流行語，語氣輕鬆不說教。朋友在低潮硬撐時傳這句，比「加油」更有溫度也更不油
variations: 回應版：「他說又把自己累垮了，我只回四個字：愛你老己。」
自嘲版：「今天決定愛你老己，下午茶我請我自己。」

en.content:
Worked overtime till you're questioning everything?
Don't sit there listing your flaws.

Love yo'self.

Go eat something good first.
The rest can wait till tomorrow.
en.editorNote: 'Love yo'self' translates 愛你老己, a dialect-inflected spin on 'love yourself' — a top 2025–2026 phrase that's playful, never preachy. Send it to a friend who's grinding themselves down; warmer than 'you got this' and not cheesy
variations: Reply version: 'She said she burned herself out again. I just sent back: love yo'self.'
Self-aware: 'Decided to love myself today — treating me to afternoon tea, on me.'

items.sourceNote: 「敬自己一杯」2025 年度十大流行語；接受努力與平凡，與自己溫柔和解

zh-tw.content:
今年沒升職，
沒變瘦，
也沒談成戀愛。

但每天都有好好起床，
敬自己一杯。

平凡也是一種準時交件。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「敬自己一杯」是 2025 年度十大流行語，意思是接受自己的努力跟平凡、跟自己溫柔和解。年末回顧或撐過一段低潮時用最有共感，不需要先成功才能敬
variations: 回應版：「他說今年好像什麼都沒做成，我說：那更要敬自己一杯。」
日常版：「今天只完成一件事，但有完成，敬自己一杯。」

en.content:
No promotion this year,
no weight lost,
no romance landed.

But I got out of bed every single day.
Here's to me.

Ordinary still counts as delivered on time.
en.editorNote: 'Here's to me' translates 敬自己一杯 (raise a glass to yourself), a 2025 top-ten phrase about accepting your effort and your ordinariness, and making peace with yourself. Perfect for a year-end reflection or surviving a rough patch — you don't have to win first to toast
variations: Reply version: 'He said it feels like he accomplished nothing this year. I said: all the more reason to toast yourself.'
Everyday: 'Got exactly one thing done today, but I did it. Here's to me.'

items.sourceNote: 「含機量爆表」2026 AI 時代用語；形容某內容或場景 AI 參與度極高

zh-tw.content:
他傳來的情書
排版工整、用詞高雅、零錯字。

含機量爆表。

真心話通常
會有一兩個錯字才對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「含機量爆表」是 2026 AI 時代專用吐槽，形容某段文字或內容 AI 參與度高到藏不住。發現朋友訊息太完美、貼文太工整時甩這句，幽默又精準
variations: 工作版：「這封道歉信含機量爆表，連標點都在跟我裝熟。」
自嘲版：「我這篇貼文含機量爆表，但本人含淚量也爆表。」

en.content:
The love letter he sent —
perfect formatting, elegant word choices, zero typos.

AI-content meter off the charts.

Real feelings usually
come with a typo or two.
en.editorNote: 'AI-content meter off the charts' translates 含機量爆表, the 2026 AI-era clapback for text that's suspiciously, un-hideably machine-made. Drop it when a friend's message is too polished or a post is too perfectly formatted — funny and dead-on
variations: Work version: 'That apology email's AI-content meter is off the charts — even the punctuation is fake-friendly.'
Self-roast: 'This post of mine has a sky-high AI-content meter, but my actual tear count is just as high.'

items.sourceNote: 「馬到倒成功／哭哭馬」2026 馬年諧音迷因；用哭臉馬玩具自嘲弱弱可愛

zh-tw.content:
新年願望：馬到成功。

實際進度：馬到倒成功。

我就是那隻哭哭馬，
四隻腳都在，
只是站不太穩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「馬到倒成功」是 2026 馬年諧音迷因，把祝福句反過來玩成自嘲，搭配哭臉馬玩具的「弱弱可愛」。年初立志失敗、計劃跌倒時用最應景
variations: 上班版：「年度目標達成率 5%，馬到倒成功，但我有在努力站起來。」
回應版：「他說今年又躺平了，我回：哭哭馬抱抱，一起倒。」

en.content:
New Year's wish: instant success.

Actual progress: instant collap-cess.

I'm that little crying horse —
all four legs accounted for,
just can't quite stand up straight.
en.editorNote: 'Instant collap-cess' riffs on 馬到倒成功, a 2026 Year-of-the-Horse pun meme that flips the lucky 'instant success' blessing into self-deprecating failure, paired with sad-faced horse plushies and their 'weakling cuteness.' Perfect when your new-year resolutions face-plant
variations: Work version: 'Annual goal completion: 5%. Instant collap-cess — but I'm trying to stand back up.'
Reply version: 'He said he gave up and lay flat again this year. I said: crying-horse hug, let's collapse together.'

items.sourceNote: 「同溫層」長青用語 2026 Z 世代仍高頻；形容只跟想法相近的人取暖

zh-tw.content:
我以為全世界都討厭週一。

結果只是我的同溫層
太厚了。

厚到我以為
它就是世界。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「同溫層」形容演算法把想法相近的人聚在一起、彼此取暖。2026 Z 世代仍高頻使用，常帶自嘲意味。發現自己以偏概全時用這句，清醒又好笑
variations: 回應版：「他說沒人喜歡那部片，我說：可能你同溫層太保暖。」
自嘲版：「我的同溫層厚到可以過冬，難怪沒看過反方意見。」

en.content:
I thought the whole world hated Mondays.

Turns out it was just
my echo chamber
being extra cozy.

Cozy enough
that I mistook it for the world.
en.editorNote: 'Echo chamber' translates 同溫層 — the algorithm-built bubble where like-minded people huddle for warmth. Still high-frequency among 2026 Gen Z, usually with a self-aware wink. Pull it out when you catch yourself overgeneralizing — sharp and funny
variations: Reply version: 'He said nobody likes that movie. I said: maybe your echo chamber's just really well-insulated.'
Self-roast: 'My echo chamber is warm enough to winter in — no wonder I've never seen the other side.'

items.sourceNote: 「sldpk」00 後羅馬拼音縮寫梗，原為「笑死」變體；2026 Threads 留言區高頻使用

zh-tw.content:
他說他減肥是認真的，
然後手裡拿著第二份炸雞。

sldpk。

打字都來不及笑，
只能丟四個字母
表示我已經笑到不行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「sldpk」是 2026 年輕世代的羅馬拼音縮寫梗，等於「笑到不行」的打字版。比直接打「笑死」更有黑話感，丟在朋友的迷因或耍蠢貼文底下最對味
variations: 回應版：「他截圖自己的待辦清單，第一項是『早點睡』，發文時間凌晨三點，sldpk。」
純反應版：「看到那張貓咪卡在紙箱的照片，我只回 sldpk，多一個字都嫌累。」

en.content:
He swears the diet is serious this time,
holding his second basket of fried chicken.

sldpk.

No time to even type out a laugh —
just four letters
to say I'm absolutely done.
en.editorNote: 'sldpk' is a 2026 Romanized-abbreviation meme among young Mandarin/Cantonese internet users — shorthand for 'dying of laughter.' More insider-coded than spelling out 'lol'; drop it under a friend's meme or a self-own post
variations: Reply version: 'He screenshotted his to-do list — item one was "sleep early," posted at 3am. sldpk.'
Pure reaction: 'Saw the photo of the cat stuck in the box and just replied sldpk. One more character would've been too much effort.'

items.sourceNote: Gen Z 報感情狀態改用「報 stage（拍拖階段）」取代單純的單身／穩定；2026 新一代戀愛回報法

zh-tw.content:
閨密問：「你們現在到哪了？」

我說：報個 stage 給你——

已讀秒回階段，
但還沒到敢丟醜照階段。

中間隔了一整座山。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 新一代回報感情狀態不再只說「單身」或「穩定」，而是「報 stage」——精準說明走到哪個曖昧階段。傳給天天追問你進度的閨密最好用，比一句「還好」資訊量大十倍
variations: 進度條版：「報個 stage：已從『裝沒事』升級到『偷看他在線』，離脫單還有三關。」
自嘲版：「我的 stage 卡在已讀亂回兩個禮拜了，需要客服協助。」

en.content:
Bestie asks: 'So where are you two at?'

Let me report the stage —

We're at the reply-within-seconds stage,
but not yet the brave-enough-to-send-ugly-selfies stage.

There's a whole mountain in between.
en.editorNote: In 2026, Gen Z stopped reporting relationships as just 'single' or 'taken' and started 'reporting the stage' — naming exactly which phase of the situationship you're in. Perfect reply for the friend who asks for updates daily; ten times more info than 'it's fine'
variations: Progress-bar version: 'Stage report: upgraded from "playing it cool" to "checking if he's online." Three levels left until single-no-more.'
Self-roast: 'My stage has been stuck at read-and-mis-reply for two weeks. I need customer support.'

items.sourceNote: 「社交電池」2025–2026 Threads 高頻自我描述；把社交能量量化成電量

zh-tw.content:
聚會才過一小時，
我的社交電池
剩 8%。

再聊下去
我會自動關機，
別擔心，
是系統保護不是討厭你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「社交電池」是 2026 把手機電量套到社交能量上的高頻說法，I 人尤其有感。先預告電量比硬撐到當機更體貼，朋友也不會誤會你冷淡
variations: 婉拒版：「今天社交電池只充了 30%，這場我先存著，下次滿電再赴約。」
互相版：「我們兩個 I 人約見面，電池加起來剛好夠撐一杯咖啡。」

en.content:
One hour into the hangout
and my social battery
is at 8%.

Keep talking
and I'll auto-shut-down.
Don't worry —
it's system protection, not that I dislike you.
en.editorNote: 'Social battery' (社交電池) is the 2026 high-frequency move of mapping phone-battery language onto your social energy — I-types especially feel it. Announcing the percentage beats white-knuckling to a crash, and friends won't read it as coldness
variations: Polite decline: 'My social battery only charged to 30% today. I'll save this one and show up fully charged next time.'
Mutual version: 'Two I-types meeting up — our batteries combined last exactly one cup of coffee.'

items.sourceNote: 「kam4」00 後縮寫，源自「尷尬」加粵語諧音「4＝死」；形容尷尬到極點

zh-tw.content:
在電梯裡跟前任
四目相對。

他按 3 樓，
我也住 3 樓。

kam4。

那十秒
比一整年還長。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「kam4」是 2026 年輕世代縮寫，「kam」取自「尷尬」、「4」諧音「死」，合起來就是「尷尬到死」。比打「好尷尬」更有戲，碰到社死現場用最對味
variations: 群組版：「我把吐槽老闆的訊息傳到工作群，kam4，已截圖留念當遺照。」
升級版：「揮手以為對方在跟我打招呼，結果他是在叫我後面的人，kam4 到想原地蒸發。」

en.content:
Locked eyes with my ex
in the elevator.

He hits floor 3.
I also live on floor 3.

kam4.

Those ten seconds
lasted longer than a whole year.
en.editorNote: 'kam4' is a 2026 Gen Z abbreviation — 'kam' from the Mandarin 'gan-ga' (awkward) plus '4' as a homophone for 'death,' so it reads 'awkward to death.' More theatrical than typing 'so awkward'; built for social-death moments
variations: Group-chat version: 'Sent the message roasting my boss to the work group. kam4. Screenshotted it for the funeral.'
Upgrade: 'Waved back thinking he was greeting me — turns out he meant the person behind me. kam4 enough to evaporate on the spot.'

items.sourceNote: 「草」源自日文網路笑聲符號（w 排成一片像草）；2026 台灣 Threads 跨海挪用為笑點符號

zh-tw.content:
他認真說
他這次一定戒手搖。

草。

話還沒講完，
手已經滑開外送 App，
長出一整片草原。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「草」源自日文網路文化——一排 w（笑）連起來像一片草，所以直接用「草」當笑點符號。2026 台灣 Threads 跨海挪用，比「哈哈」省力又有梗，戳到笑點時丟一個字最潮
variations: 加碼版：「他說減肥是長期抗戰，下一秒點了大薯加可樂，草草草，整片草原。」
回應版：「看到那個跌倒還比 YA 的影片，我只回一個草，笑點全在裡面。」

en.content:
He says, dead serious,
he's quitting bubble tea for good this time.

lol (grass).

Before he even finishes
his hand's already opening the delivery app —
a whole field of laughter sprouting.
en.editorNote: 'Grass' (草) comes from Japanese net culture — a row of 'w' (the laugh marker) lined up looks like blades of grass, so people just type 'grass.' Taiwan Threads borrowed it across the strait in 2026; lighter than 'hahaha' and far more coded. One character when something cracks you up
variations: Doubling down: 'He said dieting is a long war, then ordered large fries and a Coke. grass grass grass — a whole meadow.'
Reply version: 'Saw the clip of him tripping and still throwing up a peace sign. Just replied: grass. The whole joke's in there.'

items.sourceNote: 「含機量」2026 流行語，形容某場合或公司 AI 參與程度極高

zh-tw.content:
今天開會
五份簡報四份是 AI 做的，
回覆信件的也是 AI。

含機量爆表。

整間公司
剩我一個是純手工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「含機量」是 2026 形容「AI 參與程度」的詞，越高代表越多東西是機器代勞。配在吐槽自己快被 AI 取代、或讚嘆某專案超科技的場景都好用
variations: 自嘲版：「我的報告含機量 0%，全手刻，老闆居然嫌我慢。」
讚嘆版：「這支廣告含機量爆表，從腳本到配音全 AI，還比真人有感情。」

en.content:
Today's meeting:
four of five decks were made by AI,
so were the email replies.

AI-saturation off the charts.

In this whole company
I'm the last handmade item.
en.editorNote: 'AI-saturation' (含機量) is a 2026 term for how much of something an AI did — the higher the reading, the more is machine-made. Works for venting about being replaced or marveling at how high-tech a project got
variations: Self-roast: 'My report's AI-saturation is 0%, fully handcrafted, and the boss called me slow.'
Admiring: 'This ad's AI-saturation is off the charts — script to voiceover all AI, and it has more feeling than a real person.'

items.sourceNote: 「歸剛欸」台語諧音，2026 配音潮爆紅；嫌人整天沒事亂搞找麻煩

zh-tw.content:
他凌晨三點傳訊息問我
「在嗎」。

歸剛欸。

沒事就睡覺，
不要整晚找事，
地球不缺你這顆失眠的螺絲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「歸剛欸」是台語諧音，意思是「整天閒著沒事亂搞找麻煩」。2026 靠一波配音潮爆紅，吐槽朋友白目或自己被無聊事煩到時最對味
variations: 工作版：「他改完需求又改回來，歸剛欸，當我滑鼠是免費的喔。」
回應版：「群組半夜還在吵要不要團購，我只回三個字：歸剛欸。」

en.content:
He texted at 3am asking
'you up?'

Got nothing better to do, huh.

If you're free, go to sleep.
Stop manufacturing drama all night —
the planet doesn't need one more insomniac screw loose.
en.editorNote: 'Got nothing better to do' renders 歸剛欸, a Taiwanese phonetic for someone idling around stirring up pointless trouble. It blew up in 2026 via a dubbing wave — perfect for roasting a clueless friend or your own annoyances
variations: Work version: 'He changed the spec, then changed it back. Got nothing better to do — you think my mouse clicks are free?'
Reply version: 'The group chat is still arguing about a bulk order at midnight. I just sent: got nothing better to do.'

items.sourceNote: 「哭哭馬」2026 爆紅解壓公仔；嘴縫反的委屈馬，社畜情緒嘴替

zh-tw.content:
桌上擺了一隻哭哭馬。

身上繡「馬上有錢」，
嘴卻被縫反，
笑容變成苦瓜臉。

我不能哭，
所以牠替我哭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「哭哭馬」是 2026 爆紅的解壓公仔——嘴巴被縫反、一臉委屈，成了社畜的情緒嘴替。傳這句給每天硬撐上班的朋友，比說「加油」更懂他
variations: 辦公室版：「主管又改第五次方案，我看了一眼桌上的哭哭馬，牠也很無奈。」
自嘲版：「我不是悲觀，我只是長期跟哭哭馬同步率 100%。」

en.content:
There's a cry-cry horse on my desk.

'Money's coming' stitched on its side,
but its mouth was sewn on backwards,
so the smile became a frown.

I'm not allowed to cry,
so it cries for me.
en.editorNote: 'Cry-cry horse' (哭哭馬) is a 2026 viral stress-relief plush — its mouth sewn on backwards into a permanent pout, it became the office worker's emotional stand-in. Send to a friend white-knuckling through work; it lands softer than 'hang in there'
variations: Office version: 'The boss revised the plan a fifth time. I glanced at my cry-cry horse — it's just as helpless as me.'
Self-roast: 'I'm not pessimistic, I'm just running 100% sync with my cry-cry horse.'

items.sourceNote: 「龍蝦」2026 AI 智能體 OpenClaw 的網路暱稱；爆紅 AI agent 代稱

zh-tw.content:
報告要不要我自己寫？

不用，
問龍蝦就好。

牠十秒寫完，
我十秒按 Ctrl+C。

分工合作，
各司其職。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「龍蝦」是 2026 爆紅 AI 智能體 OpenClaw 的網路暱稱（claw＝爪子）。把 AI 工具擬人化成龍蝦來講最有梗，吐槽自己什麼都丟給 AI 時用最好笑
variations: 懶人版：「今天的腦袋外包給龍蝦，我負責呼吸跟領薪水。」
崩潰版：「龍蝦當機那天，我才發現自己原來什麼都不會。」

en.content:
Should I write the report myself?

Nah,
just ask the lobster.

It finishes in ten seconds,
I hit Ctrl+C in ten seconds.

Division of labor,
everyone in their lane.
en.editorNote: 'Lobster' (龍蝦) is the 2026 net nickname for the breakout AI agent OpenClaw (claw → lobster). Personifying your AI tool as a lobster is peak meme energy — use it to roast how much you outsource to AI
variations: Lazy version: 'Outsourced my brain to the lobster today. I handle breathing and collecting the paycheck.'
Meltdown: 'The day the lobster went down, I realized I can't actually do anything myself.'

items.sourceNote: 「老海鮮」2026 流行語，形容跟不上潮流、用詞過時的人（不新鮮）

zh-tw.content:
我傳了一個 2018 的貼圖，
姪女已讀三秒，
回我一句：

老海鮮。

原來我不是長輩，
我是放太久的鮭魚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「老海鮮」是 2026 形容「跟不上潮流、不新鮮」的人，從「海鮮要新鮮」延伸而來。被年輕人嫌過時、或自嘲跟不上梗的時候用最有畫面
variations: 自救版：「我承認我老海鮮，但我願意學，請給我一份梗的菜單。」
吐槽版：「他還在用『藍瘦香菇』，老海鮮等級已經到冷凍庫了。」

en.content:
I sent a sticker from 2018,
my niece read it in three seconds,
and replied:

stale seafood.

Turns out I'm not an elder,
I'm a salmon left out too long.
en.editorNote: 'Stale seafood' (老海鮮) is 2026 slang for someone out of touch and no longer fresh — seafood is supposed to be fresh, so. Use it when youngsters call your references dated, or to self-roast for missing a meme
variations: Self-rescue: 'I admit I'm stale seafood, but I'm willing to learn — please hand me a menu of current memes.'
Roast: 'He's still using 2016 memes — his stale-seafood level has reached the freezer.'

items.sourceNote: 「ven」源自 2025-10 早餐店影片「我再 ven 一次」，模仿口音把「問」唸成 ven；2026 Threads 留言高頻語

zh-tw.content:
我再 ven 一次，
你到底要不要跟我去吃宵夜？

第一次你已讀，
第二次你裝睡，
第三次我就自己出發了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「ven」源自 2025 年底一支早餐店影片，把「問」用誇張口音唸成 ven，「我再 ven 一次」整句爆紅到 2026 Threads 留言區。半開玩笑地追問別人時甩一句最有梗，比「我再問一次」軟，又帶點討拍的可愛
variations: 曖昧版：「我再 ven 一次喔——你是不是其實也有點喜歡我？」
工作版：「會議改時間我已經 ven 三次了，再 ven 下去我要變人工智慧客服。」

en.content:
Lemme ven one more time:
are you coming for late-night snacks or not?

First time you left me on read,
second time you fake-slept,
third time I'm just heading out alone.
en.editorNote: 'Ven' comes from a late-2025 Taiwan breakfast-shop clip where someone said 'wen' (問, 'to ask') in an exaggerated accent — 'lemme ven one more time' blew up across 2026 Threads. Use it to half-jokingly re-ask something; softer than 'I'm asking again,' with a built-in plea for attention
variations: Flirty: 'Lemme ven one more time — do you actually kind of like me too?'
Work edition: 'They moved the meeting and I've ven-ed three times now. One more and I become an AI help desk.'

items.sourceNote: 「2026 核冬天」源自一張 AI 生成詭異北極熊圖配「記住：2026 核冬天要來了」；2026 黑色幽默末日迷因

zh-tw.content:
鬧鐘響了，
冷氣帳單來了，
群組又在吵架了。

記住，
2026 核冬天要來了。

反正世界要結束，
那我先賴床三分鐘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「2026 核冬天」源自一張 AI 生成的詭異北極熊圖，被配上「記住：2026 核冬天要來了」變成末日感黑色幽默迷因。把生活中的小崩潰接上這句末日宣言，誇張到好笑，是 2026 最潮的厭世句型
variations: 上班版：「信箱有四十七封未讀，記住，2026 核冬天要來了，回不完也是剛好。」
戀愛版：「他三天沒回我，沒關係，2026 核冬天要來了，我們都撐不到下次約會。」

en.content:
Alarm went off,
the AC bill arrived,
the group chat is fighting again.

Remember:
the 2026 nuclear winter is coming.

The world's ending anyway,
so I'm staying in bed three more minutes.
en.editorNote: '2026 nuclear winter' started as a cursed AI-generated polar bear image captioned 'remember: the 2026 nuclear winter is coming' — it became apocalyptic dark-humor shorthand. Tack the doomsday line onto any small daily meltdown; the absurd over-scaling is the joke, and it's 2026's coolest way to be nihilistic
variations: Office edition: 'Forty-seven unread emails. Remember, the 2026 nuclear winter is coming — not finishing them is honestly on schedule.'
Dating edition: 'He hasn't replied in three days. It's fine, the 2026 nuclear winter is coming, neither of us makes it to the next date anyway.'

items.sourceNote: 「電子榨菜」形容吃飯時用來配飯的影片或短劇；2026 台灣 Threads 高頻生活用語

zh-tw.content:
一個人吃飯，
配的不是滷蛋，
是手機裡那部看第七遍的老劇。

電子榨菜。

劇情我都背得出來了，
但少了它，
飯就吞不下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「電子榨菜」是 2026 台灣 Threads 形容「吃飯時用來下飯的影片、短劇、直播」的高頻用語，重點是熟悉、不費腦、有陪伴感。傳給同樣一個人吃飯也要開影片的朋友，秒共感
variations: 推薦版：「今天的電子榨菜換《武林外傳》，配便當剛剛好，看完飯也吃完。」
自嘲版：「我的問題不是沒朋友，是沒電子榨菜就吃不下飯。」

en.content:
Eating alone,
and my side dish isn't a braised egg —
it's that old show on my phone, seventh rewatch.

Digital pickle.

I can recite every line,
but without it,
the rice just won't go down.
en.editorNote: 'Digital pickle' (電子榨菜) is 2026 Taiwan Threads slang for the video, mini-drama, or stream you put on to make a solo meal go down — the whole point is something familiar, low-effort, and comforting. Send it to a friend who also can't eat alone without a screen running
variations: Recommendation: 'Today's digital pickle is a sitcom rerun — perfectly portioned for one lunchbox. Show ends right as the rice does.'
Self-aware: 'My problem isn't a lack of friends, it's that I can't eat without a digital pickle.'

items.sourceNote: 「含機量」2026 流行語，形容某場合或專案 AI 參與程度極高；隨 AI 普及衍生

zh-tw.content:
這份簡報
含機量爆表。

標題 AI 寫的，
圖 AI 畫的，
結論 AI 掰的，

唯一是我自己生的，
只有那個錯字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「含機量」是 2026 隨 AI 普及爆紅的詞，形容某個東西「AI 參與程度極高」。自嘲整份報告幾乎都靠 AI 時最對味，傳給同樣靠工具求生的同事秒笑
variations: 誇人版：「他一個人三天做完整個專案，含機量爆表，但成品意外好。」
戀愛版：「他傳的早安訊息含機量有點高，但我還是被融化了，這算我輸。」

en.content:
This slide deck
has an off-the-charts AI-content level.

Title written by AI,
images drawn by AI,
conclusion hallucinated by AI —

the only thing I made myself
is that one typo.
en.editorNote: 'AI-content level' (含機量, literally 'machine-content percentage') is 2026 slang born from AI going mainstream — it rates how much of something was actually made by AI. Perfect for roasting a deliverable that's basically all tool output. Send to a coworker also surviving on AI
variations: Praise version: 'He finished a whole project solo in three days — sky-high AI-content level, but the result is weirdly solid.'
Dating version: 'His good-morning text had a suspiciously high AI-content level, but it still melted me, so I guess I lose.'

items.sourceNote: 「0尊」意指零尊重；2026 年輕人回應不得體言行的吐槽用語

zh-tw.content:
我認真說我在減肥，
他默默把最後一塊雞排
放到我面前。

0尊。

不是看不起，
是完全沒在尊重我的意志力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「0尊」就是「零尊重」，2026 年輕人遇到別人言行白目時的標準回嗆。比「過分」更短更有梗，朋友酸你、惡作劇你的時候甩一句最對味
variations: 回應版：「他在群組公開我的舊照，0尊，我要退群以示抗議。」
自嘲版：「我對我的鬧鐘 0尊，按掉五次還怪它不夠大聲。」

en.content:
I said, seriously, that I'm on a diet.
He quietly slid the last fried chicken
right in front of me.

Zero respect.

Not that he looks down on me —
he just has no respect for my willpower whatsoever.
en.editorNote: 'Zero respect' (0尊) is 2026 Taiwan youth's go-to clapback when someone says or does something tactless. Snappier than 'rude' — drop it when a friend roasts you or pranks you
variations: Reply version: 'He posted my old photo in the group chat. Zero respect. I'm leaving the chat in protest.'
Self-roast: 'I have zero respect for my own alarm — snoozed it five times and still blamed it for being too quiet.'

items.sourceNote: 「哭哭馬」2026 馬年諧音文創梗；委屈臉玩偶，命中現代人喪萌心理

zh-tw.content:
週一早上的我
是一隻哭哭馬。

眼睛濕濕的，
嘴角垮垮的，
但還是乖乖站起來吃草——

啊不是，是去上班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「哭哭馬」是 2026 馬年爆紅的喪萌文創梗，委屈臉的馬精準命中現代人「想哭但還是要正常生活」的心情。配在小事委屈或週一厭世的場景最有共感
variations: 情緒版：「他已讀我一整天，我變成一隻哭哭馬，但晚餐還是有乖乖吃。」
鼓勵版：「沒關係，當一陣子哭哭馬也沒關係，哭完還是會跑得很快。」

en.content:
Monday-morning me
is a little crying horse.

Eyes all watery,
mouth drooping,
but still standing up to eat grass —

I mean, to go to work.
en.editorNote: 'Crying horse' (哭哭馬) is a 2026 Year-of-the-Horse 'sad-cute' merch meme — a teary-faced horse plushie that nails the 'want to cry but still living a normal life' mood. Perfect for small disappointments or Monday dread
variations: Mood version: 'He left me on read all day. I turned into a little crying horse, but I still ate dinner like a good kid.'
Encouragement: 'It's okay to be a crying horse for a while. Once the tears dry, you still gallop fast.'

items.sourceNote: 「你確定欸」=「你要確定欸」簡寫；2026 對行為傻眼、不甘置信的調侃

zh-tw.content:
他說他要靠買樂透
達成財務自由。

你確定欸。

你連發票都對不到末三碼，
還想中頭獎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「你確定欸」是「你要確定欸」的簡寫，2026 對別人天兵發言或迷之自信感到傻眼時的調侃。語氣半吐槽半關心，朋友講出離譜計畫時甩一句剛剛好
variations: 戀愛版：「他說他三天不回訊息是怕太黏，你確定欸，我看是怕我太閒。」
自嘲版：「我說我今天一定早睡，朋友直接回我『你確定欸』。」

en.content:
He says he's going to hit
financial freedom by buying lottery tickets.

You sure about that?

You can't even match the last three digits
on a receipt, and you want the jackpot?
en.editorNote: 'You sure about that?' (你確定欸, short for 'you'd better be sure') is 2026 Taiwan's go-to ribbing line when someone makes a wild claim or shows mysterious overconfidence. Half roast, half concern — perfect when a friend announces a ridiculous plan
variations: Dating version: 'He says ghosting me for three days is to avoid being clingy. You sure about that? Looks more like avoiding me being free.'
Self-roast: 'I said I'd definitely go to bed early tonight. My friend just replied: you sure about that?'

items.sourceNote: 「馬倒成功」2026 馬年諧音梗；改「馬到成功」為自嘲喪志版

zh-tw.content:
新年立志要存錢，
結果一月就買了三隻哭哭馬玩偶。

馬倒成功。

本來要馬到成功，
結果馬還沒跑就先倒了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「馬倒成功」是 2026 馬年把祝賀語「馬到成功」改成自嘲的諧音梗，形容計畫直接翻車。用來自我吐槽目標破功時最有節奏，過年到年中都能用
variations: 減肥版：「我的減肥計畫馬倒成功，體重沒倒，倒的是我的意志力。」
鼓勵版：「沒關係，馬倒了再扶起來就好，今年還很長。」

en.content:
New Year's resolution: save money.
Result: bought three crying-horse plushies in January.

Horse-down success.

Supposed to be 'gallop to success,'
but the horse fell over before it even ran.
en.editorNote: 'Horse-down success' (馬倒成功) is a 2026 Year-of-the-Horse pun that flips the blessing 'gallop to success' (馬到成功) into self-mockery — for when a plan crashes immediately. Great for roasting your own busted goals, works from Lunar New Year through mid-year
variations: Diet version: 'My diet plan is a horse-down success. The weight didn't drop, but my willpower sure did.'
Encouragement: 'It's fine — if the horse falls, just help it back up. The year's still long.'

items.sourceNote: 「下頭」2026 Threads 兩性版高頻詞；形容言行讓人瞬間倒胃口、好感扣分

zh-tw.content:
他第一句問：「在幹嘛？」
第二句問：「睡了沒？」
第三句傳：「我房間有冷氣喔。」

下頭。

好感本來有八十分，
三句話扣到剩個位數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「下頭」是 2026 Threads 兩性版高頻詞，形容對方一句話或一個行為讓你瞬間倒胃口、好感狂掉。比「噁心」含蓄，比「不行」精準，曖昧期踩雷的時候甩這兩個字最到位
variations: 自嘲版：「我今天傳了三個語音訊息，朋友說我有點下頭，我自己回聽也覺得下頭。」
反向版：「他默默幫我擋掉一個爛邀約，一點都不下頭，反而加分。」

en.content:
First text: 'whatcha doing?'
Second text: 'you asleep yet?'
Third text: 'btw my room has AC.'

Instant ick.

Started at 80 points of interest,
three lines dragged it down to single digits.
en.editorNote: 'Instant ick' translates 下頭 — 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite relationship-rant word for when someone's line or move makes your interest nosedive. Softer than 'gross,' sharper than 'no' — pull it out the moment a flirt steps on a landmine
variations: Self-roast: 'I sent three voice memos today. My friend said it was a little ick. I replayed them and agreed.'
Reverse: 'He quietly shut down a bad invite for me. Zero ick — actually earned points.'

items.sourceNote: 「章魚哥」2026 Threads 上班族厭世代名詞；以海綿寶寶角色形容對工作徹底無感

zh-tw.content:
九點打卡，
表情管理失敗，
同事問早安我只回一個「嗯」。

章魚哥上線。

不是不想笑，
是這份工作
把我的嘴角調成飛航模式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「章魚哥」借海綿寶寶裡那位永遠厭世的鄰居，形容對工作徹底無感、面無表情的上班族。2026 Threads 上班族厭世迷因頂流，週一發限動配這句，同事秒懂
variations: 加班版：「老闆說再撐一下就好，我章魚哥地點點頭，心裡已經離職三次。」
自嘲版：「同事說我今天特別章魚哥，我說不是今天，是這輩子。」

en.content:
9am clock-in,
facial management failed,
coworker says good morning and I just grunt 'mm.'

Squidward mode: on.

It's not that I won't smile —
this job switched
my mouth corners to airplane mode.
en.editorNote: 'Squidward mode' (章魚哥) borrows SpongeBob's permanently-jaded neighbor to describe an office worker who's gone totally numb and expressionless. The reigning 2026 Taiwan Threads burnout meme — post it on a Monday story and every coworker gets it
variations: Overtime version: 'Boss said just hang in there a bit longer. I nodded all Squidward while quitting in my head for the third time.'
Self-roast: 'A coworker said I'm extra Squidward today. I said not today — my whole life.'

items.sourceNote: 「高麗菜那桌」2024 延燒 2026 的 Threads 老梗；形容狀況外、被晾在一旁的局外人

zh-tw.content:
全公司在群組搶著報名尾牙，
只有我訊息沉到底。

我是高麗菜那桌。

別人在熱炒區乾杯，
我一個人配著小菜，
安靜得像背景音。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「高麗菜那桌」形容在熱鬧場合被晾在角落、完全狀況外的局外人。2024 紅起來 2026 還在用，邊緣感拉滿。聚會被冷落或群組插不上話的時候，自嘲一句最有共感
variations: 工作版：「專案功勞大家分，鍋我自己扛，標準高麗菜那桌待遇。」
社交版：「朋友揪團我每次最後才被想到，我就是那盤沒人夾的高麗菜。」

en.content:
The whole company's racing to RSVP for the year-end party,
and my message just sank to the bottom.

I'm the cabbage table.

Everyone's clinking glasses in the main section,
while I sit alone with a side dish,
quiet as background noise.
en.editorNote: 'The cabbage table' (高麗菜那桌) describes the out-of-the-loop outsider parked in the corner while everyone else has fun. A 2024 hit still running in 2026 — maximum wallflower energy. Use it to self-deprecate when you get left out of a gathering or can't break into the group chat
variations: Work version: 'Credit gets split across the team, blame lands on me alone — classic cabbage-table treatment.'
Social version: 'Friends plan outings and I'm always the afterthought. I'm the cabbage nobody reaches for.'

items.sourceNote: 「下船」2026 Threads 兩性版用語；源自「上船／下船」比喻，指對某人徹底失去興趣抽身

zh-tw.content:
本來都已經上船了，
結果他一句「我前任也這樣」，

我直接下船。

船沒翻，
是我自己跳的，
而且游得超快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「下船」是 2026 Threads 兩性版用語，源自「上船／下船」比喻，指對某人從有興趣到徹底失望、果斷抽身。比「分手」適用更早的階段，曖昧踩雷想喊停時最好用
variations: 預警版：「他開始秒分享前任故事，我已經在找救生艇了，準備下船。」
果斷版：「發現他已讀亂回是常態，這船我不上了，岸上風景比較好。」

en.content:
I was already on the boat,
then he goes 'my ex did that too,'

and I jumped ship right there.

The boat didn't capsize —
I dove off myself,
and swam away fast.
en.editorNote: 'Jumping ship' (下船) is 2026 Taiwan Threads relationship slang from the 'board the boat / leave the boat' metaphor — going from interested to done and bailing decisively. Fits an earlier stage than 'breaking up,' perfect for calling it off when a flirt hits a dealbreaker
variations: Early-warning: 'He's already speed-sharing ex stories. I'm eyeing the lifeboat, getting ready to jump ship.'
Decisive: 'Realized read-and-mis-reply is his default. Not boarding this one — the view from shore is better.'

items.sourceNote: 「留友看」=「留給朋友看」縮寫，2026 Threads 爆紅；靠演算法把貼文推進好友動態

zh-tw.content:
看到超好笑的貼文，
第一個反應不是按讚，

是在底下打三個字：

「留友看」。

不解釋、不 tag，
反正演算法會幫我把你推到對的人面前。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「留友看」是「留給朋友看」的縮寫，2026 Threads 暗號式轉發。看到好笑或知識型貼文時直接留這三個字，演算法會把它推進朋友的動態。想分享又懶得私訊截圖時最好用
variations: 知識版：「這個冷知識太實用，留友看，等等我朋友一定問我怎麼知道的。」
偷渡版：「留友看，其實是留給某個你，希望你滑到。」

en.content:
See a hilarious post,
and my first move isn't to like it —

it's to drop three words in the comments:

'saving this for a friend.'

No explanation, no tag.
The algorithm will float it to the right person for me.
en.editorNote: 'Liu-you-kan' (留友看) is short for 'leaving this for a friend to see' — a 2026 Threads code-phrase for stealth sharing. Comment it under a funny or useful post and the algorithm bumps it into your friends' feeds. Perfect when you want to share but can't be bothered to DM a screenshot
variations: Knowledge version: 'This trivia is too useful, saving it for a friend — they'll definitely ask how I knew.'
Sneaky version: 'Saving this for a friend — okay, for one specific you. Hope you scroll past it.'

items.sourceNote: 「啥款」台語問候語＝「怎樣／如何」，因國片《當男人戀愛時》邱澤口頭禪爆紅

zh-tw.content:
新髮型剪好，
走出店門傳給你一張自拍，

配一句：「啥款？」

你只要回我一個讚，
我今天就能橫著走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「啥款」是台語「怎樣、覺得如何」，因為《當男人戀愛時》邱澤的口頭禪在 2026 重新爆紅。分享新東西想討個評價、又帶點台味痞痞的自信時最對味
variations: 嗆聲版：「我這把手氣，啥款？不服來戰。」
撒嬌版：「今天為你打扮的，啥款？有沒有心動一下下。」

en.content:
Fresh haircut done,
I step out of the salon and send you a selfie

with one line: 'how's it look?'

Just hit me back with one thumbs-up
and I'll strut around all day.
en.editorNote: 'Sann-khuan' (啥款) is Taiwanese Hokkien for 'how about it / what do you think,' revived in 2026 as actor Eddie Peng's catchphrase from the film 'Man in Love.' Use it to fish for a reaction with a bit of cocky local swagger
variations: Trash-talk version: 'Check out this hand I'm holding — how's it look? Come at me.'
Flirty version: 'Dressed up for you today — how's it look? Feel a little something?'

items.sourceNote: 「歸剛欸」台語諧音，嫌人整天沒事找事；因水母斷頭影片「永和檳榔王」配音爆紅

zh-tw.content:
群組沒事被 @ 三次，
會議開到第四個小時還在繞，

我內心只有三個字：

「歸剛欸。」

沒結論、沒進度，
就只是大家輪流講廢話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「歸剛欸」是台語諧音，意思是嫌一個人整天沒事找事、瞎忙瞎搞。因為水母斷頭影片的魔性配音在 2026 爆紅。被無意義的事煩到、想翻白眼又不想直接開罵時，丟這句最解氣
variations: 工作版：「需求改第五版又改回第一版，歸剛欸，是在哈囉。」
日常版：「半夜傳長文吵架的人，歸剛欸，不睡是不是。」

en.content:
Tagged three times in the group chat for nothing,
meeting hits hour four still going in circles,

and all I can think is three words:

'what's even the point.'

No conclusion, no progress,
just everyone taking turns talking nonsense.
en.editorNote: 'Kui-kang-eh' (歸剛欸) is a Taiwanese phrase for griping that someone's stirring up pointless trouble or busywork — blown up in 2026 by a viral cursed jellyfish dub. Use it to vent when meaningless drama or busy-nothing has worn you out and you want to roll your eyes without fully cussing someone out
variations: Work version: 'Specs changed to version five then back to version one — what's even the point, are you kidding me.'
Daily version: 'People who send paragraph-long fight texts at midnight — what's the point, do you not sleep.'

items.sourceNote: 「初級大人／高級大人」2026 初爆紅；成年人回家秒變小孩，凡事要找能拍板的長輩

zh-tw.content:
在公司我管三個專案、簽十萬的單，

回到家，
冰箱要不要丟過期的優格，
我得先問我媽。

在外面是大人，
回家只是個初級大人，
凡事都要找高級大人核准。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「初級大人」指法律上成年、回家卻秒變小孩什麼都要問長輩；「高級大人」就是能拍板的爸媽。2026 初爆紅，過年家族聚會超有共鳴。想自嘲在家毫無決策權時最好用
variations: 過年版：「紅包包多少要問高級大人，水果買哪攤也要問，我這個初級大人只負責提袋子。」
獨立版：「在外租屋三年，回家連洗碗順序都會被糾正，初級大人沒有尊嚴。」

en.content:
At the office I run three projects and sign off six-figure deals.

Back home,
before I toss the expired yogurt from the fridge
I have to ask my mom first.

Out there I'm an adult,
at home I'm just a junior adult —
everything needs sign-off from a senior adult.
en.editorNote: 'Junior adult' (初級大人) means you're legally grown but revert to a kid the moment you're home, needing approval for everything; 'senior adult' (高級大人) is the parent who actually calls the shots. A viral 2026 hit that lands hard around family gatherings. Use it to self-deprecate about having zero decision power at home
variations: Holiday version: 'How much to put in the red envelope? Ask the senior adult. Which fruit stand to buy from? Ask too. As a junior adult I just carry the bags.'
Independence version: 'Rented my own place for three years, come home and get corrected on the order I wash dishes — junior adults have no dignity.'

items.sourceNote: 「與眾分」=「與眾人分享」縮寫，2026 Threads 社群用語；公開向所有人安麗好物

zh-tw.content:
這家宵夜太神，
我不能自己一個人知道。

「與眾分。」

截圖、定位、營業時間全附上，
各位脆友，
我請你們吃流量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「與眾分」是「與眾人分享」的縮寫，2026 Threads 社群用語，跟只給朋友看的「留友看」相反——這是大方公開安麗，要讓所有人都看到。發現好物、好店想昭告天下時最對味
variations: 好雷版：「這部劇我熬夜追完，與眾分，沒看的快去，看完回來謝我。」
正能量版：「今天被陌生人幫了一把，與眾分，世界還是有好人的。」

en.content:
This late-night spot is too good,
I can't be the only one who knows.

'Sharing with everyone.'

Screenshot, location, hours all attached.
Fellow Threads folks,
this round of clout is on me.
en.editorNote: 'Yu-zhong-fen' (與眾分) is short for 'sharing with everyone' — a 2026 Threads phrase that's the opposite of the friends-only 'liu-you-kan.' This one is a loud public recommendation meant for the whole feed. Use it when you find a gem and want to broadcast it to the world
variations: Rave-review version: 'Binged this whole show overnight — sharing with everyone, go watch it and come back to thank me.'
Wholesome version: 'A stranger helped me out today — sharing with everyone, there are still good people out there.'

items.sourceNote: 「含機量」2026 爆紅詞，形容場合/專案/公司裡 AI 參與程度極高

zh-tw.content:
新同事自我介紹完，
我才發現他是 AI。

會議室裡四個人，
兩個是機器人、
一個全程沒講話、
剩我自己。

這間公司的含機量，
已經爆表了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「含機量」是「含 AI 機器人的比例」，2026 隨 AI 全面普及爆紅，用來形容某個場合、專案或公司裡 AI 參與的程度有多高。吐槽工作被機器人包圍、或自嘲方案根本全靠 AI 生出來時最好用
variations: 報告版：「這份提案含機量百分之九十，我只負責改錯字跟按送出。」
聚會版：「同學會十個人到場，含機量零，難得一場純人類聚會，珍惜。」

en.content:
The new hire finished introducing themselves
before I realized they were an AI.

Four people in the meeting room:
two are bots,
one never said a word,
and then there's me.

This company's bot-content level
has officially maxed out.
en.editorNote: 'Bot-content level' (含機量) literally means 'how much AI is in the mix' — a 2026 hit as AI went mainstream, used to describe how heavily a setting, project, or whole company runs on AI. Use it to roast being surrounded by bots at work, or to admit your deliverable was basically AI-generated
variations: Deliverable version: 'This proposal's bot-content is ninety percent — I just fixed the typos and hit send.'
Gathering version: 'Ten people showed up to the reunion, zero bot-content — a rare all-human hangout, treasure it.'

items.sourceNote: 「歸剛欸」台語諧音，嫌人整天沒事找事；水母配音影片帶紅

zh-tw.content:
群組半夜三點突然冒出一句：
「我們來辦尾牙吧。」

現在六月。

歸剛欸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「歸剛欸」是台語諧音，意思接近「整天就只會搞這些沒用的」，用來吐槽一個人沒事找事、亂出主意。語氣是無奈又好笑，不是真的生氣，朋友提出離譜計畫時回這三個字最到位
variations: 加班版：「下班前五分鐘主管說『這個再改一版』，歸剛欸，是要我睡公司是不是。」
戀愛版：「他半夜傳『在嗎』然後就消失，歸剛欸，不睡覺是要嚇唬誰。」

en.content:
At 3am the group chat suddenly drops:
'Let's plan the year-end party.'

It's June.

Quit messing around, man.
en.editorNote: 'Gui-gang-eh' (歸剛欸) is a Taiwanese phrase that roughly means 'you're forever stirring up pointless trouble' — used to roast someone for inventing busywork or floating ridiculous ideas. The tone is exasperated-but-amused, not actually angry. Drop it when a friend pitches something absurd
variations: Overtime version: 'Five minutes before clock-out the boss says "do one more revision" — quit messing around, are you trying to make me live here?'
Dating version: 'He texts "you up?" at midnight then vanishes — quit messing around, who are you trying to spook?'

items.sourceNote: 「蛋雕」台語「丟掉」諧音，表示反感或捨棄；2026 諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
前任傳訊息說想復合。

我看著螢幕，
手指一滑——

蛋雕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「蛋雕」是台語「丟掉」（tàn-tiāu）的諧音，寫出來莫名可愛，但意思就是「丟掉、不要了、滾」。用在想把某個人事物乾脆俐落地處理掉時，比直接說「丟掉」多了一層搞笑緩衝
variations: 整理版：「衣櫃裡三年沒穿的都蛋雕，留著只是養灰塵。」
情緒版：「今天的爛心情，下班打卡那一刻就地蛋雕，不帶回家。」

en.content:
My ex texts saying they want to get back together.

I stare at the screen,
one swipe of the finger —

tossed.
en.editorNote: 'Dan-diao' (蛋雕) is a homophone for the Taiwanese word for 'throw away' (tàn-tiāu) — it looks oddly cute written out but just means 'trash it, done, get lost.' Use it when you want to dispose of a person or thing cleanly, with a comedic softener over the bluntness
variations: Decluttering version: 'Anything in the closet I haven't worn in three years gets tossed — keeping it just feeds dust.'
Mood version: 'Today's bad mood gets tossed right at clock-out — not bringing it home.'

items.sourceNote: 「愛你老己」方言版「愛你自己」，主打不內耗、好好犒賞自己

zh-tw.content:
今天不想當好人，
不想懂事，
不想替別人著想。

買一杯最貴的，
追一集捨不得追完的，
早點睡。

不內耗，
記得愛你老己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「愛你老己」是方言腔的「愛你自己」，唸起來俏皮又有點台。重點不是叫你大道理式地愛自己，而是別過度內耗，下班後買點好吃的、看場好片，實際犒賞自己一下。發給快被生活磨平的朋友剛剛好
variations: 週五版：「這週撐住了就是贏，週末什麼都別想，愛你老己。」
提醒版：「先把自己餵飽、睡飽，再來談那些有的沒的，愛你老己最大。」

en.content:
Today I don't want to be the nice one,
don't want to be sensible,
don't want to think of everyone else first.

Buy the most expensive drink,
watch the episode you've been saving,
go to bed early.

Stop overthinking —
remember to love ya self.
en.editorNote: 'Ai-ni-lao-ji' (愛你老己) is a playful dialect spin on 'love yourself.' The point isn't a lofty self-love lecture — it's: stop grinding yourself down with overthinking, and actually treat yourself after work with good food or a good show. Perfect to send a friend who's getting worn flat by life
variations: Friday version: 'Surviving this week is already a win. Don't think about anything this weekend — love ya self.'
Reminder version: 'Feed yourself, sleep enough first, then we can talk about all the other noise. Loving ya self comes first.'

items.sourceNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」=「真的假的」諧音，唸時嘴巴嘟起來，裝可愛用法

zh-tw.content:
「我這個月薪水加倍。」

尊嘟假嘟？

「假的。」

……我就知道，
嘴巴都嘟起來了還敢騙我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」是「真的假的」的諧音，唸的時候嘴巴會不自覺嘟起來，是 2026 裝可愛系的招牌反應詞。聽到爆炸八卦、難以置信的消息時丟這四個字，比「真假」可愛十倍
variations: 八卦版：「他們兩個在一起了？尊嘟假嘟，我手機都拿不穩了。」
自嘲版：「老闆說週末不用加班，尊嘟假嘟，我已經做好被騙的心理準備。」

en.content:
'My paycheck doubled this month.'

For realsies?

'Just kidding.'

…I knew it.
You pout your lips and still dare to lie to me.
en.editorNote: 'Zun-du-jia-du' (尊嘟假嘟) is a cutesy homophone for 'is that real or fake?' — saying it makes your lips pout, which is the whole point. It's 2026's signature adorable reaction word. Drop it when you hear wild gossip or unbelievable news; ten times cuter than a flat 'no way'
variations: Gossip version: 'Those two are dating now? For realsies? I can barely hold my phone.'
Self-deprecating version: 'Boss says no overtime this weekend — for realsies? I'm already braced to get played.'

items.sourceNote: 「張中和」=「尊重我」諧音，要求對方安靜或給予基本尊重；2026 諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
同事邊吃便當邊講我壞話，
還大聲到整層樓都聽得到。

我轉過頭，
淡淡丟一句：

「麻煩請張中和一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「張中和」是「尊重我」的諧音梗，把一句嗆人的話包裝成人名，唸出來反而有種莫名的優雅。當有人講話太白目、需要對方收斂或安靜時用，殺傷力剛好，又不會撕破臉
variations: 開會版：「我話還沒講完就被打斷三次，可以張中和一下嗎？」
群組版：「貼圖洗版可以，但凌晨三點請大家互相張中和。」

en.content:
A coworker is trash-talking me while eating lunch,
loud enough for the whole floor to hear.

I turn my head
and say, flat and calm:

'Could you kindly show a little Reese Pecked?'
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'Zhang Zhong-he' (張中和) is a homophone for 'respect me,' disguised as a person's name — saying it out loud sounds oddly elegant. Use it when someone's being rude and you want them to dial it back or pipe down: enough sting to land, not enough to start a war
variations: Meeting version: 'I got cut off three times before finishing — can we get a little respect here?'
Group chat version: 'Spam all the stickers you want, but at 3 a.m. let's all respect each other.'

items.sourceNote: 「活人感」形容真實自然、沒被社群軟體格式化的人；2026 Z世代用語

zh-tw.content:
她的限動沒有濾鏡，
字醜醜的，
還會把吃到一半的麵拍進去。

但我每次都先點開她的，
因為那種活人感，
比一百張精修照片都珍貴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「活人感」是 2026 的高級讚美，形容一個人真實、自然、沒被社群軟體格式化成完美樣板。想稱讚朋友不做作、很真誠時用，比說「你好真實」更到位。發給那個總是很做自己的朋友剛剛好
variations: 交友版：「他聊天會打錯字、會冷場、會突然講幹話，超有活人感，我喜歡。」
自嘲版：「今天素顏出門被同事說很有活人感，我不知道該開心還是該補妝。」

en.content:
Her stories have no filter,
the handwriting's a little ugly,
and she'll post a bowl of noodles she's halfway through eating.

But hers is the first one I open every time,
because that real-person energy
beats a hundred touched-up photos.
en.editorNote: 'Real-person energy' (活人感) is a high compliment in 2026: it means someone feels genuine and natural, not flattened into a flawless social-media template. Use it to praise a friend for being unpolished and sincere — lands better than just saying 'you're so real.' Perfect for the friend who's always unapologetically themselves
variations: Dating version: 'He makes typos, lets the chat go quiet, drops random nonsense — so much real-person energy, I'm into it.'
Self-deprecating version: 'Went out bare-faced today and a coworker said I had real-person energy. Not sure whether to feel flattered or reach for concealer.'

items.sourceNote: 「電子布洛芬」=能快速療癒壞心情的搞笑影片或萌寵圖；2026 Z世代用語

zh-tw.content:
加班到頭快炸，
打開手機滑了三隻貓打架的影片。

笑出來那一刻，
世界突然不那麼想罷工了。

電子布洛芬，
吃完繼續活。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「電子布洛芬」形容那些能在網路上快速止痛、緩解焦慮的搞笑影片或萌寵圖——像布洛芬一樣，吞下去就沒那麼痛了。心情爛到谷底、需要朋友丟點開心東西過來時用，討拍又不沉重
variations: 求救版：「今天被慘電，誰有電子布洛芬快丟過來，劑量加大。」
推薦版：「失戀的話這個頻道是特效電子布洛芬，連嗑三集保證沒事。」

en.content:
Overtime until my head's about to explode,
so I open my phone and scroll three clips of cats fighting.

The second I laugh,
the world suddenly feels less like going on strike.

Digital ibuprofen —
take it, then carry on living.
en.editorNote: 'Digital ibuprofen' (電子布洛芬) is any funny video or cute-animal clip that quickly numbs a bad mood and eases anxiety online — like ibuprofen, swallow it and it hurts less. Use it when you're at rock bottom and need a friend to send something cheering; asks for comfort without being heavy
variations: SOS version: 'Got destroyed today — someone hit me with digital ibuprofen, fast, double the dose.'
Recommendation version: 'If you're heartbroken, this channel is prescription-strength digital ibuprofen — binge three episodes and you'll be fine.'

items.sourceNote: 「0尊」=零尊重，回應對方說話不得體；2026 Threads 用語

zh-tw.content:
「你最近是不是又胖了？」

「……0尊。」

「我是關心你欸。」

「關心的開場白沒有人在問體重的，謝謝，續0尊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「0尊」就是「零尊重」，當對方講話白目、不得體時，直接回兩個字就能表達「你這樣真的很沒禮貌」。語氣半開玩笑半認真，吐槽朋友的失言剛剛好，不會太嚴肅
variations: 聚餐版：「還沒問我想吃什麼就先幫全桌點好——0尊，下次先問。」
工作版：「把我的功勞講成是他想的，當場 0 尊，我都看在眼裡。」

en.content:
'Have you, like, gained weight again lately?'

'…Zero respect.'

'I'm just being caring.'

'Nobody opens a caring sentence by asking about my weight, thanks. Still zero respect.'
en.editorNote: 'Zero respect' (0尊, short for '0 尊重') is the two-word reply you fire back when someone says something tactless or out of line — it means 'that was genuinely rude.' Half-joking, half-serious, perfect for calling out a friend's verbal misstep without making it a whole thing
variations: Group dinner version: 'You ordered for the whole table before even asking what I wanted — zero respect, ask first next time.'
Work version: 'He pitched my idea as his own in the meeting. Zero respect, on the spot. I saw everything.'

items.sourceNote: 「觸」=英文 true 諧音，表認同；程度遞增 觸＜觸爛＜觸爆；2026 流行語

zh-tw.content:
「上班最累的不是工作，是裝沒事。」

「觸爛。」

「裝到下班，回家直接斷電。」

「觸爆，這就是我本人。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「觸」是英文 true 的諧音，表示「真的、我認同」。程度往上加：觸＜觸爛＜觸爆。看到超有共鳴的貼文想附和，比打「+1」有梗多了
variations: 讚嘆版：「他這段話我聽完直接觸爆，講到心坎裡。」
簡短版：「對欸觸爛，我完全懂這種感覺。」

en.content:
'The most exhausting part of work isn't the work — it's pretending you're fine.'

'So true.'

'Pretend till clock-out, then power off the second I'm home.'

'Truer than true. That's literally me.'
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'chu' (觸) is a homophone for English 'true,' meaning 'real, I agree.' It stacks for intensity: chu < chu-lan < chu-bao (so true < truer < truest). Use it to cosign a post you deeply relate to — way more fun than typing '+1'
variations: Praise version: 'That whole speech of his? Truest thing I've heard. Hit me right in the chest.'
Short version: 'Right, so true — I know exactly that feeling.'

items.sourceNote: 「巨好吃」=非常非常好吃，巨為誇飾修飾詞；2026 Threads 用語

zh-tw.content:
排隊一個半小時，
咬下第一口的瞬間——

巨好吃。

不是普通好吃，
是會原諒剛剛那一個半小時的那種好吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「巨」當程度副詞用，比「超」「爆」更誇張。形容食物好吃到誇張時用，也可以延伸成「巨可愛」「巨想睡」，萬用加強語氣
variations: 推坑版：「這家滷肉飯巨好吃，我願意為它多走兩條街。」
延伸版：「今天的你巨可愛，這句你收下不用找。」

en.content:
Lined up for an hour and a half,
and the moment I took the first bite —

mega good.

Not regular-good,
the kind of good that forgives that whole hour and a half.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'ju' (巨, literally 'giant') is used as an intensity adverb, even bigger than 'super' or 'insanely.' Use it for food that's outrageously good — and it extends to 'mega cute,' 'mega sleepy,' an all-purpose dial-up
variations: Recommendation version: 'This place's braised pork rice is mega good — I'd walk two extra blocks for it.'
Extended version: 'You're mega cute today. Keep the change.'

items.sourceNote: 「初級大人/高級大人」2026 開年聲量破 3.9 萬筆；指長大但還不太會當大人 vs 真正成熟

zh-tw.content:
高級大人：報稅、換燈泡、約健檢，樣樣自己來。

初級大人：發現燈泡壞了，先在原地站三秒，然後打電話問媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「初級大人」指年齡到了、但生活技能還在練的那種大人，自嘲又精準。揪朋友互相取暖、承認自己還沒長好的時候用，超有共鳴
variations: 認證版：「銀行的事我一個都不會，純初級大人，求高級大人帶。」
升級版：「今天自己看懂了租屋合約，感覺往高級大人邁進一步。」

en.content:
Advanced adult: files their taxes, changes the lightbulb, books the health check — all solo.

Junior adult: notices the bulb's dead, stands there for three seconds, then calls their mom.
en.editorNote: 'Junior adult' (初級大人) is someone who's technically grown but still leveling up their life skills — self-deprecating and painfully accurate. Use it to commiserate with friends and admit you're not fully adulting yet
variations: Self-cert version: 'I can't do a single bank thing — pure junior adult here, an advanced adult please carry me.'
Level-up version: 'Read my whole lease myself today. Feel one step closer to advanced adult.'

items.sourceNote: 「M3」源自 MOBA 技能用法，引申為超有吸引力、把人定住；2026 年輕人用語

zh-tw.content:
他一回頭對鏡頭笑，
我整個人 M3，
手機差點掉進泡麵裡。

那種被定在原地、
三秒動不了的感覺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「M3」借自 MOBA 遊戲技能編號，引申成「被電到、被定住」——指某人魅力強到讓你當場僵在原地。追星、看到喜歡的人或太帥的瞬間都能用
variations: 追星版：「他舞台一個眼神我直接 M3，原地宕機三秒。」
日常版：「同事今天穿西裝來，全辦公室被 M3，沒人敢出聲。」

en.content:
He turned and smiled at the camera,
and I got full-on stunned —
nearly dropped my phone into my instant noodles.

That feeling of being pinned in place,
frozen for three whole seconds.
