en.editorNote: 'M3' is borrowed from a MOBA game skill slot, extended to mean 'hardstunned' — someone's charm is so strong it freezes you on the spot. Use it for stan moments, a crush, or any too-handsome split second
variations: Stan version: 'One glance from him on stage and I was hardstunned — buffered in place for three seconds.'
Everyday version: 'A coworker showed up in a suit today and the whole office got stunned — nobody dared speak.'

items.sourceNote: 「YCE」=你要確定欸縮寫，半開玩笑表懷疑；2026 流行語

zh-tw.content:
「我這次一定減肥成功。」

「YCE？」

「真的，我已經把零食全丟了。」

「丟到你嘴巴裡那種丟嗎，YCE。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「YCE」是「你要確定欸」的縮寫，用來半開玩笑地表達懷疑。朋友又在立 flag、講大話時丟這三個字，吐槽得剛剛好又不會撕破臉
variations: 立 flag 版：「你說十點睡？YCE，昨天也是這樣講的。」
傻眼版：「他說這次絕對準時——全場 YCE，沒人信。」

en.content:
'This time I'm definitely gonna lose the weight.'

'You sure about that?'

'For real, I already threw out all my snacks.'

'Threw them straight into your mouth? You sure about that.'
en.editorNote: 'YCE' stands for the Mandarin '你要確定欸' (you sure about that?), a half-joking way to flag your doubt. Drop it when a friend's making big claims or planting a flag — it ribs them just enough without starting a fight
variations: Flag-planting version: 'You said bed by ten? You sure about that — said the same thing yesterday.'
Disbelief version: 'He swore he'd be on time this once — whole room went you sure about that, nobody bought it.'

items.sourceNote: 「世一」=世界第一縮寫，2026 用來吹捧偶像、店家、朋友；Threads 高頻讚美詞

zh-tw.content:
別家滷肉飯我都吃過了，

這間，世一。

沒有第二，
冠軍直接從缺，
因為位子被它坐滿了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「世一」是「世界第一」的縮寫，2026 拿來吹捧偶像、愛店或朋友超浮誇又可愛。想大力誇一個人或一家店，丟兩個字比落落長一串更有力
variations: 告白版：「認識這麼多人，你在我心裡世一，沒有要排名了，就你。」
吐槽版：「他遲到的功力也是世一，沒人贏得了，這點我服。」

en.content:
I've tried every braised-pork rice in town,

and this one — world's number one.

No runner-up.
The title's just vacant,
because this place is sitting in every seat.
en.editorNote: 'World's number one' (世一) is the 2026 shorthand for hyping your idol, favorite shop, or a friend in the most over-the-top, affectionate way. When you want to big someone up, two words hit harder than a paragraph
variations: Confession version: 'Met a lot of people, and in my book you're world's number one. I'm done ranking — it's just you.'
Roast version: 'His talent for being late is also world's number one. Nobody beats him. On that, I bow.'

items.sourceNote: 「顯眼包」2026 流行語，形容自帶社牛屬性、行為誇張惹眼又好笑的人

zh-tw.content:
全班拍畢業照，
後排默默比 YA 的我，

變成班級顯眼包。

別人記不住名字，
但都記得「那個比超大 YA 的」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「顯眼包」是 2026 形容「不怕出糗、誇張又好笑、自帶社牛屬性」的人，惹眼但討喜。誇朋友是團體裡的歡樂擔當、或自嘲愛搶鏡時用最對味
variations: 誇人版：「每次聚餐沒你就少一半笑聲，你就是我們這桌的顯眼包，缺你不可。」
自嘲版：「合照站最邊邊還是被拍到，我大概天生顯眼包,想低調都難。」

en.content:
Whole class lines up for the grad photo.
Me, quietly throwing a peace sign in the back row,

becomes the class scene-stealer.

Nobody remembers my name,
but everyone remembers 'the one doing the giant peace sign.'
en.editorNote: 'Scene-stealer' (顯眼包) is the 2026 word for someone unafraid to look silly — loud, funny, with main-character energy, eye-catching but lovable. Use it to praise the friend who brings the joy, or to self-roast your camera-hogging
variations: Compliment version: 'Every dinner loses half its laughs without you. You're our table's scene-stealer — non-negotiable.'
Self-roast: 'Stood at the very edge of the group photo and still got caught. Guess I'm a born scene-stealer; low-key isn't an option.'

items.sourceNote: 「搭子」2026 高頻關係詞，指只一起做某件事的淺社交夥伴，如飯搭子、旅遊搭子

zh-tw.content:
我們不是很熟，

但你是我的飯搭子。

不用聊心事，
只要中午十二點
準時出現在便當店門口，
這份默契就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「搭子」是 2026 形容「只一起做某件事的淺社交夥伴」，飯搭子、健身搭子、追劇搭子都算。剛好夠近又不用掏心掏肺，傳給那位專屬某件事的夥伴最對味
variations: 揪人版：「下週馬拉松缺一個運動搭子，全程不聊天只一起喘，有沒有人。」
升級版：「本來只是咖啡搭子，聊著聊著變成什麼都能講的那種,搭子升級了。」

en.content:
We're not really close,

but you're my meal buddy.

No need to trade secrets —
just show up at the lunch spot
at twelve sharp,
and that's all the chemistry we need.
en.editorNote: 'Buddy' (搭子) is the 2026 word for a low-stakes partner for one specific thing — meal buddy, gym buddy, binge-watch buddy. Close enough to count, not close enough to spill your heart. Send it to your one-activity sidekick
variations: Recruiting version: 'Need a workout buddy for next week's marathon — no chatting, just panting together the whole way. Anyone?'
Upgrade version: 'Started as just a coffee buddy, then we kept talking and now I can tell you anything. Buddy: upgraded.'

items.sourceNote: 「陳皮糖」2026 爆紅梗，形容追劇追到上頭、嘴裡像含著一顆甜到化不開的糖

zh-tw.content:
你今天吃陳皮糖了嗎？

我吃了，
而且整顆含在心口，
甜到捨不得吞。

劇還沒播完，
我已經開始想念。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「陳皮糖」是 2026 追劇圈爆紅的形容詞，指被某對 CP 或某段劇情甜到上頭、像嘴裡含著一顆化不開的糖。跟同好聊到欲罷不能的劇時，這句一出大家秒懂
variations: 安利版：「這部新劇就是一顆超大陳皮糖，含進去保證下不來，慎入。」
心碎版：「陳皮糖吃完了，結局把糖收走了，現在嘴裡只剩空虛。」

en.content:
Did you eat your tangerine candy today?

I did,
and I'm holding the whole piece against my chest,
too sweet to swallow.

The show isn't even over
and I already miss it.
en.editorNote: 'Tangerine candy' (陳皮糖) is a 2026 fandom term for being so swept up by a ship or storyline that it's like a sweet melting on your tongue. Drop it with a fellow fan about a show you can't quit and they'll get it instantly
variations: Rec version: 'This new drama is one giant piece of tangerine candy — once it's in, you're not coming back. Enter at your own risk.'
Heartbreak version: 'Finished the candy, and the finale took the sweetness with it. Now there's just emptiness on my tongue.'

items.sourceNote: 「ㄅ級分」2026 爆紅評分梗，把英文字母評分改成注音，ㄅ＝最高分

zh-tw.content:
這家早餐
蛋餅外酥內嫩、
奶茶比例完美，

我給ㄅ級分。

沒有ㄅ＋，
不然我馬上加上去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「ㄅ級分」是 2026 爆紅的評分梗，把英文字母 A、B、C 換成注音ㄅㄆㄇ，ㄅ就是最高分。誇一家店、一部片、一個人到滿分時，這三個字最潮也最台
variations: 極致版：「他今天的穿搭直接突破ㄅ級分，我要新發明一個ㄅ ＋＋給他。」
吐槽版：「這場會議的廢話濃度也是ㄅ級分，無人能及，恭喜。」

en.content:
This breakfast joint —
egg crepe crisp outside, soft inside,
milk tea ratio perfect,

I'm giving it a bo-grade.

There's no bo-plus,
or I'd slap it on right now.
en.editorNote: 'Bo-grade' (ㄅ級分) is a 2026 rating meme that swaps the A-B-C scale for Taiwan's bopomofo letters — 'bo' (ㄅ) is the top mark. When something or someone scores full marks, these three characters are the most current, most local way to say it
variations: Peak version: 'His outfit today straight-up broke past bo-grade. I need to invent a bo-plus-plus for him.'
Roast version: 'The nonsense density of that meeting was also bo-grade. Unmatched. Congrats.'

items.sourceNote: 「很躁」2026 常用流行語，意指快生氣但還沒到生氣的煩躁感，類似台語「阿雜」

zh-tw.content:
天氣很躁、
捷運很躁、
群組九十九則未讀很躁，

但我最躁的是——
你還沒回我訊息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「很躁」是 2026 常用的情緒詞，指「快生氣但還沒生氣」的煩躁，比「我好煩」更精準。心情卡卡又不想解釋太多時，丟這句剛剛好
variations: 極限版：「我躁到連我自己的呼吸聲都嫌吵。」
撩人版：「全世界都讓我很躁，只有你的訊息能讓我秒消火。」

en.content:
The weather's got me on edge,
the subway's got me on edge,
ninety-nine unread in the group chat — on edge,

but the thing that gets me most?
You still haven't texted back.
en.editorNote: 'On edge' (很躁) is a go-to 2026 mood word for that almost-mad-but-not-quite irritation — sharper than 'I'm so done.' Perfect when you're prickly and don't feel like explaining why
variations: Max version: 'I'm so on edge I'm annoyed at the sound of my own breathing.'
Flirty version: 'The whole world puts me on edge — only your texts switch it off in a second.'

items.sourceNote: 「破防」原為遊戲突破防禦，網路引申為被戳中痛點、心理防線被攻破

zh-tw.content:
我自以為已經練到刀槍不入，

結果你一句「辛苦了」，
直接破防。

防線是紙做的，
你早就知道吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「破防」原本是遊戲術語，指突破對方防禦，現在用來形容「被一句話戳中、瞬間繃不住」。被溫柔或被講中時都適用，傳給懂你的人最有共鳴
variations: 硬撐版：「我沒哭，我只是被冷氣吹到破防而已。」
告白版：「別人怎麼撩我都沒事，只有你能讓我破防。」

en.content:
I thought I'd trained myself bulletproof.

Then you said 'you've worked so hard,'
and my whole guard cracked.

The wall was paper the whole time —
you knew that already, didn't you.
en.editorNote: 'Cracking your guard' (破防) started as gaming slang for breaking an opponent's defense; now it means a single line punches straight through your composure. Works for being touched or being called out — send it to someone who really gets you
variations: Tough-it-out version: 'I'm not crying, the AC just cracked my guard, that's all.'
Confession version: 'Everyone else can flirt with me and I'm fine. Only you can crack my guard.'

items.sourceNote: 「暈爛」是「暈」的加強版，形容對某人事物迷戀到無法自拔

zh-tw.content:
別人只是暈，
我是暈爛。

暈到地基都軟了，
暈到下次見面前
我會先把表情先練好，
免得整張臉藏不住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「暈爛」是「暈」的究極加強版，指迷戀到無可救藥、徹底淪陷。對人對偶像對一部劇都能用，是 2026 戀愛腦的最高級形容詞
variations: 追星版：「他舞台一個轉身，我當場暈爛，散場還沒站起來。」
曖昧版：「我不只暈你，我已經暈爛了，這個鍋你得背。」

en.content:
Other people just swoon.
I'm swooned to a puddle.

Swooned till the foundation went soft,
swooned so hard that before I see you next
I need to rehearse my face —
or it'll give the whole thing away.
en.editorNote: 'Swooned to a puddle' (暈爛) is the maxed-out version of 'swoon' — hopelessly, irreversibly gone. Use it on a person, an idol, or a show; it's 2026's top-tier word for being absolutely down bad
variations: Stan version: 'One spin on stage and I was swooned to a puddle — still couldn't stand up after the show ended.'
Flirty version: 'I'm not just into you, I'm swooned to a puddle. This one's on you.'

items.sourceNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」是「真的假的」的諧音梗，發音時嘴巴會嘟起來，裝可愛說法

zh-tw.content:
「我中了發票兩百萬。」
尊嘟假嘟？

「騙你的。」

那一秒嘟起來的嘴，
現在不知道該怎麼收回去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」是「真的假的」的諧音梗，唸的時候嘴會不自覺嘟起來，自帶裝可愛效果。聽到誇張八卦想回又想賣萌時，這四個字最好用
variations: 震驚版：「尊嘟假嘟？？我嘴已經嘟到收不回來了。」
吐槽版：「又是這種尊嘟假嘟的八卦，我嘴都嘟到痠了。」

en.content:
'I just won two million on a lottery receipt.'
For weal?

'Nah, kidding.'

My lips puckered up for a second there —
and now I don't know how to un-pucker them.
en.editorNote: 'For weal?' renders 尊嘟假嘟, a baby-talk pun on 'for real?' (真的假的) where saying it makes your lips pucker up cute. Pull it out when you hear wild gossip and want to react and look adorable at once
variations: Shook version: 'For weal?? My lips are stuck in pucker mode now, no take-backs.'
Roast version: 'Another one of these for-weal gossip drops — my lips are sore from puckering.'

items.sourceNote: 「普信」是「普通卻自信」的縮寫，形容外貌或能力普通卻過度自信的人

zh-tw.content:
他長得普通、
能力普通、
聊天內容也很普通，

但他的自信
是頂規、是滿配、是頂天的。

這就是傳說中的普信吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「普信」是「普通卻自信」的縮寫，吐槽那種實力跟自信完全不成正比的人。當朋友被某個迷之自信的對象搭訕後，這兩個字就是最精準的事後總結
variations: 自嘲版：「我大概也有點普信，不然今天怎麼敢穿這件出門。」
勸退版：「對普信的人不用生氣，他的世界裡只有他發光。」

en.content:
His looks are average,
his skills are average,
even his chat is painfully average,

but his confidence?
Top-tier, fully loaded, sky-high.

This, my friends, is the legendary mid-but-mighty.
en.editorNote: 'Mid-but-mighty' (普信) is short for 'ordinary yet supremely confident' — the roast for someone whose self-belief wildly outpaces the receipts. The perfect debrief after a friend gets hit on by a mysteriously self-assured stranger
variations: Self-roast version: 'I'm probably a little mid-but-mighty myself — how else did I dare leave the house in this outfit?'
Let-it-go version: 'No point getting mad at the mid-but-mighty. In their world, they're the only one shining.'

items.sourceNote: 「最頂／超頂」2026 Z世代口頭禪，指事物達到極致、令人驚豔

zh-tw.content:
這家牛肉麵不是好吃，
是最頂。

吃完直接想跟老闆鞠躬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「最頂」是 2026 萬用稱讚詞，比「超讚」更有氣勢，意思是天花板等級、無法再高。誇美食、誇穿搭、誇人都能用，語氣帶點誇張很對味
variations: 升級版：「我看過的所有電影，這部最頂，沒有之一。」
反差版：「他平常很低調，一上台表演就最頂。」

en.content:
This beef noodle joint isn't just good.
It's peak.

I finished the bowl and wanted to bow to the chef.
en.editorNote: 'Peak' (最頂) is 2026's go-to top-tier compliment — ceiling-level, can't-go-higher. Works for food, fits, or people, and lands best with a touch of exaggeration
variations: Dialed-up version: 'Of every movie I've ever seen, this one is peak. No competition.'
Contrast version: 'He's low-key off stage, but the second he performs he's straight-up peak.'

items.sourceNote: 「綠茶」流行語：表面溫柔無害、私下心機，2026 Threads 吐槽人際常用

zh-tw.content:
她當著我面誇我可愛，
轉頭跟全辦公室說我心機。

綠茶的茶，
泡得真濃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「綠茶」形容表面柔弱無害、背地裡耍心機的人。吐槽那種當面一套背後一套的對象很精準，講的時候配個無奈表情更傳神
variations: 自保版：「我不是要說誰綠茶，但你們心裡都有數。」
認證版：「能在三句話內把所有人得罪光又裝沒事，這綠茶段位我服。」

en.content:
She called me adorable to my face,
then told the whole office I'm a schemer.

That's some seriously
strong green tea.
en.editorNote: 'Green tea' (綠茶) is the label for someone sweet and harmless on the surface but quietly scheming underneath — perfect for roasting a two-faced acquaintance. Deliver it with a weary look
variations: Cover-your-tracks version: 'I'm not naming any green tea here, but y'all know exactly who.'
Grudging-respect version: 'Offending everyone in three sentences and still playing innocent? That green-tea level deserves applause.'

items.sourceNote: 「哈基米」源自貓咪迷因，2026 變成裝可愛萬用語助詞

zh-tw.content:
老闆：這週末要加班。
我：哈基米。

（翻譯：我已經沒有靈魂了。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 「哈基米」原本是貓咪迷因的擬聲詞，2026 變成一個放在句尾裝可愛、裝沒事的萬用語助詞。遇到崩潰但又不想正面回應的時刻丟這三個字最剛好
variations: 賣萌版：「帳單來了，哈基米，假裝沒看到。」
結尾版：「今天也是努力活著的一天，哈基米。」

en.content:
Boss: We're working this weekend.
Me: Hakimi.

(Translation: my soul has officially left the building.)
en.editorNote: 'Hakimi' started as a cat-meme sound and by 2026 became an all-purpose cutesy tag-on you drop at the end of a sentence to deflect with charm. Ideal for moments you're quietly falling apart but won't say so
variations: Playing-cute version: 'The bill arrived. Hakimi. Pretending I didn't see it.'
Sign-off version: 'Survived another day of trying my best. Hakimi.'

items.sourceNote: 「那咩」為 2026 新興語助詞，帶撒嬌、轉折語氣，Threads 對話常見

zh-tw.content:
我知道我又遲到了，
那咩⋯⋯
你還是會原諒我對不對？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「那咩」是 2026 帶撒嬌與轉折的語助詞，放在句子中間軟化語氣，討拍或道歉時超好用。講的時候尾音拉長一點，殺傷力加倍
variations: 討拍版：「我也不想犯錯啊那咩，人都會累嘛。」
轉折版：「本來想生氣，那咩看到你傳貓貓我就消氣了。」

en.content:
I know I'm late again,
but like, you know⋯
you're still gonna forgive me, right?
en.editorNote: 'Na-me' (那咩) is a 2026 softening filler with a coaxing, pivoting vibe — slot it mid-sentence to take the edge off when you're apologizing or fishing for sympathy. Drag the vowel for extra effect
variations: Sympathy-fishing version: 'I didn't mean to mess up, you know, everyone gets tired.'
Pivot version: 'I was about to be mad, but you know, then you sent a cat pic and I melted.'

items.sourceNote: 「city不city」中英混搭迷因，問「夠不夠潮、夠不夠都會」，2026 仍在台灣社群延燒

zh-tw.content:
朋友：我們去鄉下露營吧。
我：那邊 city 不 city？
朋友：完全不 city。
我：那我帶三條毛巾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「city 不 city」是中英混搭迷因，問一個地方或一件事「夠不夠潮、夠不夠都會感」。用半開玩笑的語氣丟出來，朋友群組瞬間活絡
variations: 自嘲版：「我穿拖鞋去市區，整個人非常不 city。」
稱讚版：「這間咖啡廳也太 city 了吧，連水都很有態度。」

en.content:
Friend: Let's go camping in the countryside.
Me: Is it city or not city out there?
Friend: Absolutely not city.
Me: Then I'm packing three towels.
en.editorNote: 'City or not city' is a Mandarin-English mashup meme asking whether a place or thing is trendy and metropolitan enough. Toss it out half-joking and the group chat instantly comes alive
variations: Self-roast version: 'I wore flip-flops downtown — extremely not city of me.'
Compliment version: 'This cafe is way too city, even the water has attitude.'

items.sourceNote: Threads 網友提議用「扁掉」取代「破防」「走心」，主打可愛有視覺感，2026 上半年發酵中

zh-tw.content:
看到帳單那一刻，
我整個人扁掉。
像被現實壓成一張紙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「扁掉」是 2026 想取代「破防」的新講法，重點在那個畫面感——人被打擊到扁成一張紙。比破防可愛，適合用在不嚴重但很有戲的小崩潰
variations: 誇飾版：「他說已讀我，我直接扁到貼在地板上。」
可愛版：「我好餓，餓到扁掉，可以餵我嗎。」

en.content:
The second I saw the bill,
I just flattened.
Like reality pressed me into a sheet of paper.
en.editorNote: 'Flattened' is a 2026 Taiwanese challenger to the gaming-borrowed 'defense broken' — the appeal is the visual: a person squashed flat as paper by a small blow. Cuter and lighter, perfect for low-stakes dramatic meltdowns
variations: Exaggerated version: 'He left me on read and I flattened straight onto the floor.'
Cute version: 'I'm so hungry I've gone flat. Somebody feed me.'

items.sourceNote: 「逆風」2026 Threads 常見用語，指明知會被罵還是發表不討喜的意見

zh-tw.content:
我先逆風一下喔，
那部大家狂推的劇，
我看了三集真的睡著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「逆風」是 2026 常見發言開場，意思是「我知道接下來要講的會被圍剿，但我還是要說」。先丟這兩個字當緩衝，留言區的火氣會小一點
variations: 預防版：「逆風發言，珍奶我都點無糖，全糖那個太甜了。」
反串版：「不逆風，我覺得他講得超對，只是沒人敢承認。」

en.content:
Going against the wind here, but
that show everyone won't shut up about?
Three episodes in and I fell asleep.
en.editorNote: 'Against the wind' is a 2026 opener meaning 'I know this opinion is about to get me roasted, but I'm saying it anyway.' Drop it as a buffer and the replies come in slightly less hot
variations: Pre-emptive version: 'Against the wind, but I order all my bubble tea unsweetened — full sugar is too much.'
Ironic version: 'Not against the wind at all, he's completely right, nobody just wants to admit it.'

items.sourceNote: 「巴比Q」（BBQ）諧音梗，指事情完蛋、被烤了，2026 仍在台灣社群流通

zh-tw.content:
鬧鐘沒響，
會議十分鐘前才醒，
這下真的巴比Q了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「巴比Q」是 BBQ 的諧音，意思是「完蛋了、被烤焦了」。用在自己闖禍或事態失控的瞬間，比直接說「完了」多一點自嘲的喜感
variations: 誇張版：「報告檔沒存到，三天的努力直接巴比Q。」
預告版：「老闆走過來了，我感覺等一下要巴比Q。」

en.content:
Alarm didn't go off,
woke up ten minutes before the meeting —
yeah, I'm officially barbecued.
en.editorNote: 'Barbecued' (from BBQ) means you're toast, done for, getting grilled. Use it the moment you mess up or things spiral, and it lands with more self-mocking comedy than a flat 'I'm done'
variations: Dramatic version: 'Didn't save the file — three days of work, straight to the grill.'
Heads-up version: 'Boss is walking over. Feels like I'm about to get barbecued.'

items.sourceNote: 「鬆弛感」2026 流行語，形容毫不費力、不緊繃的從容狀態

zh-tw.content:
她遲到還能笑著走進來，
點一杯咖啡慢慢坐下，
這種鬆弛感我真的學不來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「鬆弛感」是 2026 用來誇人的詞，指那種毫不費力、不緊張兮兮的從容。可以拿來羨慕別人，也可以反過來自嘲自己一點都沒有
variations: 羨慕版：「他穿拖鞋去面試還錄取，鬆弛感拉滿。」
自嘲版：「我連等個外送都焦慮，鬆弛感是零。」

en.content:
She strolled in late, still smiling,
ordered a coffee, sat down slow —
I will never have that kind of ease.
en.editorNote: 'Looseness' (the unbothered ease) is a 2026 compliment for that effortless, never-frazzled composure. Use it to admire someone, or flip it to roast yourself for having exactly none of it
variations: Admiring version: 'He wore flip-flops to the interview and still got hired — ease level maxed.'
Self-roast version: 'I get anxious just waiting on a food delivery. My ease level is zero.'

items.sourceNote: 「含金量」2026 流行語，由「含金量」延伸，形容一件事的價值或含量高低

zh-tw.content:
這頓飯有貴賓還有甜點，
還有人幫我們拍照，
含金量整個爆表。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「含金量」原本講純度，現在拿來形容一件事「有多值得、份量多重」。誇活動、誇貼文、誇某人說的話都很好用，反過來嫌也行
variations: 稱讚版：「他這篇貼文含金量超高，每句都能拿來當座右銘。」
吐槽版：「開了一小時的會，含金量趨近於零。」

en.content:
VIP guest, dessert on the house,
someone even took our photos —
the gold content was off the charts.
en.editorNote: 'Gold content' originally meant purity; now it rates how worthwhile or substantial something is. Great for hyping an event, a post, or someone's words — and just as good for dunking when it's empty
variations: Praise version: 'His post had insane gold content — every line could be a life motto.'
Dunk version: 'One-hour meeting, gold content approaching zero.'

items.sourceNote: 「初級大人／高級大人」2026-02 過年後爆紅，2026 開年相關討論破 3.9 萬筆；形容成年人回家秒變小孩

zh-tw.content:
在外面我簽合約、扛專案、帶新人，
回到家連水費單放哪都要問我媽。

我是貨真價實的初級大人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「初級大人」指在外能獨當一面、一回家就退化成三歲的成年人；「高級大人」才是真的會處理水電帳單、看病掛號、人情往來的那種。過年家族聚會發這句最有共鳴，誰看了都對號入座
variations: 認證版：「出門不帶錢包、生果要問哪攤好吃——初級大人實錘。」
升級版：「等我哪天會自己約健檢、記得繳稅，再叫我高級大人。」

en.content:
Out there I sign contracts, run projects, train the new hires.
Back home I have to ask my mom where the water bill is.

I'm a certified junior adult.
en.editorNote: A 'junior adult' handles everything out in the world but reverts to a toddler the second they walk in the front door; a 'senior adult' actually deals with the bills, the doctor's appointments, the family obligations. Post this during a holiday family gathering and watch everyone tag themselves
variations: Confession version: 'Left the house with no wallet, had to ask which fruit stand is good — junior adult, confirmed.'
Upgrade version: 'The day I book my own checkup and remember to file taxes, then call me a senior adult.'

items.sourceNote: 「確定欸」=「你要確定欸」的簡寫，2026 年輕人口頭禪，用於傻眼、不可置信的調侃

zh-tw.content:
你說這餐你請，
結果結帳前去廁所十分鐘——

確定欸？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「確定欸」是「你要確定欸」的縮寫，看到離譜操作時丟這三個字，比直接罵人更殺。語氣要平、要慢，效果最好
variations: 驚訝版：「現在十一點半你才說要開始洗碗，確定欸？」
吐槽自己版：「我把鬧鐘設成下午六點還想說怎麼睡飽飽，確定欸我。」

en.content:
You said this meal was on you,
then vanished into the bathroom for ten minutes right before the check —

you sure about that?
en.editorNote: A clipped 'you sure about that?' is the 2026 way to call out a ridiculous move — drier and deadlier than actually getting mad. Deliver it flat and slow for maximum damage
variations: Disbelief version: 'It's 11:30 at night and you're just NOW starting the dishes — you sure about that?'
Self-roast version: 'Set my alarm for 6pm and wondered why I slept so great. You sure about that, me?'

items.sourceNote: 「歸剛欸」源自台語諧音，意思是嫌一個人整天閒著沒事、找事做

zh-tw.content:
群組裡半夜兩點丟長輩圖，
還@全部人問早安吃飽沒——

你是不是歸剛欸？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「歸剛欸」是台語諧音，專嫌人整天閒著沒事、淨找事做。語氣半開玩笑，朋友之間互嗆最對味，認真用會傷感情
variations: 互嗆版：「放假不睡覺，跑去整理三年前的截圖，歸剛欸喔。」
自嘲版：「我半夜爬起來研究別人的限動，純粹歸剛欸。」

en.content:
Two in the morning, dropping boomer-tier good-morning memes in the group chat,
@-ing everyone to ask if they've eaten —

you got nothing better to do?
en.editorNote: From a Taiwanese-Hokkien pun, this ribs someone for having way too much free time and inventing pointless things to do. Keep it playful — it's for roasting friends, not for an actual complaint
variations: Mutual roast version: 'Day off and instead of sleeping you're organizing screenshots from three years ago? Nothing better to do, huh.'
Self-roast version: 'Got up at 3am to study someone else's story highlights. Purely nothing-better-to-do energy.'

items.sourceNote: 「躁」2026 年輕人用語，意指很煩躁、不耐煩，近似台語「阿雜」

zh-tw.content:
冷氣壞了、訊息已讀不回、
外送遲到還送錯——

我今天的躁，含金量超高。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「躁」是 2026 把整句「我好煩」濃縮成一個字的用法，接近台語的「阿雜」，那種說不上哪裡不對、就是全身不耐煩。一個字傳出去，朋友秒懂
variations: 極簡版：直接傳一個「躁。」對方就知道今天別逗你。
加碼版：「躁到想把手機丟進海裡再撈回來繼續躁。」

en.content:
AC broke, texts left on read,
delivery showed up late AND wrong —

my 'on edge' today is off the charts.
en.editorNote: 'On edge' (the single Mandarin word 'zao') compresses a whole 'I'm so done' into one syllable — that restless, can't-sit-still irritation where nothing's specifically wrong, you're just over it. One word and your friend instantly gets it
variations: Minimalist version: just send 'on edge.' and they'll know not to poke the bear today.
Escalation version: 'So on edge I want to throw my phone in the ocean and fish it back out just to stay on edge.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 馬年梗：網友把祝賀語改成自嘲的「馬倒成功」

zh-tw.content:
新年目標：馬到成功。
七月結算：馬倒成功。

計畫全倒，
只有體重成功站起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「馬倒成功」是 2026 馬年爆紅的自嘲諧音，把「馬到成功」倒過來玩，形容計畫全崩但還是硬撐著活著。年中檢討、目標跳票時發這句最應景，比直接說「我廢掉了」有喜感
variations: 低調版：「今年不求馬到成功，馬倒成功就好，至少還在動。」
職場版：「季報做完，主管問進度，我只想回一句馬倒成功。」

en.content:
New Year's goal: gallop to success.
July check-in: galloped into the ground.

Every plan face-planted —
only my weight successfully stood back up.
en.editorNote: A 2026 Year-of-the-Horse pun that flips the classic blessing 'gallop to success' into 'galloped into the ground' — self-mockery for when every plan collapsed but you're somehow still upright. Perfect for mid-year reviews and dead resolutions; funnier than just saying 'I gave up'
variations: Low-key version: 'Not aiming to gallop to success this year — galloped-into-the-ground is fine, at least I'm still moving.'
Work version: 'Finished the quarterly report, boss asks about progress, all I've got is: galloped into the ground.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 喪萌代表物：哭哭馬玩偶，精準命中現代人喪萌心理

zh-tw.content:
我不是不快樂，
我是喪萌。

就像那隻哭哭馬——
眼角掛著淚，
嘴角還是彎的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「喪萌」是 2026 的情緒關鍵字：又喪又可愛，哭哭馬玩偶就是代言人。想告訴朋友「我狀態不好但還撐得住、還笑得出來」時很好用，比「我沒事」真誠，比「我快崩潰」不嚇人
variations: 自嘲版：「今天的我是人形哭哭馬，麻煩輕拿輕放。」
陪伴版：「你現在很喪萌沒關係，我陪你一起掛著淚彎嘴角。」

en.content:
I'm not unhappy,
I'm sad-cute.

Just like that crying-horse plushie —
tears in the corner of the eyes,
mouth still curved into a smile.
en.editorNote: 'Sad-cute' is a 2026 mood word: down but still adorable, embodied by the crying-horse plushie. Use it to tell a friend 'I'm not okay but I'm holding on and can still smile' — more honest than 'I'm fine,' less alarming than 'I'm falling apart'
variations: Self-deprecating version: 'Today I'm a human crying-horse plushie — please handle with care.'
Support version: 'It's okay to be sad-cute right now. I'll sit with you, tears and curved smile and all.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 分級大人用語新增中間層：中級大人（介於初級與高級之間）

zh-tw.content:
初級大人：熬夜檢討自己。
高級大人：關燈直接睡。
中級大人：

躺在床上檢討自己，
檢討到一半睡著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「中級大人」是 2026 從初級/高級大人衍生出的中間層，形容那種「想成熟但只成熟了一半」的真實狀態。發在深夜、或朋友在自我檢討時很有共鳴，比直接安慰更好笑也更貼
variations: 存錢版：「初級大人月光，高級大人理財，中級大人存了三千又忍不住買了三千的東西。」
自我認領版：「測驗結果：我是中級大人，成熟度加載中，進度 50%。」

en.content:
Junior adult: stays up dissecting their flaws.
Senior adult: lights off, straight to sleep.
Mid-tier adult:

lies in bed dissecting their flaws,
falls asleep halfway through.
en.editorNote: 'Mid-tier adult' is the 2026 middle rung spun off from junior/senior adult — that very real 'trying to be mature but only got halfway there' state. Lands well posted late at night or when a friend's spiraling into self-criticism; funnier and more relatable than a straight pep talk
variations: Money version: 'Junior adult lives paycheck to paycheck, senior adult invests, mid-tier adult saves three grand then instantly spends three grand.'
Self-claiming version: 'Quiz result: I'm a mid-tier adult. Maturity loading, 50% complete.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 Z世代縮寫：sldpk（笑到死／超好笑）

zh-tw.content:
朋朋傳來一張迷因，
我：sldpk。

笑到把嘴裡的珍奶噴在螢幕上，
螢幕跟我一起 sldpk。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「sldpk」是 2026 從縮寫玩起來的用語，意思接近「笑到不行」。留言區看到超好笑的東西，一個 sldpk 比打一串「哈哈哈哈」更潮。用在朋朋群組最自然
variations: 純回應版：直接一句「sldpk」，什麼都不用多說，對方就知道你笑爛了。
誇飾版：「這個梗讓我 sldpk 到隔壁鄰居來按門鈴問我還好嗎。」

en.content:
Palsy sends me a meme.
Me: sldpk.

Laughed so hard I sprayed bubble tea on the screen,
now the screen's sldpk-ing with me.
en.editorNote: 'sldpk' is a 2026 acronym-style slang meaning roughly 'dying laughing.' Drop it in the comments when something's hysterical — one sldpk reads cooler than a wall of 'hahaha.' Feels most natural in a buddy group chat
variations: Pure reaction version: just 'sldpk' on its own — no context needed, they'll know you lost it.
Exaggerated version: 'This meme had me sldpk so loud the neighbor rang the doorbell to check on me.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 Z世代用語：0尊（零尊重，用來吐槽不得體的發言）

zh-tw.content:
他遲到一小時，
進門第一句：「你怎麼點這麼少？」

0尊。

對我的等待 0尊，
對這桌菜也 0尊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「0尊」是 2026 常見吐槽語，意思是「零尊重」，用來回應那些沒禮貌又理直氣壯的發言。朋友之間半開玩笑地嗆最好用，語氣要輕，太認真就變吵架
variations: 接話版：「你這樣講真的 0尊，我尊重值直接歸零。」
自嘲版：「我對自己的鬧鐘也是 0尊，響八次一次都沒起。」

en.content:
He shows up an hour late,
first words through the door: 'why'd you order so little?'

Zero-respect.

Zero respect for the wait I put in,
zero respect for this whole table of food.
en.editorNote: 'Zero-respect' is a common 2026 comeback for replying to rude, self-assured remarks. Works best as a half-joking jab between friends — keep the tone light; play it too serious and it tips into an actual argument
variations: Reply version: 'Saying that is straight-up zero-respect — my respect meter just hit zero.'
Self-deprecating version: 'I've got zero-respect for my own alarm too — rang eight times, got up on none of them.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 流行語「硬啦」：源自遊戲硬控，延伸為「硬要」「就是要」的加強語氣

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點，
室友：「不要再追劇了啦。」

我：「硬啦。」

然後又點了下一集。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「硬啦」是 2026 從「硬控」長出來的口頭禪，意思接近「就是要」「硬要」，帶點死鴨子嘴硬的可愛。用在明知不該做但還是想做的事情上最傳神，語氣拖長那個「啦」才夠味
variations: 撒嬌版：「我知道很晚了，但我硬要跟你講電話，硬啦。」
自嘲版：「錢包空了還是點了那杯貴的，硬啦，開心最重要。」

en.content:
2 a.m.
Roommate: 'stop bingeing the show already.'

Me: 'nah, I'm doing it anyway.'

Then I hit play on the next episode.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'ying-la' grew out of the gaming term for 'hard control' and now means a stubborn, cute 'I'm doing it anyway.' Perfect for the stuff you know you shouldn't do but will — drag out that final syllable for full effect
variations: Sweet version: 'I know it's late, but I'm calling you anyway. Deal with it.'
Self-deprecating version: 'Wallet's empty and I still got the expensive drink — doing it anyway, joy comes first.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 用語「kam4」：kam=尷尬，4 諧音「死」，即「尷尬到死」

zh-tw.content:
跟主管揮手說掰掰，
結果他只是要走去廁所，

下一秒我們在洗手台又遇到。

kam4。
再揮一次更 kam4。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「kam4」是 2026 縮寫梗，kam 是尷尬、4 諧音「死」，合起來就是「尷尬到死」。拿來記錄自己的社交小車禍最好用，配一張捂臉貼圖，朋友一看就懂那種想鑽地洞的感覺
variations: 連鎖版：「電梯門關到一半又打開，我跟他大眼瞪小眼，double kam4。」
回應版：朋友講完糗事，你回一句「這真的 kam4」就是最到位的共感。

en.content:
Waved goodbye to my boss,
turns out he was just heading to the bathroom,

next second we meet again at the sink.

Mortified.
Waving a second time — even more mortified.
en.editorNote: 'kam4' is a 2026 acronym mash-up: 'kam' means awkward and '4' sounds like 'death' in Mandarin, so together it's 'awkward to death.' Ideal for logging your own tiny social crashes — pair it with a facepalm sticker and everyone gets the want-to-disappear feeling
variations: Chain-reaction version: 'The elevator door closed halfway then reopened and we just stared at each other — double kam4.'
Reply version: when a friend shares an embarrassing story, 'that's genuinely kam4' is the most on-point way to commiserate.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 流行語「大癲」：源自誇張、瘋癲的行為，形容某事很扯很浮誇

zh-tw.content:
他為了搶超商第二件半價，
一次買了六罐同款咖啡，

喝到第三罐開始手抖，
還跟我說很划算。

這人有夠大癲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「大癲」是 2026 形容「誇張到很瘋」的用語，介於吐槽和佩服之間。拿來講朋友那種明明不需要卻硬要衝的浮誇行為最傳神，語氣可以又好氣又好笑
variations: 佩服版：「他為了看一場演唱會飛三個國家，大癲，但我尊敬。」
自嘲版：「我半夜手滑買了露營帳篷，我家連陽台都沒有，超大癲。」

en.content:
To score the buy-one-get-one-half-off deal,
he bought six cans of the exact same coffee,

hands shaking by the third can,
still telling me what a steal it was.

This guy is next-level unhinged.
en.editorNote: 'da-dian' is a 2026 word for 'so over-the-top it's wild' — landing somewhere between roasting someone and being impressed. Best for a friend's flashy, totally-unnecessary stunts; the tone can be equal parts exasperated and delighted
variations: Impressed version: 'He flew through three countries for one concert — unhinged, but I respect it.'
Self-deprecating version: 'Fat-fingered a camping tent at midnight. I don't even have a balcony. Peak unhinged.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 上班族用語「班味」：形容上班太久身上散發的疲憊、社畜氣息

zh-tw.content:
放假第一天照鏡子，
黑眼圈還在，
嘴角還是自動下垂。

班味這種東西，
洗三次澡都洗不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「班味」是 2026 上班族自嘲用語，指上班太久身上那股洗不掉的疲憊氣息。連假前後傳到群組最有共鳴，配一句「我班味還沒退」大家都懂那種靈魂被抽乾的狀態
variations: 抱怨版：「開了一整天的會，我現在班味濃到可以醃起來。」
療癒版：「這趟旅行的任務只有一個：把身上的班味通通蒸發掉。」

en.content:
First day off, I look in the mirror,
the dark circles are still there,
the corners of my mouth still droop on their own.

This office-worn smell —
three showers won't wash it off.
en.editorNote: 'ban-wei' (office smell) is a 2026 self-mocking term for the worn-out, wage-slave aura that sticks to you after too much work. It hits hardest right around a long weekend — 'my office smell hasn't faded yet' and everyone knows that soul-drained feeling
variations: Complaint version: 'Meetings all day — my office smell is strong enough to cure into jerky now.'
Healing version: 'This trip has exactly one mission: steam every last trace of office smell off me.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 用語「好解」：形容一段內容非常有共感、能解到心坎裡

zh-tw.content:
滑到一篇貼文寫著：
「不是不想努力，
是連努力的力氣都要先攢好久。」

好解。
這句直接把我沒說出口的話講完了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「好解」是 2026 形容「超有共感、解到心坎裡」的用語。看到一句話剛好講中自己的狀態，留言一個「好解」比長篇大論更有力。用在深夜療癒系貼文的留言區最自然
variations: 加強版：「這段文字太好解了，我一邊點頭一邊眼眶熱。」
回應版：朋友吐露心事，你回「我懂，這真的好解」就是最溫柔的接住。

en.content:
Scrolled past a post that read:
'It's not that I don't want to try,
it's that even the energy to try takes forever to save up.'

So relatable it hurts.
That line finished the sentence I never said out loud.
en.editorNote: 'hao-jie' is a 2026 term for 'so relatable it lands right in your chest.' When a line nails exactly how you feel, dropping a 'hao-jie' says more than a paragraph ever could. Feels most natural in the comments of a late-night comfort post
variations: Emphatic version: 'This passage is so relatable — I was nodding along with my eyes welling up.'
Reply version: when a friend opens up, 'I get it, this really lands' is the gentlest way to catch them.'

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 這個梗有夠冷，我尷尬到腳趾都摳出三房兩廳了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：朋友講了超乾的笑話、群組裡有人硬要玩梗但完全不好笑時，用來吐槽對方讓你尷尬到不行。
variations: 笑話冷成這樣，我腳趾都要摳出一間套房了。
拜託不要再講了，我尷尬癌都要末期了。

en.content: That joke was so bad I secondhand-cringed into another dimension.
en.editorNote: Use when a friend drops a painfully unfunny joke and you want to roast them for the awkwardness.
variations: That was so dry I felt myself age ten years.
The secondhand embarrassment nearly took me out.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 先說好，這件事我根本不緊張，是這件事該緊張遇到我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：面試、比賽、報告前幫自己或朋友壯膽打氣，展現一種霸氣自信的態度。
variations: 不是我要面對挑戰，是挑戰要面對我。
緊張的應該是對手，不是我。

en.content: I'm not nervous about this—this should be nervous about meeting me.
en.editorNote: A cocky hype line to psych yourself or a friend up before an interview, game, or big presentation.
variations: The challenge isn't ready for me, honestly.
Nervous? Nah, the competition should be worried.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 月底了，我的錢包比我的行事曆還空，連風都吹得進來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：發薪日前手頭很緊、朋友揪吃大餐卻不好意思說沒錢時，用幽默的方式自嘲。
variations: 月底的我，只剩呼吸是免費的。
錢包空到可以拿來養蚊子了。

en.content: It's the end of the month—my wallet's so empty you can hear an echo.
en.editorNote: A self-deprecating line for when you're broke before payday and a friend suggests going somewhere pricey.
variations: By month's end the only free thing I've got left is breathing.
My bank account and I are both running on fumes.

items.sourceNote: 網路溫度計：初級／中級／高級大人 自我檢測爆紅

zh-tw.content:
我是初級大人。
會自己繳帳單，但不會自己面對帳單金額。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：Threads 自我介紹、跟朋友互相取暖時。「初級大人」形容表面成年、內心還在裝忙的狀態，比說「我好累」有梗多了。
variations: 中級大人的意思是：帳單看得懂了，還是不想繳。
我卡在初級大人升不上去，因為升級要收費。

en.content:
I'm a junior-level adult.
I can pay my own bills, I just can't look at the number on them.
en.editorNote: For a Threads bio or commiserating with friends. The Mandarin 'junior adult' trend describes people who are technically grown up but still winging it — funnier than just saying 'I'm exhausted.'
variations: Mid-level adult means you finally understand the bill and still don't want to pay it.
I'm stuck at junior adult because leveling up costs extra.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：delulu / delulu is the solulu

zh-tw.content:
他兩天沒回訊息，但已讀是一種回應。
我不是妄想，我是超前部署的樂觀。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：曖昧卡關、朋友吐槽你想太多的時候。對應英文的 delulu（妄想式樂觀），用來自嘲又保有一絲希望。
variations: 他還沒拒絕我，等於默許，這叫策略性樂觀。
妄想不是問題，妄想就是解答。

en.content:
He hasn't texted in two days, but leaving me on read is technically a reply.
I'm not delusional, I'm just early to the optimism.
en.editorNote: For when a talking stage stalls and your friends call you out for overthinking. Plays on 'delulu is the solulu' — self-aware copium with a wink.
variations: He hasn't said no yet, so that's basically a yes. Strategic optimism.
Delulu isn't the problem, delulu is the solulu.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：side quest（生活支線任務）

zh-tw.content:
我只是出門買咖啡，結果現在人在朋友家頂樓看夕陽。
主線任務可以先擱著，這條支線比較好玩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容原本要辦正事卻被生活岔開、玩心大發的一天。對應 Gen Z 的 side quest（支線任務），用來替臨時脫軌合理化。
variations: 本來要回家的，被一隻路邊的貓觸發了隱藏支線。
主線今天請假，我去跑支線了。

en.content:
I left the house to grab a coffee and somehow I'm now on a friend's rooftop watching the sunset.
The main quest can wait — this side quest is way better.
en.editorNote: For a day that gets happily hijacked by little detours. 'Side quest' is the perfect excuse for going off-script and enjoying it.
variations: I was heading home until a cat on the sidewalk triggered a hidden side quest.
Main quest's on break today, I'm out here grinding side quests.

items.sourceNote: 2026 迷因整理：ㄅ級分（最高滿分評價）

zh-tw.content:
這頓宵夜，我給ㄅ級分。
沒有更高的了，這就是天花板。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吃到、看到、體驗到超讚的東西想給滿分時。「ㄅ級分」是 2026 迷因式最高評價，比說「滿分」多一份親切的無厘頭。
variations: 這部影集追到凌晨還不想睡，直接ㄅ級分。
今天的天氣ㄅ級分，只可惜我要上班。

en.content:
This late-night snack? I'm giving it a solid A-plus-plus.
There's no higher grade — this is the ceiling.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'B-grade' meme flips a low-looking mark into the top score, so it lands as playful praise. Use it to hype up anything genuinely great.
variations: Binged the whole show till 3am and still didn't want it to end — top marks, no notes.
Today's weather is a perfect score, shame I have to work.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：404 coded（狀況外、反應慢）

zh-tw.content:
大家在笑什麼我沒跟到，我整個人 404。
訊號有，人沒載入完成。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：群組聊天跟不上、腦袋當機時自嘲。借用網頁 404 找不到頁面的梗，形容整個人狀況外、還在緩衝。
variations: 早上第一杯咖啡前的我，處於 404 狀態。
他問我在想什麼，我腦袋回傳 404。

en.content:
Everyone's laughing and I did not catch the joke — I'm fully 404 right now.
Signal's there, I'm just not done loading.
en.editorNote: For when a group chat moves too fast or your brain buffers. '404 coded' borrows the missing-page error to say you're out of the loop and still catching up.
variations: Pre-first-coffee me is running on a 404.
He asked what I was thinking and my brain returned a 404.

items.sourceNote: 2026 台灣流行語「電子布洛芬」：能治癒壞心情的搞笑影片

zh-tw.content:
今天被主管釘到牆上，
回家滑三支貓咪影片，
痛感瞬間退掉——
這就是我的電子布洛芬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：心情很差、需要一點無腦快樂止痛時。「電子布洛芬」指那種一看就能止痛的搞笑或療癒影片，比說「我需要放鬆」更有畫面。
variations: 分手那週我靠電子布洛芬硬撐過來的，一支接一支。
推薦你一個頻道，效果等同一整排電子布洛芬。

en.content:
Got chewed out by my boss today.
Came home, watched three cat clips,
and the ache just faded —
that's my digital ibuprofen.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'electronic ibuprofen' names those dumb-funny or soothing clips that painkill a bad mood on contact. Use it instead of a flat 'I need to unwind' — it's more vivid.
variations: The week we broke up, I survived on digital ibuprofen — one clip after another.
Let me send you a channel; it works like a whole box of digital ibuprofen.

items.sourceNote: 2026 台灣流行語「活人感」：真實自然、不像機器人的狀態

zh-tw.content:
你的貼文很有活人感，
不完美、有錯字、
還會抱怨天氣——
所以我才追蹤你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：稱讚別人真實不做作，或幫自己太隨性的貼文自嘲。「活人感」指那種不像官方帳號、有溫度有瑕疵的真實感，是 2026 很高的讚美。
variations: 限動全是碎念跟醜自拍，活人感滿分，我喜歡。
帳號經營太精緻反而沒活人感，偶爾出個包才親切。

en.content:
Your posts have real-human energy —
typos, imperfections,
and the occasional weather rant.
That's exactly why I follow you.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'live-person feel' praises content that reads like an actual messy human, not a polished brand account. In 2026 it's high praise; use it to compliment someone's realness or to shrug off your own too-casual post.
variations: Their stories are all rambling and ugly selfies — max human energy, love it.
Feeds that are too curated lose that human energy; an occasional flub makes you likable.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：crash out（情緒失控、當場崩潰）

zh-tw.content:
訊息已讀不回超過三小時，
我已經在崩潰邊緣起飛。
再不回我真的要當場 crash out。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：情緒到臨界點、想誇張地宣告自己要爆炸時。「crash out」指當場情緒失控、演一齣崩潰大戲，通常帶點自嘲、不是真的抓狂。
variations: 看到帳單金額，我在便利商店門口差點 crash out。
最後一題寫不出來，我整個人 crash out 給監考老師看。

en.content:
Read but no reply for three whole hours.
I'm already cleared for takeoff into meltdown.
One more minute and I'm gonna crash out right here.
en.editorNote: 'Crash out' means losing emotional control in spectacular, often public fashion. It's usually played for laughs — you're announcing a dramatic meltdown, not actually raging. Use it when your feelings hit the ceiling.
variations: Saw the bill total and nearly crashed out in the convenience-store doorway.
Couldn't solve the last question, so I just crashed out in front of the invigilator.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：beige flag（中性怪癖，不加分也不扣分）

zh-tw.content:
他每次點餐都點一模一樣的東西，
三年沒變過。
不是紅旗也不是綠旗，
是一面很他的米色旗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容對象那種無傷大雅、有點好笑的中性怪癖。「米色旗」介於紅旗（危險）跟綠旗（加分）之間，不扣分也不加分，但就是很有記憶點。
variations: 她把香料罐照字母排好，米色旗一面，但意外地可愛。
他手機從不設鬧鐘卻永遠準時起床，這面米色旗我看不懂。

en.content:
He orders the exact same thing every single time,
hasn't changed in three years.
Not a red flag, not a green flag —
just a very him beige flag.
en.editorNote: A 'beige flag' is a neutral quirk — neither attractive nor off-putting, just oddly memorable. Use it to describe a partner's harmless, mildly funny habit that sits between a red flag and a green flag.
variations: She alphabetizes the spice rack — total beige flag, but weirdly cute.
He never sets an alarm yet always wakes up on time; that beige flag baffles me.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：I know ball（對某領域很懂、品味很高）

zh-tw.content:
歌單交給我就對了，
不用查、不用問，
這領域我 I know ball。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：想帥氣宣告自己對某個領域很懂、品味很好時。「I know ball」原意是「我懂球」，現在泛指對音樂、時尚、美食等任何領域有內行品味的自信宣言。
variations: 選咖啡店這件事你信我，I know ball。
別人推薦的我都看不上，追劇這塊我 I know ball。

en.content:
Just hand me the playlist —
no Googling, no asking around.
This field? I know ball.
en.editorNote: 'I know ball' means you deeply get a subject and have elite taste — music, fashion, food, whatever. Use it as a confident flex that your judgment in some area can be trusted.
variations: Trust me on picking the cafe — I know ball.
Other people's recs never land for me; when it comes to shows, I know ball.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Threads 流行語 YBSG，源自日文「やばすぎ」羅馬字縮寫，正負面情境皆可用

zh-tw.content:
剛剛那個晚霞真的
YBSG，
我拍了四十張還是覺得輸給現場。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：任何「太扯、太誇張、太厲害」的當下都能丟一句。YBSG 是日文「やばすぎ」的羅馬字縮寫，好處是正面驚豔或負面傻眼都通用，語氣比「天啊」更潮一點。
variations: 他一個人吃掉整鍋，YBSG。
這價格 YBSG，我先走了。

en.content:
That sunset just now was straight-up
YBSG.
I took forty photos and none of them beat the real thing.
en.editorNote: YBSG comes from the Japanese 'yabasugi' — 'way too much.' Drop it whenever something is over-the-top, whether that's jaw-droppingly good or unbelievably bad. It works both ways, which is the whole appeal.
variations: He finished the entire pot solo. YBSG.
That price tag is YBSG. I'm out.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Threads 流行語 ㄅ級分，源自 YouTuber 金童的注音評分系統，ㄅ為最高分

zh-tw.content:
這家滷肉飯我給ㄅ級分，
沒有ㄆ級的空間，
就是ㄅ。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：想給滿分好評又想講得比別人有梗時。「ㄅ級分」用注音ㄅㄆㄇ當評分等級，ㄅ是最高分，被稱為台灣人專屬加密語言，外國人看了會一頭霧水。
variations: 這部片ㄅ級分，我要二刷。
服務態度直接掉到ㄈ級分，不會再來。

en.content:
This braised-pork rice gets a B-grade from me —
and I mean B as in the top of the scale,
no room to drop to a P.
en.editorNote: In Taiwan's 2026 Zhuyin grading meme, the phonetic symbols B-P-M-F rank things from best to worst, with B at the top. It's basically an insider code — locals get it instantly, everyone else is lost. Use it to hand out a witty top-marks review.
variations: That movie's a solid B-grade. I'm watching it twice.
Their service dropped straight to F-grade. Never going back.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Threads 流行語 高麗菜煮蛋那桌，源自插畫家 Kurt Wu 火鍋妙招，用來稱讚分享實用訣竅的人

zh-tw.content:
你這招充電線收納法太神了，
請去高麗菜煮蛋那桌坐，
那桌都是智者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：有人分享超實用小技巧時的最高敬意。這梗源自一個用高麗菜葉煮蛋的火鍋妙招，「請去那桌坐」等於「你太有智慧了」，帶點好笑又真心的佩服。
variations: 教我用夾子開易開罐的那位，高麗菜那桌留一個位子給你。
這個 Excel 快捷鍵直接送你去高麗菜煮蛋那桌。

en.content:
Your cable-storage hack is genius —
go take a seat at the wise table,
that's where all the smart ones sit.
en.editorNote: This one traces back to a viral hot-pot tip (boiling an egg on a cabbage leaf). Telling someone to 'go sit at that table' is the highest, half-joking compliment for anyone who just dropped a genuinely clever life hack.
variations: Whoever taught me to open cans with tongs — there's a seat at the wise table for you.
That Excel shortcut earns you a spot at the wise table.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：delulu（delusional 縮寫，形容不切實際的樂觀幻想，常用於感情或職涯）

zh-tw.content:
他已讀我三天了，
但我覺得他只是在忙。
我知道我很 delulu，
可是 delulu 才是我的解藥。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：自嘲那種明知不切實際、還是要抱著的幻想。delulu 是 delusional 的縮寫，多用在感情或追星，語氣是笑自己一廂情願，但又甘之如飴。
variations: 我還沒面試就在想到職的辦公室要坐哪，delulu 上身。
buy 樂透前先規劃好中獎行程，這就是 delulu 的浪漫。

en.content:
He's left me on read for three days,
but I've decided he's just busy.
I know I'm delulu —
but delulu is the solulu.
en.editorNote: 'Delulu' is short for delusional, used to poke fun at your own wildly optimistic fantasies, usually about a crush or a career. The tone is self-aware: you know it's unrealistic and you're leaning in anyway.
variations: Haven't even had the interview yet and I'm picking out my desk. Peak delulu.
Planning the vacation before buying the lottery ticket — that's the delulu romance.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：404 coded（借用 HTTP 404 錯誤，形容某人狀況外、反應慢半拍）

zh-tw.content:
整桌都在笑那個梗，
只有他一臉問號，
非常 404 coded，
頁面找不到那種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某人完全狀況外、跟不上話題。404 coded 借用網頁「404 找不到」的錯誤，形容一個人反應慢半拍或搞不清楚現況，語氣是好笑的調侃而非惡意。
variations: 他問我 YBSG 是什麼，整個 404 coded。
會議講到一半我斷線，腦袋直接 404 coded。

en.content:
The whole table's laughing at the joke,
and he's just sitting there with a question mark —
seriously 404 coded,
page-not-found energy.
en.editorNote: '404 coded' borrows the web's page-not-found error to describe someone who's totally out of the loop or slow to catch on. Use it as light-hearted teasing when a friend clearly missed the memo, not as a real insult.
variations: He asked me what YBSG means — fully 404 coded.
Zoned out mid-meeting and my brain went straight to 404 coded.

items.sourceNote: 2026 迷因「six seven／六七」：源自饒舌歌詞，被學生亂喊，本身無意義的洗腦口頭禪

zh-tw.content:
問他數學算到哪，
他只回我「六七」，
然後兩手上下亂比，
我到現在不懂那什麼意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某個沒意義卻莫名洗腦的迷因。「六七（six seven）」源自一首饒舌歌歌詞，被學生們拿來到處亂喊，本身沒有任何意思，純粹好笑又煩人。看到有人無故喊六七、還配上手勢，就是這個梗。
variations: 老師問今天幾號，全班一起喊六七。
這題答案是六七，不對，我只是想講六七。

en.content:
Ask him what the answer is,
he just says 'six seven',
waves his hands up and down —
still no clue what it means.
en.editorNote: 'Six seven' (or 6 7) is a nonsense phrase from a rap lyric that kids started yelling everywhere in 2026, usually with a hand wave. It means absolutely nothing — that's the whole joke. Use it when something is pointlessly catchy or when you want to annoy your friends with peak brain-rot humor.
variations: Teacher asked what day it is and the whole class yelled 'six seven'.
The answer is six seven — no wait, I just wanted to say six seven.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：cooked（熟了，用食物被煮熟比喻已成定局的失敗）

zh-tw.content:
考卷發下來，
第一題就不會，
翻到最後一頁還是不會，
這次是真的熟了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容自己或某件事完蛋了、沒救了。「cooked（熟了）」用食物被煮熟比喻已成定局的失敗，語氣是半自嘲的認命。台灣網友也會直接說「我熟了」或「這局熟了」。
variations: 報告明天要交我還沒開始，熟了。
他把已讀不回的截圖傳到群組，那個人熟了。

en.content:
Grades come out,
blank on question one,
still blank on the last page —
yeah, I'm cooked.
en.editorNote: 'Cooked' means done for, hopeless, beyond saving — like food that's already been overcooked. It's usually said with a self-aware shrug. 'We're so cooked' ups the drama for a shared disaster.
variations: Deadline's tomorrow and I haven't started. I'm cooked.
He screenshotted the left-on-read text to the group chat — that guy is cooked.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：yap／yapper（話很多、嘴個不停卻沒重點）

zh-tw.content:
他一開口就是四十分鐘，
中間沒換過氣，
重點一個都沒有，
純度百分百的 yap。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某人話很多、講一堆廢話。「yap」原意是小狗汪汪叫，Z世代用來形容一個人嘴個不停卻沒重點。可以說「別 yap 了」或稱某人是「yapper」，語氣調侃居多。
variations: 群組六百則未讀，八成都是他在 yap。
會議前十分鐘全是老闆在 yap，正題三句話就講完。

en.content:
He opens his mouth and forty minutes go by,
no pause for breath,
not a single point made —
one hundred percent pure yap.
en.editorNote: 'Yap' means to talk on and on with no real substance — from the yipping of a small dog. Call someone a 'yapper', or tell a friend to 'stop yapping'. Mostly playful teasing, not a real insult.
variations: Six hundred unread in the group chat, and it's all him yapping.
First ten minutes of the meeting was pure yap, then the actual point took three sentences.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：cheugy（過氣、有點俗，努力跟潮卻慢半拍）

zh-tw.content:
他傳早安圖配一朵玫瑰，
簽名檔還有勵志名言，
不是不好，
就是有點 cheugy。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容某個東西過氣、有點俗、努力想跟上潮流卻慢半拍。「cheugy」特別指那種前幾年很紅、現在看有點尷尬的風格，語氣不是惡意，比較像溫和地吐槽。
variations: 那家店的裝潢是十年前的網美風，超 cheugy。
他還在用那個過氣濾鏡，有點 cheugy 但我沒說。

en.content:
He sends a good-morning graphic with a single rose,
inspirational quote in his signature —
not bad, exactly,
just a little cheugy.
en.editorNote: 'Cheugy' describes something out of date or trying too hard to be trendy but landing a beat behind — usually a style that was cool a few years ago and now feels slightly awkward. It's gentle ribbing, not a harsh dig.
variations: That cafe's decor is peak 2018 influencer aesthetic — so cheugy.
He's still using that dead filter. A little cheugy, but I said nothing.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：girl dinner（女子晚餐，隨手湊出來的零食剩菜一餐）

zh-tw.content:
晚餐是三片起司、
半盒餅乾、
還有昨天剩的一顆蛋，
這就是我的女子晚餐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容那種不下廚、隨手湊出來的一餐，通常是零食、剩菜、水果拼在一起。「girl dinner（女子晚餐）」沒有貶意，反而帶點理直氣壯的可愛，男生也能用。
variations: 冰箱剩什麼吃什麼，今晚女子晚餐無極限。
點外送太麻煩，洋芋片配優格就是一餐。

en.content:
Dinner is three slices of cheese,
half a sleeve of crackers,
and one leftover egg from yesterday —
that's my girl dinner.
en.editorNote: 'Girl dinner' is a low-effort meal thrown together from snacks, leftovers, and random fridge finds — no cooking involved. There's zero shame in it; it's said with a proud, cozy energy, and anyone can claim it.
variations: Eating whatever's left in the fridge — tonight's girl dinner knows no limits.
Ordering delivery felt like too much, so chips and yogurt it is.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：no cap（沒騙你、真的，用來強調自己說的是實話）

zh-tw.content:
那家滷肉飯我排了四十分鐘，
值得，
一口下去我差點哭出來，
no cap。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：想強調自己講的是真話、沒有誇張。「cap」是謊話，「no cap」就是「不蓋你、真的」，通常放句尾加強語氣。跟朋友分享真心推薦或認真表態時最好用。
variations: 我這輩子沒看過這麼扯的操作，no cap。
他真的一句廢話都沒有，全場最強，這個我 no cap。

en.content:
I waited forty minutes for that braised pork rice,
worth every second,
one bite and I nearly teared up —
no cap.
en.editorNote: 'No cap' means 'no lie, for real' — 'cap' is a lie, so 'no cap' shuts down any doubt that you're exaggerating. Drop it at the end of a sentence when you're genuinely recommending something or making an honest confession.
variations: I've never seen a play that wild in my life, no cap.
He didn't waste a single word, best in the room — and I say that no cap.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：chopped（形容很醜、很糟、狀態很差）

zh-tw.content:
早上七點被鬧鐘叫醒，
鏡子裡那張臉，
瀏海翹起來、眼睛腫一半，
只能說有夠 chopped。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某個東西很醜、很糟，或自嘲自己狀態很差。「chopped」語氣偏誇張搞笑，多半用在朋友之間開玩笑，別對真的在意外表的人亂用。
variations: 熬夜三天的我現在超 chopped，先別視訊。
這件衣服穿在我身上有點 chopped，還是算了。

en.content:
Alarm goes off at seven,
I look in the mirror,
bangs sticking up, one eye still puffy —
I can only call it chopped.
en.editorNote: 'Chopped' means ugly, busted, or looking rough. It's exaggerated and jokey, so keep it to friends who'll laugh — don't aim it at someone actually self-conscious about how they look.
variations: Three all-nighters in and I'm looking chopped — no video call right now.
This shirt looks kinda chopped on me. Gonna pass.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：bussin（超好吃、超讚，多用來形容食物）

zh-tw.content:
巷口那攤蚵仔煎，
醬料自己調的，
第一口下去我就懂了，
這個真的 bussin。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容食物超好吃，好吃到想大聲宣布。「bussin」原本專指美食，現在也能延伸稱讚任何超讚的東西。想加強可以說「bussin bussin」，語氣會更誇張。
variations: 這杯手搖半糖去冰 bussin，我要再點一杯。
他做的咖哩 bussin bussin，秒殺外面餐廳。

en.content:
That oyster omelette stall by the alley,
sauce mixed by hand,
one bite in and I got it —
this is straight-up bussin.
en.editorNote: 'Bussin' means something tastes amazing — good enough to announce out loud. It started as a food-only word but now stretches to anything that's a total hit. Double it up as 'bussin bussin' when you want to really sell how good it is.
variations: This half-sugar no-ice bubble tea is bussin — ordering another.
His curry is bussin bussin, blows the restaurants out of the water.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：steez（style + ease，形容毫不費力的自信時髦）

zh-tw.content:
他就一件白 T 配舊牛仔褲，
頭髮也沒特別弄，
走進來全場都看他，
這就是 steez。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：稱讚某人風格好，而且是那種完全不費力、渾然天成的時髦。「steez」是 style 加 ease，重點在「看起來毫不刻意」。想誇朋友很有型又不油可以用這個。
variations: 她穿搭永遠很 steez，隨便抓都好看。
重點不是衣服多貴，是那個 steez 學不來。

en.content:
Just a white tee and old jeans,
hair barely touched,
he walks in and the whole room looks —
that right there is steez.
en.editorNote: 'Steez' — style plus ease — is effortless, natural cool: looking sharp without looking like you tried. Use it to hype a friend's fit when the whole point is that it seems totally unforced.
variations: Her outfits are always steez — she could grab anything and it works.
It's not about how pricey the clothes are, it's the steez you can't fake.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：slaps（形容歌曲、東西超讚、超帶感）

zh-tw.content:
副歌一進來，
整台車的人同時安靜，
然後一起跟著吼，
這首歌真的 slaps。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容一首歌超讚、超帶感，聽了會忍不住點頭或大聲跟唱。「slaps」原本專講音樂，現在也能拿來誇任何讓人上頭的東西。反過來不好聽就說「這首不 slap」。
variations: 這張專輯每一首都 slaps，一首都跳不了。
深夜配這個 playlist，真的 slaps。

en.content:
The chorus drops,
the whole car goes silent for a second,
then everyone screams it together —
this song absolutely slaps.
en.editorNote: 'Slaps' means a track goes hard — the kind that makes you nod or belt it out loud. It started as a music word but now works for anything that hits just right. Flip it for a dud: 'this one doesn't slap.'
variations: Every track on this album slaps — can't skip a single one.
This playlist late at night? Absolutely slaps.

items.sourceNote: PopDaily 2026 Threads 爆紅梗：「脆上的人會懂的」用來圈同溫層找認同

zh-tw.content:
半夜點開對話框，
打了三行又全部刪掉，
這種心情，
脆上的人會懂的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：在 Threads 發文最後補上這句，等於「懂的都懂」，用來圈出同溫層、討一個認同。發完留言區通常馬上有人接「真的」。太細節、細節到懶得解釋的心情最適合用。
variations: 剛下班又被工作訊息追殺的那種累，脆上的人會懂的。
看到喜歡的人已讀不回，那一秒的窒息，脆上的人會懂的。

en.content:
That 3am urge to open the chat,
type out three lines,
then delete every one —
if you're on Threads, you just get it.
en.editorNote: A Threads sign-off that means 'if you know, you know.' You tack it onto a post to rally your people and fish for a 'same' in the replies. Best for a feeling too specific to spell out, when you know someone out there is nodding along.
variations: Getting chased by work messages the second you clock out — if you're on Threads, you get it.
The half-second you choke seeing your crush leave you on read — if you know, you know.

items.sourceNote: 台灣網路用語「很有事」：吐槽行為荒謬誇張，多帶寵溺搞笑語氣

zh-tw.content:
他把珍奶當醬料，
整杯淋在雞排上，
還說這是隱藏吃法，
這個人真的很有事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某人行為很誇張、很荒謬，但通常帶著寵溺又好笑，不是真的生氣。想虧朋友腦洞開太大可以用，反過來自嘲也行：「我也很有事。」
variations: 凌晨三點傳一整排迷因給我，你很有事欸。
把辦公室椅子調到最高假裝在開會，這群人有夠有事。

en.content:
He poured bubble tea like it's sauce,
soaked the whole fried chicken cutlet,
called it a secret menu —
this guy is genuinely unhinged.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'hen you shi' calls someone out for being absurd or ridiculous, but it's almost always said with a fond, laughing tone — not real anger. Use it to roast a friend's chaotic idea, or aim it at yourself: 'yeah, I'm unhinged too.'
variations: Sending me a whole row of memes at 3am — you're unhinged, you know that?
Cranking the office chair to max height to fake a meeting — this crew is so unhinged.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：glazing（過度吹捧、拍馬屁到失去客觀）

zh-tw.content:
他球隊明明連輸十場，
你還在說他是史上最強，
拜託，
你別再 glazing 了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：吐槽某人「過度吹捧」某個人事物，捧到失去客觀、有點肉麻。「glazing」原意是幫甜甜圈淋糖霜，引申成「猛灌美言」。想叫朋友別再舔了可以用這個。
variations: 偶像放個屁你都能寫成千字讚美文，你 glazing 到我都尷尬。
老闆一個爛決定你也拼命附和，別 glazing 了好嗎。

en.content:
His team's dropped ten straight
and you're still calling him the greatest ever —
please,
quit the glazing.
en.editorNote: 'Glazing' is laying compliments on way too thick — hyping someone past the point of honesty until it gets cringe. It comes from the sugar coating on a donut. Use it to tell a friend they're gassing someone up a little too hard.
variations: Your idol sneezes and you write a thousand-word tribute — the glazing is making me cringe.
The boss makes a terrible call and you cheer it on anyway? Stop glazing.

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：finna（fixing to / going to 的口語縮寫，語氣慵懶）

zh-tw.content:
鬧鐘還沒響，
但我已經睜眼躺了半小時，
I'm finna 直接放棄今天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：「finna」是「fixing to / going to」的口語縮寫，表示「正打算、馬上要」做某事。語氣很隨性，講出來自帶美式慵懶感。宣布下一步動作時特別好用。
variations: 訊息已讀我還沒想好怎麼回，I'm finna 先去洗澡逃避一下。
這集太好看，我 finna 一次追到天亮。

en.content:
Alarm hasn't even gone off,
but I've been lying here awake for thirty minutes —
I'm finna give up on today already.
en.editorNote: 'Finna' is a laid-back shortcut for 'fixing to' or 'going to' — you're about to do something, any second now. It carries an effortless, drawled vibe. Perfect for calling your next move.
variations: Read the text, still don't know how to reply — I'm finna shower and dodge it for a bit.
This episode's too good, I'm finna binge till sunrise.

items.sourceNote: 2026 teen slang：ragebait（故意激怒觀眾以騙互動的內容）

zh-tw.content:
標題寫「這樣煮義大利麵才正確」，
然後把麵折斷丟進冷水，
一看就知道是 ragebait。
zh-tw.editorNote: 使用場景：形容那種「故意寫得很欠罵、專門惹人生氣」的內容，目的是騙互動、騙留言。看破就別上當，直接留一句「這 ragebait 太明顯」最帥。
variations: 他每篇貼文都在挑一個群體開戰，根本職業 ragebait。
別被騙去留言，這種標題就是 ragebait。

en.content:
Headline says 'this is the only correct way to cook pasta,'
then snaps the noodles into cold water —
you can smell the ragebait from a mile off.
en.editorNote: 'Ragebait' is content engineered to make you mad on purpose, farming angry comments and shares. Once you spot it, don't take the bait — a cool 'this ragebait is way too obvious' is the cleanest response.
variations: Every post of his picks a fight with some group — dude's a professional ragebait machine.
Don't rage-comment, that headline is pure ragebait.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Threads 台灣爆紅梗：後面有車，源自實況意外，引申為反應過度、自己嚇自己

zh-tw.content:
同事丟一句「等等有空聊一下」，
我腦內已經演完被開除、被檢討、被句點，
結果他只是想問我午餐吃什麼。

後面有車，是我自己嚇自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「後面有車」2026 年初從一段實況意外爆紅，現在泛指「反應過度、誤判情況、自己嚇自己」。朋友在群組裡無故驚慌時丟這句最到位
variations: 吐槽別人版：「冷靜，後面根本沒車，是你眼花。」
自嘲版：「我又後面有車了，白緊張一場。」

en.content:
My boss messaged 'got a sec to talk later?'
and my brain fast-forwarded to fired, written up, done for —
turns out he just wanted lunch recommendations.

Total false alarm. I scared myself.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'there's a car behind you' blew up on Taiwanese Threads in early 2026 as shorthand for panicking over nothing and misreading a situation. Drop it when a friend is spiraling over a non-problem
variations: Reality check: 'Relax, there's no car back there — you're seeing things.'
Self-own: 'Panicked over nothing again. Classic false alarm.'

items.sourceNote: Gen Z slang 2026：lowkenuinely（low-key + genuinely 的合體，用來取代太用力的 literally）

zh-tw.content:
我不是在浮誇，
是那種很小聲、
可是超級認真的——
這部片真的改變我今天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 英文新詞「lowkenuinely」是 low-key（低調）加 genuinely（真心）的合體，用來取代太用力的「literally」。想強調某件事、又不想太浮誇時最好用
variations: 語氣版：「我低調說一句真心話，你今天這樣穿超好看。」
縮短版：「不誇張、真心，我需要放假。」

en.content:
Not gonna hype it up,
but this playlist,
lowkenuinely,
got me through the entire week.
en.editorNote: 'Lowkenuinely' mashes 'low-key' and 'genuinely' into a softer, less-shouty stand-in for 'literally.' Use it when something actually matters but you don't want to sound dramatic about it
variations: 'Lowkenuinely the best nap I've had all year.'
'I lowkenuinely think we should order that again.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 slang revival：wabi sabi / 侘寂，日本美學翻紅為流行語，意為欣賞不完美裡的美

zh-tw.content:
馬克杯缺了個角，
照片有點糊，
計畫全部歪掉——

侘寂啦，
不完美才像真的活過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「侘寂（wabi sabi）」原是日本美學，2025 年在網路翻紅成流行語，意思是「欣賞不完美裡的美」。東西壞了、事情不順時，用這句自我安慰很療癒
variations: 安慰別人版：「裂痕也是它的一部分，很侘寂。」
擺爛正名版：「這不是亂，這叫侘寂風。」

en.content:
Chipped mug,
blurry photo,
plans that fell apart —

it's wabi sabi.
Imperfect is proof it was actually lived in.
en.editorNote: 'Wabi sabi' is a 16th-century Japanese idea — finding beauty in imperfection — that went viral again in 2025. Pull it out when something breaks or goes sideways and you want a gentler take on it
variations: Comforting a friend: 'The crack is part of it now. Very wabi sabi.'
Justifying the mess: 'It's not clutter, it's wabi sabi.'

items.sourceNote: Gen Alpha slang 2026：looksmaxxing（把顏值最大化講得浮誇，實際常是睡好、保養、運動）

zh-tw.content:
別人問我最近在忙什麼，
我說我在 looksmaxxing。

翻譯：早睡、多喝水、
認真擦防曬而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「looksmaxxing」是把「顏值最大化」講得很浮誇的網路用語，其實常常就是好好睡覺、保養、運動。用來自嘲自己在認真變帥變美最剛好
variations: 低成本版：「我的 looksmaxxing 計畫就是十一點前上床。」
反串版：「別人 looksmaxxing，我在 snackmaxxing。」

en.content:
Someone asked what I've been up to.
I said I'm looksmaxxing.

Translation: sleeping early, drinking water,
and actually wearing sunscreen.
en.editorNote: 'Looksmaxxing' is an over-the-top way to say 'maxing out your appearance' — which usually just means sleep, skincare, and the gym. Perfect for poking fun at your own glow-up grind
variations: Budget edition: 'My entire looksmaxxing plan is lights out by eleven.'
Anti-version: 'You're looksmaxxing, I'm snackmaxxing.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 meme phrase：chat, is this real?（源自實況主對聊天室求證，引申為對荒謬事的難以置信）

zh-tw.content:
訊息顯示已讀，
三天了，
頭貼還每天換——

各位觀眾，這是真的嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「各位觀眾，這是真的嗎（chat, is this real?）」源自實況主對聊天室求證，現在拿來對空氣發問，表達不敢置信。遇到荒謬到不行的事最好用
variations: 震驚版：「他居然回我了，各位觀眾，這不是幻覺吧？」
無言版：「帳單這個數字，各位，這是真的嗎。」

en.content:
Read receipt on,
three days of silence,
but the profile pic changes daily —

chat, is this real?
en.editorNote: 'Chat, is this real?' comes from streamers turning to their chat for a reality check. Now it's how you ask the void for confirmation when something's too absurd to believe
variations: Shook version: 'He actually replied. Chat, am I hallucinating?'
Defeated version: 'This total on the bill — chat, is this real?'

items.sourceNote: 「精神離職」2026 職場流行語：人還在工位、心態早就辭職，只剩肉體上班

zh-tw.content:
人還坐在工位上，
靈魂上禮拜就辭職了。

精神離職——
打卡的是我，
上班的是我的空殼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「精神離職」形容人雖然還在辦公室，但心態早就辭職了，只剩肉體來上班。禮拜一發最有共鳴，配一張放空照剛好
variations: 躺平版：「主管交代的我都做，但只做到剛好不會被開除，精神離職進行式。」
問候版：「今天精神離職第 87 天，肉體照常準時出席。」

en.content:
My body's still at the desk,
my soul resigned last Tuesday.

Quiet quitting —
I clock in,
my empty shell works the shift.
en.editorNote: 'Quiet quitting' (精神離職, literally 'spiritual resignation') is when you're physically at work but mentally checked out weeks ago. Peak Monday energy — pair it with a blank-stare selfie
variations: Bare-minimum version: 'I do everything my boss assigns — just enough not to get fired. Quiet quitting, one day at a time.'
Check-in version: 'Day 87 of quiet quitting. Body still clocks in on time.'

items.sourceNote: 「choppelganger」= chopped（醜）+ doppelganger（分身），指某人像名人的崩壞版；2026 Merriam-Webster 收錄

zh-tw.content:
朋友說我長得像那個明星，
我開心了三秒。

他補一句：
是他放假沒睡、剛下飛機那個版本。

所以我是他的 choppelganger。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「choppelganger」是「chopped（醜）＋ doppelganger（分身）」，指某人像某名人「比較崩壞的版本」。損朋友或自嘲都好用，語氣越壞越好笑
variations: 自嘲版：「證件照本人，我是我自己的 choppelganger。」
反擊版：「我像明星？那你是他家門口那隻流浪貓的 choppelganger。」

en.content:
A friend said I look like that celebrity.
I was happy for three seconds.

Then he added:
the jet-lagged, no-sleep, just-off-the-plane version.

So I'm his choppelganger.
en.editorNote: 'Choppelganger' blends 'chopped' (unattractive) and 'doppelganger' — a less impressive lookalike of someone famous. Great for roasting a friend or yourself; the flatter the delivery, the funnier it lands
variations: Self-roast: 'My passport photo is my own choppelganger.'
Clapback: 'I look like a celebrity? You're the choppelganger of the stray cat outside his house.'

items.sourceNote: 「mogging / mog」源自 AMOG，指在外型或氣場上完全壓過別人；2026 社群常用

zh-tw.content:
我精心打扮兩小時，
覺得自己今天無敵。

結果朋朋素顏走進來，
全場目光瞬間轉過去。

他在 mog 我，
而且是零成本那種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「mogging（mog）」指在外型或氣場上完全壓過別人，尤其是「站你旁邊就把你比下去」。拿來稱讚朋友太亮眼、或哀嚎自己被電到最傳神
variations: 哀嚎版：「合照裡他隨便站都在 mog 我，這張我直接不發。」
稱讚版：「你今天這身，把整條街都 mog 了。」

en.content:
I spent two hours getting ready,
felt unstoppable.

Then my buddy walked in bare-faced
and every head in the room turned.

He's mogging me —
and it cost him nothing.
en.editorNote: 'Mogging' (from 'mog') means totally outclassing someone, usually in looks — the one who makes everyone else look average just by standing there. Perfect for hyping a friend or wailing that you got shown up
variations: Wail version: 'He mogs me in every group photo without even trying. Not posting this one.'
Hype version: 'That fit today? You're mogging the whole street.'

items.sourceNote: 「delusionship」= delusion + relationship，指單方面幻想、實際不存在的戀愛關係；2026 Gen Z 感情用語

zh-tw.content:
我們一共講過三句話，
其中兩句是「借過」。

但在我腦裡，
我們已經吵過架、和好、
還一起養了一隻貓。

這叫 delusionship。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「delusionship」是「delusion（妄想）＋ relationship（關係）」，指一段只存在你腦裡的單方面戀愛。用來自嘲對某人想太多剛剛好，通常帶點好笑的絕望
variations: 清醒版：「我跟他的 delusionship 今天正式分手，因為他根本不知道我是誰。」
續命版：「明知是 delusionship，但這集我還想再看一下。」

en.content:
We've exchanged three sentences total,
two of them were 'excuse me.'

But in my head
we've already fought, made up,
and adopted a cat together.

That's a delusionship.
en.editorNote: 'Delusionship' blends 'delusion' and 'relationship' — a one-sided romance that only exists in your imagination. Ideal for self-aware roasting when you've read way too much into someone, usually with a comic edge of despair
variations: Sober version: 'My delusionship officially ended today — because he has no idea who I am.'
Denial version: 'I know it's a delusionship, but I'd like to keep watching this episode a bit longer.'

items.sourceNote: 「純愛戰士應聲倒地」：旁觀者被別人的甜蜜舉動甜到當場倒下的反應語，常刷在放閃留言區

zh-tw.content:
他撐傘全程往她那邊斜，
自己半個肩膀都淋濕了，
還說「我沒事，我火氣大」。

各位純愛戰士，
應聲倒地。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「純愛戰士應聲倒地」是旁觀者被別人的甜蜜舉動甜到「當場倒下」的反應語，通常刷在放閃貼文的留言區。看到有人認真放閃、閃到你，就直接倒地報數
variations: 留言版：「這對互動也太純，純愛戰士當場團滅。」
自嘲版：「單身如我，看完這支影片，第一個應聲倒地。」

en.content:
He tilted the umbrella all the way to her side,
half his own shoulder soaked,
and said 'I'm fine, I run hot anyway.'

Pure-love warriors everywhere,
down we go.
en.editorNote: '純愛戰士應聲倒地' ('the pure-love warriors collapse on cue') is a reaction line for when someone's sweetness floors the bystanders. Drop it under a couple's mushy post — the more genuine the romance, the harder you fall
variations: Comment version: 'This couple is way too pure — pure-love warriors wiped out on the spot.'
Self-roast: 'Single as I am, one video in and I'm the first to hit the floor.'

items.sourceNote: 「點頭如搗蒜」2026 Threads 留言區高頻反應語，表達強烈認同、猛點頭

zh-tw.content:
同事說「老闆不在的下午，
連咖啡都比較好喝」，
全辦公室點頭如搗蒜，

蒜頭都被我們搗成蒜泥了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「點頭如搗蒜」是 Threads 留言區的高頻反應語，形容「太同意了，頭點到停不下來」。看到有人講出你的心聲，直接刷這句最到位
variations: 疊加版：「這句我點頭點到脖子閃到，搗蒜搗到手痠。」
反串版：「你說的每個字我都點頭如搗蒜——除了要我加班那句。」

en.content:
A coworker said, 'The coffee even tastes better
on afternoons when the boss is out,'
and the whole office nodded like bobbleheads —

we basically shook our heads loose.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin '點頭如搗蒜' literally means 'nodding like you're pounding garlic' — agreeing so hard your head won't stop. Drop it in the comments when someone says exactly what you were thinking
variations: Stacked version: 'I nodded so hard at this my neck cramped.'
Sarcastic version: 'I nod like a bobblehead at every word you say — except the one about overtime.'

items.sourceNote: 「意難平」2026 感情語錄頂流詞，指事情結束但情緒沒收尾、心裡卡著一個結

zh-tw.content:
有些人不是忘不了，
是意難平。

忘得掉的，那叫過去；
忘不掉、又和好不了的，
才叫意難平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「意難平」指一段關係或一件事結束了，但情緒沒有收尾，心裡卡著一個解不開的結。用在感情、追劇結局、或某個沒說出口的遺憾都很合
variations: 追劇版：「男二明明更好，編劇卻不給他，我這集意難平。」
釋懷版：「意難平就意難平吧，至少證明我曾經很認真。」

en.content:
Some people you haven't forgotten —
you just can't make peace with them.

What you can forget, that's the past.
What you can't forget and can't fix,
that's the ache that won't settle.
en.editorNote: '意難平' names the feeling when something's over but your heart never got its ending — an unresolved knot you can't untie. Works for a breakup, a show's finale, or a regret you never said out loud
variations: TV version: 'The second lead was clearly better and the writers still didn't give him a shot — this episode left me unsettled.'
Letting-go version: 'Fine, let it ache. At least it proves I once cared this much.'

items.sourceNote: 「chronically online」= 泡在網路太久、思維與現實脫節、講話全是迷因梗；2026 Gen Z 自嘲用語

zh-tw.content:
你問我週末做什麼，
我說「滑手機」；
你問我滑到什麼，
我說「滑手機的人」。

對，我就是那種網路重症患者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「chronically online」指整個人泡在網路裡、講話全是迷因梗、跟現實有點脫節，中文常說「網路重症」。自嘲或吐槽朋友都好用，重點是要講得心甘情願
variations: 吐槽版：「他連現實吵架都想按倒讚，這人 chronically online 到沒救了。」
反省版：「決定戒網一天，結果我用滑手機來查怎麼戒網。」

en.content:
You ask what I did this weekend,
I say 'scrolled.'
You ask what I scrolled,
I say 'people scrolling.'

Yeah — I'm chronically online.
en.editorNote: 'Chronically online' describes someone so soaked in internet culture that their takes have drifted from real life and every sentence is a meme reference. Best used as cheerful self-diagnosis, or to gently roast a friend who's too far gone
variations: Roast version: 'He tried to downvote a real-life argument. Man's chronically online beyond saving.'
Self-aware version: 'Decided to do a digital detox, then used my phone to look up how to do one.'

items.sourceNote: 「let him cook」= 別打斷，讓他好好發揮；對方正在做看似瘋狂但其實有戲的事;2026 常用迷因語

zh-tw.content:
朋友傳來第七段語音，
劇情越講越離譜，
你手指懸在螢幕上想打斷——

先別。let him cook，
讓他煮，這鍋正精彩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「let him cook」字面是「讓他煮」，意思是「別打斷，讓他好好發揮」，通常對方正在做一件看似瘋狂、其實很有戲的事。也能反諷「讓他煮結果什麼都沒端出來」
variations: 力挺版：「他這個計畫聽起來很瘋，但你們讓他煮，成品會嚇到你。」
反諷版：「我們讓他煮了三小時，最後端上來一鍋白開水。」

en.content:
Your friend fires off a seventh voice memo,
the story getting more unhinged by the second,
your thumb hovering over the screen to cut in —

don't. Let him cook.
This dish is just getting good.
en.editorNote: 'Let him cook' means step back and let someone run with it, because whatever looks reckless is actually about to pay off. Flip it for sarcasm too — 'we let him cook and he served nothing'
variations: Backing version: 'His plan sounds insane, but let him cook — the final product will floor you.'
Sarcastic version: 'We let him cook for three hours and he plated a bowl of plain water.'

items.sourceNote: 「Ohio」= 詭異、獵奇、cringe 到不合理；源自把俄亥俄州當成怪事發源地的迷因

zh-tw.content:
這個群組今天發生的事，
完全沒辦法用常理解釋，
只能說一句——

太 Ohio 了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「Ohio」在迷因裡代表「詭異到不合理、很獵奇」的意思，源自把俄亥俄州當成怪事發源地的老哏。形容一件離譜到無法解釋的事，比「傻眼」更有網感
variations: 加強版：「早餐店阿姨找我八十七塊還附一張塔羅牌，這太 Ohio 了。」
自嘲版：「我人生現在的走向只能用一個字形容：Ohio。」

en.content:
What went down in the group chat today
cannot be explained by any known law of physics.

All I can say is —
that was so Ohio.
en.editorNote: 'Ohio' is meme shorthand for something so weird and cursed it defies explanation, born from the running joke that Ohio is where reality glitches out. Reach for it when 'that's messed up' just isn't strong enough
variations: Dialed up: 'The deli lady handed me my change plus a random tarot card. That was so Ohio.'
Self-roast: 'The current trajectory of my life can be summed up in one word: Ohio.'

items.sourceNote: 「standing on business」= 說到做到、認真扛起責任、絕不找藉口；2026 常用宣示語

zh-tw.content:
我說過今年要存到錢，
沒在跟你開玩笑。

不解釋、不找藉口，
這件事我 standing on business。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「standing on business」意思是「說到做到、認真扛起責任」，不是嘴上講講而已。用來宣示決心，或稱讚一個講話算話的人都很到位
variations: 稱讚版：「他答應的事一件都沒少做，這人是真的 standing on business。」
反諷版：「你 standing on business 三天就放棄了喔？」

en.content:
I said I'd save money this year,
and I was not joking.

No excuses, no explaining myself —
I'm standing on business.
en.editorNote: 'Standing on business' means you're dead serious and you follow through — no excuses, no backing down. Use it to declare you actually mean it, or to praise someone who really keeps their word
variations: Praise: 'He did every single thing he promised. Man is genuinely standing on business.'
Sarcastic: 'You were standing on business for a whole three days, huh?'

items.sourceNote: 「mewing」原為練下顎線的舌頭動作，衍生成手指壓嘴唇「我在 mewing 不能講話」的搞笑手勢

zh-tw.content:
老師問誰要第一個上台，
全班瞬間安靜，
每個人手指壓著嘴唇——

不好意思，我正在 mewing，不能說話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「mewing」原本是網路瘋傳「舌頭頂上顎練下顎線」的動作，後來衍生成一個手指壓嘴唇的手勢，意思是「我在 mewing 不能講話」，拿來搞笑逃避回答問題。裝忙、裝沒聽到都好用
variations: 逃避版：「媽問我什麼時候要交男朋友，我默默比出 mewing 手勢。」
吐槽版：「他一被問到要不要請客，立刻開始 mewing。」

en.content:
The teacher asks who wants to present first.
The whole class goes dead silent,
every finger pressed to every pair of lips —

sorry, I'm mewing, can't talk right now.
en.editorNote: 'Mewing' started as the viral tongue-on-the-roof-of-your-mouth jawline trick, then morphed into a finger-to-lips gesture that means 'I'm mewing, I can't answer.' Perfect for comically dodging any question you don't want to touch
variations: Dodge: 'Mom asked when I'm getting a boyfriend. I silently held up the mewing gesture.'
Roast: 'The second someone asked if he'd pay, he started mewing.'

items.sourceNote: 「it's giving ⋯」= 散發出某種氛圍、既視感；後面接具體畫面最好笑；2026 常用形容句型

zh-tw.content:
你今天這身穿搭，
黑外套配墨鏡，
站在便利商店門口——

it's giving 韓劇男主角失戀那集。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「it's giving ⋯⋯」用來形容「散發出某種氛圍或既視感」，後面接一個具體到好笑的畫面最有效。誇人、吐槽、形容店家氣氛都能用
variations: 誇獎版：「這家咖啡廳 it's giving 週末賴床不想上班的浪漫。」
吐槽版：「你這份提案 it's giving 昨天凌晨三點才趕出來。」

en.content:
Your fit today —
black coat, dark shades,
posted up outside the convenience store —

it's giving K-drama lead in the breakup episode.
en.editorNote: 'It's giving ___' describes the vibe or energy something radiates, and it lands hardest when you follow it with a hyper-specific image. Great for compliments, roasts, and pinning down the mood of literally anything
variations: Compliment: 'This cafe? It's giving lazy-Sunday, never-want-to-work-again energy.'
Roast: 'Your pitch is giving thrown-together-at-3am energy.'

items.sourceNote: 「小丑竟是我自己」= 自作多情、白忙一場，最後發現丟臉的是自己；持續熱門的自嘲語

zh-tw.content:
我準備了一整晚的驚喜，
跑到門口才發現
他早就跟別人約好了。

忙了半天，
小丑竟是我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「小丑竟是我自己」用來自嘲「自作多情、白忙一場，最後發現丟臉的是自己」。感情、工作、被已讀都能用，重點是要笑著講才不會真的 emo
variations: 感情版：「傳了三天早安，對方連貼圖都懶得回，小丑竟是我自己。」
工作版：「加班到半夜改的版本，結果客戶要的是最早那版，小丑竟是我自己。」

en.content:
I spent all night planning the surprise,
ran all the way to his door,
only to find out
he already had plans with someone else.

All that effort —
turns out I was the clown the whole time.
en.editorNote: 'I was the clown the whole time' is the go-to line for realizing you were the fool — you overinvested, tried too hard, and ended up looking silly. Works for crushes, work, being left on read; just say it with a laugh so you don't actually spiral
variations: Crush version: 'Sent good-morning texts for three days, didn't even get a sticker back. I was the clown the whole time.'
Work version: 'Stayed up till 2 a.m. on the new draft; client wanted the very first one. I was the clown the whole time.'

items.sourceNote: 「情勒」=「情緒勒索」縮寫；2026 Threads 用來一秒點破用愧疚感逼人就範的話術

zh-tw.content:
「我這麼辛苦還不都是為了你」，
「你再這樣我會很難過」——

這不是關心，
這叫情勒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「情勒」是「情緒勒索」的縮寫，2026 Threads 上用來一秒點破那種「用愧疚感逼你就範」的話術。把界線講清楚，比默默委屈有用
variations: 反擊版：「你的難過我接住，但這件事我還是不會答應，這不是情勒能解決的。」
自省版：「等等，我剛剛那句是不是也有點情勒？收回。」

en.content:
"I work this hard and it's all for you."
"If you keep this up, you'll really hurt me."

That's not love —
that's emotional blackmail.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin 'qing-le' is short for emotional blackmail — 2026 Threads shorthand for calling out guilt-tripping the second it happens. Naming the tactic out loud works better than quietly caving
variations: Clapback: 'I hear that you're upset, and the answer is still no. Guilt-tripping won't change it.'
Self-check: 'Wait, was that a little guilt-trippy of me? Let me take it back.'

items.sourceNote: 「slop」（電子餿水）2026 專指 AI 大量生成、看似有料實則空洞的低質內容

zh-tw.content:
你傳給我的那篇「深度好文」，
六隻手指、三個結論、
每段都在講廢話——

這不是內容，是電子餿水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「slop」（電子餿水）2026 專指 AI 大量生成、看起來有料實則空洞的低質內容。滑到那種一眼假的圖文，一句「這根本 slop」最到位
variations: 職場版：「這份報告整段都是 AI slop，換句話說就是沒有人真的想過。」
自嘲版：「我昨天發的那則也是 slop，抱歉污染大家的動態。」

en.content:
That "must-read deep dive" you sent me —
six fingers in the photo, three conclusions,
every paragraph saying nothing.

That's not content, that's slop.
en.editorNote: 'Slop' is 2026's word for the flood of AI-generated filler that looks substantial but says nothing. When something is obviously machine-churned, 'this is pure slop' nails it
variations: Work version: 'This whole report is AI slop — which is a polite way of saying nobody actually thought about it.'
Self-roast: 'That post I dropped yesterday was slop too. Sorry for polluting the feed.'

items.sourceNote: 「nonchalant」形容毫不費力的冷靜姿態；2026 Gen Z 捧為戀愛與生活的美學

zh-tw.content:
他傳訊息，我隔三小時才回；
心裡明明在尖叫，
表面雲淡風輕——

這就是 nonchalant era。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「nonchalant」形容那種「什麼都無所謂、毫不費力的冷靜」姿態，2026 被 Gen Z 捧成一種戀愛與生活的美學。重點是內心翻江倒海，外表滴水不漏
variations: 戀愛版：「越喜歡越要 nonchalant，已讀不能秒回，這是原則。」
反諷版：「我超 nonchalant 的，只是剛好把他每則限動都看完而已。」

en.content:
He texts, I reply three hours later.
Inside I'm screaming,
outside I'm completely unbothered.

Welcome to my nonchalant era.
en.editorNote: 'Nonchalant' is the effortlessly-unbothered posture Gen Z turned into a whole 2026 aesthetic. The trick is total chaos on the inside, not a single crack on the outside
variations: Dating version: 'The more I like you, the more nonchalant I get. No instant replies — it's a principle.'
Ironic version: 'I'm so nonchalant. I just happen to have watched every single one of his stories.'

items.sourceNote: 「womp womp」= 敷衍的悲傷音效，2026 用來故意潑冷水、假裝可憐反被吐槽

zh-tw.content:
「我今天心情不好。」
「womp womp。」

「你都不安慰我？」
「womp womp womp。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「womp womp」是那種「哦是喔、我不在乎」的敷衍音效，2026 用來故意潑冷水或裝可憐反被吐槽。語氣要欠揍才好笑，只能對很熟的人用
variations: 自嘲版：「加班到十點，薪水沒漲，womp womp，繼續。」
吐槽版：「你遲到還怪路上塞車？womp womp。」

en.content:
"I'm in a bad mood today."
"Womp womp."

"You're not even going to comfort me?"
"Womp womp womp."
en.editorNote: 'Womp womp' is the little sad-trombone noise for 'oh no, anyway' — 2026's favorite way to mock-dismiss someone's complaint. It only lands when it's cheeky, so save it for people who know you're joking
variations: Self-deprecating: 'Worked till 10, no raise, womp womp, moving on.'
Roast: 'You're late and blaming traffic? Womp womp.'

items.sourceNote: 「touch grass」= 去摸摸草，勸泡網太久、為小事鑽牛角尖的人關機接觸現實

zh-tw.content:
你為了一個陌生人的留言
氣到睡不著、
打了六百字回覆——

關掉手機，
去 touch grass 吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「touch grass」字面是「去摸摸草」，2026 用來勸一個泡網太久、為了小事鑽牛角尖的人：關機出門、接觸一下現實世界。損中帶關心
variations: 關心版：「你已經滑三小時了，起來走走，touch grass 一下。」
自嘲版：「我為了遊戲排名破防，好我知道，我這就去 touch grass。」

en.content:
You lost sleep over a stranger's comment
and typed out a six-hundred-word reply —

put the phone down
and go touch grass.
en.editorNote: 'Touch grass' literally means go outside and feel some grass — 2026 shorthand for telling someone who's too online, spiraling over nothing, to log off and rejoin reality. Half insult, half genuine care
variations: Caring version: 'You've been scrolling for three hours. Get up, walk around, touch some grass.'
Self-aware: 'I lost it over a game ranking. Yeah, I know, I'm going to go touch grass.'

items.sourceNote: 「人機」= 說話像 NPC、沒有情緒起伏的人，2026 常用來吐槽罐頭式回覆

zh-tw.content:
我：「今天超崩潰。」
他：「辛苦了。」

我：「我被主管唸了一小時。」
他：「辛苦了。」

我：「我要移民火星。」
他：「辛苦了。」

你是不是人機？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「人機」形容一個人講話像遊戲裡的 NPC，回什麼都同一句、沒有情緒起伏。用來吐槽罐頭式回覆或敷衍已讀最傳神，對方要夠熟才不會真的翻臉
variations: 自嘲版：「開會開到第四小時，我已經進入人機模式，只會點頭。」
曖昧版：「他回訊息永遠三個字，我懷疑我在跟人機談戀愛。」

en.content:
Me: "Today was a disaster."
Him: "That sucks."

Me: "My boss lectured me for an hour."
Him: "That sucks."

Me: "I'm moving to Mars."
Him: "That sucks."

Are you an NPC?
en.editorNote: Calling someone an NPC means they reply like a game character on a loop — same canned line, zero emotion. Perfect for roasting copy-paste texters, but only with people who know you're kidding
variations: Self-deprecating: 'Hour four of this meeting and I've gone full NPC — I just nod now.'
Dating version: 'He answers every text in three words. I think I'm dating an NPC.'

items.sourceNote: 「嘴替」= 嘴巴的替身，替大家把不好說出口的話講出來

zh-tw.content:
會議上大家都在點頭，
只有他舉手說：
「這個時程根本做不完。」

全場沒人接話，
但每個人心裡
都在幫他鼓掌。

他是我們的嘴替。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「嘴替」是「嘴巴的替身」，指那個替大家講出真心話的人。留言區看到有人一針見血，丟這句最省字，也是一種公開的支持
variations: 留言版：「這則留言完全是我的嘴替，我一個字都不用打了。」
追星版：「主持人問了全網想問的那題，堪稱粉絲嘴替。」

en.content:
Everyone in the meeting was nodding.
One guy raised his hand:
"This timeline is impossible."

Nobody backed him up out loud,
but everyone was
clapping on the inside.

He said what we were all thinking.
en.editorNote: The Chinese term literally means 'mouth stand-in' — the person who voices what everyone else is too polite to say. Drop it in a comment section when someone nails your exact thought so you don't have to type it
variations: Comment version: 'This comment said everything I wanted to say. I can log off now.'
Fandom version: 'The host asked the one question the whole internet wanted answered. Absolute legend.'

items.sourceNote: 「出片」= 隨便拍都好看、很容易拍出成品照的場景

zh-tw.content:
這間咖啡廳
窗邊光線斜斜灑進來，
手隨便一舉、快門隨便一按——

有夠出片。

拍了三十七張，
三十七張都能發。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「出片」形容一個地方或穿搭「隨便拍都好看、很容易拍出成品」。約朋友出門、推薦景點時最好用，比說「很美」更精準也更有畫面
variations: 推薦版：「這條巷子超出片，帶相機去絕對不虧。」
反差版：「風景很出片，可是我很不出片，全部都是我閉眼。」

en.content:
This café,
late light slanting in through the window,
lift your phone, tap once —

every shot is a keeper.

Took thirty-seven photos.
All thirty-seven are postable.
en.editorNote: A place that's 'photogenic' in this sense isn't just pretty — it makes your photos come out good with zero effort. Use it when recommending a spot to friends; it's more useful than just saying 'it's nice'
variations: Recommendation: 'That alley shoots beautifully. Bring an actual camera, you won't regret it.'
Contrast version: 'The view was gorgeous. I was not. Blinked in every single one.'

items.sourceNote: 「六邊形戰士」= 各項能力都滿格的全能型人物，源自能力雷達圖

zh-tw.content:
會寫程式、會做簡報、
會殺價、會修水管、
聚餐還負責訂位跟收錢——

這種人我們稱之為
六邊形戰士。

雷達圖畫出來
是一個完美的圓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「六邊形戰士」來自能力雷達圖，形容一個人每項能力都接近滿格、沒有短板。誇同事、誇朋友都好用，帶點半開玩笑的敬佩感
variations: 反向版：「我也是六邊形戰士，只是六個角都往內縮。」
職場版：「他一個人扛設計、企劃、客服，公司請一個等於請三個。」

en.content:
Codes, builds decks,
haggles, fixes plumbing,
and books the group dinner and collects everyone's cash —

we call that person
a hexagon warrior.

Draw their stat radar
and it comes out a perfect circle.
en.editorNote: 'Hexagon warrior' comes from video-game stat charts — someone maxed out on every axis with no weak spot. Great for hyping up a friend or coworker with a bit of joking awe
variations: Reverse version: 'I'm a hexagon warrior too. All six points just face inward.'
Work version: 'He handles design, planning, and support solo. One hire, three salaries' worth.'

items.sourceNote: 「抽象」= 荒謬到無法解釋、不照常理出牌的人事物，2026 常見形容詞

zh-tw.content:
他把生日蛋糕放進微波爐，
說要吃「熱的」。

蠟燭融進奶油裡，
整個廚房都是塑膠味。

我沒有生氣，
我只是覺得
這個人有夠抽象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「抽象」現在不是講藝術，是形容一個人或一件事荒謬到無法用常理解釋。看到離譜但無害的行為，用這兩個字比長篇吐槽更有力
variations: 自嘲版：「我把冰箱門開著出門三天，我自己都覺得我很抽象。」
群組版：「這個群組的聊天紀錄拿去給外人看，會被當成行為藝術。」

en.content:
He microwaved the birthday cake
because he wanted it "warm."

The candles melted into the frosting.
The whole kitchen smelled like plastic.

I'm not mad.
I just think
this man is unhinged.
en.editorNote: In 2026 Chinese slang, 'abstract' stopped meaning art and started meaning behavior too absurd to explain rationally. Two syllables that do more work than a whole paragraph of complaining
variations: Self-deprecating: 'Left the fridge door open and went away for three days. Even I think I'm unhinged.'
Group chat: 'Show an outsider this chat log and they'd file it under performance art.'
